recalls.Description: Petzl America has announced a recall of its Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to a potential fall hazard. The affected Nomic axes are identified by model number U021AA00 and serial numbers ranging from 17L 0000000 000 to 20F 9999999 999. The Ergonomic axes, model U022AA00, have serial numbers from 18F 0000000 000 to 21L 9999999 999. Both models feature an aluminum shaft, steel pick, and reinforced plastic grip, with the Nomic axes in black and the Ergonomic axes in orange. The serial number can be found on a label located on the upper part of the shaft.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Issues Recall for Nomic and Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Petzl America at 877-807-3805 during business hours (8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday), email Returns@petzl.com, or visit https://qr.petzl.com/a/recall and select 'Safety Recall' for further details.

Products.Name: Petzl Nomic Ice Climbing Axe
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,200 (In addition, about 1,160 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Nomic Ice Climbing Axe
Recalled Ergonomic Ice Climbing Axe
Location of serial number
Recalled Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 26 reports of the ice axe shafts breaking at the handle. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, Moosejaw Mountaineering, and various specialty stores across the country, as well as online at petzl.com, REI.com, and Backcountry.com from December 2017 to March 2022, priced between $380 and $440.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of ice axe shaft breaking at the handle during use, leading to potential falls and serious injuries or fatalities.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled axes and contact Petzl America for a complimentary repair kit. To obtain a pre-paid shipping label for returning the shaft, register at https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Sport/Contact?request=product-recall. Petzl America will provide a repair kit that includes a new shaft, grip rest hardware, and assembly instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl recalls Nomic and Ergonomic ice climbing axes due to a fall hazard. Stop using them and contact Petzl for a repair kit.

recalls.Description: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets have been recalled due to serious safety concerns that could lead to head injuries or fatalities. These helmets, designed for large sizes fitting head circumferences of approximately 22.8 to 24.4 inches, are pink with black padding, straps, a buckle, and an adjustable knob. The model number 'GH018L' is found on an internal label, while the brand name and size are displayed on the packaging label.
recalls.Title: Aisstxoer Adult Bicycle Helmets Recalled for Safety Violations and Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers should reach out to YXTDZ Store via email at yxtdzamz@126.com and visit the 'Recalls and Product Safety Alerts' section on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Aisstxoer Adult Bike Helmet (pink)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from October 2025 through November 2025 for approximately $25.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhenshiyongxintaidianziyouxiangongsi (Shenzhen Yongxintai Electronics Co., Ltd.), dba, Yxtdz Store, of China

Hazards.Name: The helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability, increasing the risk of head injury or death in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the helmets immediately and contact YXTDZ Store for a full refund. They will need to destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to yxtdzamz@126.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Aisstxoer adult bicycle helmets recalled due to safety violations posing serious injury risks. Contact YXTDZ Store for refunds.

recalls.Description: Specialized is recalling all Turbo Como SL electric bicycles due to a potential fall hazard linked to the bicycle forks. The affected models, identifiable by 'Como SL' printed on the seat tube or top tube, include various colors such as Cast Umber, Dove Grey, Raspberry (Como SL 4.0), and Smoke, Brassy Yellow, Brushed Silver (Como SL 5.0). To confirm your model, connect your bike to the Specialized app. Only the SL versions of the Como series are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of Specialized Turbo Como SL Electric Bicycles Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Specialized Rider Care at 877-808-8154 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or reach out via email at ridercare@specialized.com. Additional information can be found through Live Chat on www.specialized.com or by visiting http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices.

recalls.Description:
ProRider has issued a recall for several models of bicycle helmets, including the Economy Bicycle Helmet, Bike Helmet with Turn Ring, Black Foam Bike Helmet, BMX Helmet, and Toddler Bike Helmet. These helmets were available in various colors such as blue, green, red, black, and purple. Each helmet features a label inside that displays the model number, manufacturing date, and serial number for identification purposes.

The recall affects helmets manufactured in December 2022 and September 2022, which do not meet the required safety standards for impact protection and labeling. As a result, these helmets may not adequately protect users in the event of an accident, leading to potential serious injuries or fatalities.
recalls.Title: ProRider Recalls Multiple Bicycle Helmet Models Due to Serious Head Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled helmets immediately and reach out to ProRider at 800-642-3123 from 8 a.m. to 1 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. They can also email org@prorider.com or visit www.prorider.com and click on the 'Recall' link for further details.

Recalled BMX Helmet
Recalled Economy Bicycle Helmet
Recalled Bike Helmet with Turn Ring

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Distributed by Wisconsin Bike Federation, Kiwanis Club, and Foster Love across the nation, and available for purchase online at ProRider.com from June 2022 through May 2023, priced between $5 and $18.

Importers.Name: ProRider, Inc., of Kent, Washington

Hazards.Name: The helmets fail to meet mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, posing a significant risk of head injury or death in case of an accident.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the helmets and contact ProRider for a full refund. To complete the refund process, consumers must destroy the helmet by cutting the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to org@prorider.com.

recalls.metaDescription: ProRider recalls several bicycle helmets due to serious safety risks. Stop using them immediately and contact ProRider for a refund.

recalls.Description: Evajoy is recalling all above-ground pools measuring 48 inches or taller, including models EJ-HF032, EJ-HF044, and EJ-HF045. Model EJ-HF032 is 16 feet in diameter and 48 inches high, EJ-HF044 measures 18 feet in diameter and 52 inches high, and EJ-HF045 is 24 feet in diameter and 52 inches high. The pool's model details and Evajoy logo can be found printed on the outer liner. The design includes a compression strap that could allow children to access the pool, potentially leading to drowning incidents, even if the ladder is removed. For pool safety resources, visit CPSC's website at www.PoolSafely.gov for educational materials on safety measures such as barriers and alarms.
recalls.Title: Evajoy Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Recalled Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Evajoy at 888-846-2988 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email support@evajoy.co. More information can be found online at https://evajoy.co/pages/poolrecall or by visiting www.evajoy.co and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Recalled Evajoy Above-Ground Pool Model EJ-HF045

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2023 to June 2024, priced between $550 and $1,050 based on size and model.

Hazards.Name: Drowning risk due to foothold created by compression strap

Remedies.Name: Request a free repair kit to remove the compression strap while maintaining pool integrity. Ensure children cannot access the pool unattended or drain the pool until repairs are made.

recalls.metaDescription: Evajoy recalls above-ground pools 48 inches and taller due to drowning hazards. Learn how to get a free repair kit and ensure safety.

recalls.Description: Todson is recalling its Concord-branded 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up bike helmets due to safety concerns. These helmets, designed for individuals with a head circumference of approximately 22.8 to 24 inches, feature a black exterior and an integrated LED lighting system. The brand name 'Concord' is displayed on the back, and the helmets include adjustable straps and a fitting knob at the rear.
recalls.Title: Todson Issues Recall for Concord 360 Degree Rechargeable Light-Up Bike Helmets Due to Serious Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Todson at 800-278-2565 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email 360concordhelmet@todson.com, or visit todson.com/360-concord-recall for further details.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and on Walmart.com from January 2025 to September 2025 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Todson, Inc., of Foxboro, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: The helmets do not meet mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, compromising the retention system and positional stability. This failure increases the risk of serious injury or death from head impacts during a crash.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled helmets immediately and contact Todson for a full refund. Helmets should be destroyed by cutting off the straps, and photos of the modified helmets can be sent to 360concordhelmet@todson.com to process the refund. Proper disposal of the lithium-ion battery is crucial; do not discard in regular trash or recycling. Check with local hazardous waste centers for safe disposal options.

recalls.metaDescription: Todson recalls Concord bike helmets due to serious injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds. Learn more here.

The affected models include Grandee, Envoy, Vanguard, Aria, Prodigy, Sovereign, Jetsetter, and Jetsetter LX from the model years 2023, 2024, and 2025. recalls.Description: DriBiz is recalling its Ammo Tabs, which are rectangular, off-white fiber desiccant tiles measuring approximately 2 inches by 4 inches and 2.5mm thick. These tiles are designed to be placed in ammunition cans and pistol cases to absorb moisture. The model number AMT-010 can be found on the packaging. Consumers should be aware that these tiles may not effectively protect firearms and ammunition from humidity, leading to potential corrosion and rust, which could increase the risk of malfunction and injury.
recalls.Title: DriBiz Recalls Ammo Tabs Desiccant Tiles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact DriBiz at 877-896-7238 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email info@dribiz.com, or visit https://www.norustzone.com. More information can also be found at https://www.norustzone.com/recall by clicking on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description:
Semfri is recalling its children's multi-purpose helmets due to safety concerns. These helmets, designed for head circumferences of 20.5 to 22.8 inches, were available in various colors including orange, black, blue, navy blue, pink, red, and white. Each helmet features black padding, straps, and a buckle, with an adjustable fitting knob located at the back. The size can be found on a black circular label inside the helmet.

The recall is due to these helmets not meeting mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, specifically regarding impact attenuation and positional stability. This non-compliance poses a significant risk of head injuries in the event of a crash.
recalls.Title: Semfri Children's Helmets Recalled Due to Serious Head Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Semfri via email at Semfri_recall@163.com.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the helmets and contact Semfri for a full refund. To properly dispose of the helmet, cut the straps and send a photo of the destroyed helmet to Semfri_recall@163.com.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for the rear wheel bolts on 2026 model year Trek FX+ 1 and Electra Townie Go! electric bicycles. recalls.Description:
Johnson Health Tech has expanded its recall to include several Matrix Retail and Vision treadmill models due to a potential fire hazard. The affected models include Matrix T30, TF30, T50, TF50, T75, and Vision T9800, TF20, T40, and T80, all manufactured between June 2007 and January 2024. These treadmills feature a running platform measuring 60 inches by 20 inches and a 33-inch-wide console. They were sold without a power cord bracket, which can lead to the power cord loosening from the power socket.

This recall follows an earlier one involving Matrix T1 and T3 Series Commercial Treadmills for similar issues. The remedy for this expanded recall includes providing consumers with a replacement power cord that has an automatic locking-style plug, eliminating the need for tools during installation. Consumers are advised to check their treadmills for a power cord bracket and ensure the cord is securely inserted before use until repairs are made.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix and Vision Treadmills Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Johnson Health Tech North America at 800-211-4590 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email retailpowercord@johnsonfit.com. Visit https://matrixhomefitness.com/recalls or https://www.visionfitness.com/eng/recalls and click on 'Recalls' for further details.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for impact protection and stability, increasing the risk of serious head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and reach out to R.X.Y for a full refund. Helmets should be destroyed by cutting the straps, and consumers are required to send photos of the destroyed helmet to RXY-recall@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: R.X.Y recalls adult bicycle helmets due to safety violations, posing a risk of serious head injuries. Contact for refunds and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: PNW Components is recalling the Loam Pedal Gen 2, an aluminum alloy mountain bike pedal. These platform-style pedals, identifiable by a laser-etched 'Tectonic' logo on the left corner, were sold through various online retailers and bicycle shops. Available colors include black, silver, green, orange, and purple.
recalls.Title: PNW Components Recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 Bicycle Pedals Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact PNW Components, Inc. at 833-902-3381 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, via email at info@pnwcomponents.com, or visit https://www.pnwcomponents.com/pages/voluntary-recall-loam-pedal-gen-2 or www.pnwcomponents.com and click the Recall link in the footer.

Images.Caption: Recalled Loam Pedal Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedal
Recalled Loam Pedals Gen 2 aluminum alloy bicycle pedals

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of the pedal axle cracking and detaching from the crank. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle dealers and online at PNWComponents.com, REI.com, Backcountry.com, and JensonUSA.com from June 2025 through November 2025, priced around $160 per pair.

Importers.Name: PNW Components, Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential for pedal axle cracking, leading to detachment and fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pedals immediately and contact PNW Components for free replacement axles when available. In the meantime, consumers will receive the Range Pedal at no cost. Replacement axle shipments are expected to start on January 5, 2026.

recalls.metaDescription: PNW Components recalls Loam Pedal Gen 2 bicycle pedals due to a fall hazard. Contact for replacements and interim solutions.

recalls.Description: Blue Wave is recalling all above-ground pools that are 48 inches or taller due to a potential drowning hazard. The affected models feature a compression strap that may allow children to climb into the pool, even if the ladder is removed. The model details can be found on the original packaging and instruction manual, along with the Blue Wave logo on the pool liner. Safety is a priority, and consumers are urged to take immediate action to prevent access to these pools by children.
recalls.Title: Blue Wave Recalls Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Blue Wave at (800) 603-0475 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email recall@bluewaveproducts.com. More information is available online at www.bluewaveproducts.com/pages/recall or by visiting www.bluewaveproducts.com and selecting 'Product Recalls' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Blue Wave 15-Ft Round 48-In Active Frame Pool

Recalled Blue Wave Above-Ground Pool Models NB19909 and NB19910

Importers.Name: Blue Wave Products Inc., of Batavia, Illinois

Hazards.Name: Compression strap around pool legs may allow children to access the pool, leading to drowning risk.

Remedies.Name: Contact Blue Wave for a free repair kit. In the meantime, ensure children cannot access the pool, or drain the pool until repairs are made.

recalls.metaDescription: Blue Wave recalls above-ground pools 48 inches and taller due to drowning hazards. Immediate action is required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: SereneLife is recalling all above-ground pools that are 48 inches or taller due to a serious drowning hazard. This includes models SLPORND15 and SLPORND18, identifiable by the SereneLife logo printed on the pool liner. The design features a compression strap around the vertical support poles, which can inadvertently create a foothold for children, allowing them to access the pool even if the ladder is removed. This poses a significant risk of drowning.
recalls.Title: SereneLife Recalls Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact SereneLife Home at 888-619-6770 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, email recall@serenelifehome.com, or visit https://serenelifehome.com/pages/recalls. You can also find more information by clicking on 'Product Recalls' on the SereneLife homepage.

Products.Name: SereneLife Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller

Images.Caption: Recalled SereneLife 48” Above-Ground Pool Models SLPORND15 and SLPORND1

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Wayfair.com, Target.com, and the SereneLife website from December 2021 through May 2025, priced between $300 and $500 based on size and model.

Importers.Name: Sound Around Inc. dba SereneLife Home

Hazards.Name: Foothold created by compression strap may allow child access, posing drowning risk.

Remedies.Name: Immediately stop using the recalled pools and contact SereneLife for a full refund. Photographic proof of destruction or disposal is required for the refund.

recalls.metaDescription: SereneLife recalls above-ground pools 48 inches and taller due to a drowning hazard. Find out how to get a refund and stay safe.

recalls.Description: TopHomer has issued a recall for its 8-inch pool drain covers, which pose significant risks of entrapment and drowning, violating safety standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. The drain covers are white, made of ABS plastic, and feature a top grate with a 7.2-inch diameter and a bottom mounting plate measuring 8 inches. Important safety markings on the grate include 'Listed suction fitting,' 'SP-1053-B,' and '88D9 148 GPM.'
recalls.Title: TopHomer Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Serious Entrapment and Drowning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact TopHomerUS via email at Tophomer.recall@outlook.com or visit the TopHomerUS Amazon Seller Profile page for further assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover
Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover and Base

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from August 2024 to October 2025, priced between $15 and $26.

Distributors.Name: Guangzhoushuoshenghaoyikejiyouxiangongsi, dba TopHomerUS, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and drowning hazards due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, remove the cover from the pool, photograph it, and email the image to Tophomer.recall@outlook.com. Ensure all pools have VGBA-compliant drain covers and educate children about drain safety.

recalls.metaDescription: TopHomer recalls pool drain covers due to serious entrapment and drowning risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: Pedego has issued a recall for its Fat Tire Trike, a three-wheeled electric tricycle made of aluminum, which features a rectangular label on the downtube displaying the brand name 'Pedego.' These trikes were available in a matte black finish. The recall is due to potential safety hazards that could lead to falls or lacerations.
recalls.Title: Recall of Pedego Fat Tire Trikes Due to Fall and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Pedego at 800-646-8604 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@pedego.com, or visit https://pedegoelectricbikes.com/pages/product-recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Pedego Fat Tire Trike

Injuries.Name: Pedego has received 16 reports of the trike frame breaking, including one report of a laceration to the leg.

Retailers.Name: Available at Pedego stores across the country and online at PedegoElectricBikes.com from March 2024 to March 2025, priced between $3,500 and $4,000.

Importers.Name: Pedego, LLC, of Fountain View, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and laceration due to potential hairline fractures in the trike frame.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled trikes and reach out to Pedego to arrange for a free repair that includes replacing a section of the frame, performed by a Pedego dealer.

recalls.metaDescription: Pedego recalls Fat Tire Trikes due to fall and laceration hazards. Contact Pedego for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Pacific Cycle has announced a recall of the Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, identified by Model SW80356. This carrier features a black design with a gray seat cushion, black harness straps, and red buckles. It is designed to attach to a rear bike rack that was included with the carrier. The affected date codes are 712A0329BE and 712A0421BE, which can be found on the underside of the product.
recalls.Title: Pacific Cycle Issues Recall for Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261, available from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email customerservice@pacific-cycle.com or visit www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls/ for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356
Recalled Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carrier, Model SW80356 attached to rear bike rack (sold with carrier)

Injuries.Name: Pacific Cycle is aware of one incident where the buckles released. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com and schwinnbikes.com from May 2025 to September 2025, priced between $190 and $230

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to buckle failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the carriers and contact Pacific Cycle for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Pacific Cycle recalls Schwinn Ovation Bicycle Child Carriers due to a fall hazard. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Amp Fit has issued a recall for the MP2 model of its AMP Smart Fitness Machine, a wall-mounted device designed for full-body workouts. This fitness machine features resistance training with an adjustable arm and connects to the Amp Fit app for personalized fitness experiences. However, a safety issue has been identified where the arm does not lock correctly, leading to potential laceration or serious injury risks.
recalls.Title: Amp Fit Recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machines Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Amp Fit at 888-708-9560, available Monday through Sunday from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT. You can also reach them via email at support@ampfit.com or visit http://joinamp.com/blog/product-safety-recall. For more details, go to https://joinamp.com/go and navigate to the "Recalls" section at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine
Recalled MP2 Model of Amp Smart Fitness Machine

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the arm on the fitness machine not locking properly, resulting in lacerations to the head and bruising to the shoulder and other body.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Ampfit.com from January 2025 to August 2025 for approximately $1,800.

Importers.Name: Amp Fit, Inc., of Middletown, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration or serious injury due to the arm not locking properly

Remedies.Name: Stop using the machine immediately and contact Amp Fit for a free inspection and repair by an authorized technician.

recalls.metaDescription: Amp Fit recalls AMP MP2 Smart Fitness Machines over serious injury risks due to a locking mechanism failure. Immediate action advised.

recalls.Description: Luyuan is recalling the X-Pro Eagle 40cc Youth ATV and the FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATV due to serious safety concerns. These ATVs, identifiable by "X PRO" or "FRP" labels, may not meet mandatory safety standards, posing risks of severe injury or death. The mechanical suspension and handlebars can lead to dangerous crash situations and laceration hazards for young riders.
recalls.Title: Luyuan Recalls Youth ATVs Due to Crash Hazard and Safety Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Luyuan at 855-663-2121 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email luyuanusa@gmail.com, or visit www.luyuanusa.com and select the "Recall" option for more details.

Recalled X-PRO ATV Eagle 40cc (side view)

FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs could be found on Amazon.com, Temu.com, and Frpmoto.com from April 2025 to September 2025, costing between $460 and $480.

Importers.Name: Luyuan Inc., of City of Industry, California

Hazards.Name: Violation of mandatory ATV safety standards leading to crash hazards and potential laceration risks.

recalls.metaDescription: Luyuan recalls X-Pro and FRP Sahara 40cc Youth ATVs due to serious crash hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: TREK is recalling several electric bicycle models from the 2026 lineup, including the Domane+ ALR 5, Domane+ ALR 6 AXS, Checkpoint+ SL 6, and Checkpoint+ SL 7. These bicycles are available in various colors and sizes, with the brand name "TREK" displayed on the downtube and the model name on the top tube. The recall is due to a safety issue that could lead to falls or crashes.
recalls.Title: TREK Recalls Electric Bicycles for Fall and Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Trek at 800-373-4594, available from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/recalls or by visiting https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/ and selecting "Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 5 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Domane+ ALR 6 AXS Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL 6 Electric Bicycle
Recalled MY26 Checkpoint+ SL7 Electric Bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the bicycle chainring coming loose on the recalled bikes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers and authorized Trek retailers across the country, as well as online, from July 2025 to September 2025, priced between $5,000 and $8,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Loose chainring bolts may cause separation, leading to potential falls or crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the affected bicycles and reach out to an authorized Trek dealer for a complimentary repair. The dealer will correctly tighten the chainring bolts. Participants in the recall will also receive a $20 in-store credit for Trek, Electra, or Bontrager products, valid until December 31, 2026.

recalls.metaDescription: TREK recalls select 2026 electric bicycles due to safety hazards. Immediate action recommended for affected users.

recalls.Description: Outdoor Master has issued a recall for two models of their children's and youth helmets due to safety concerns. The OM-TD BIKE model features a blue dinosaur design and is intended for children with a head circumference of 48 to 52 cm. The OM-KSKB model is deep green and fits youth with a head circumference of 46 to 52 cm. Both helmets do not meet the required safety standards, which could lead to serious head injuries in the event of a fall or crash.
recalls.Title: Recall of Outdoor Master Children's and Youth Helmets Due to Head Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach out to Outdoor Master via email at care@outdoormaster.com or visit their website at https://outdoormaster.com/pages/recall. Click on the 'Recall' link at the top of the page for further details.

Importers.Name: Maysun Products, Inc., dba Outdoor Master, of Chino, California

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, increasing the risk of head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the helmets immediately and contact Outdoor Master for a full refund process.

recalls.metaDescription: Outdoor Master recalls children's and youth helmets due to serious head injury risks. Learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Ozark Trail is recalling its Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves, model number BG2247A1, due to potential fire and explosion hazards. These stoves, featuring a dark green design with an orange 'Ozark Trail' logo, have the model number located on a gray label inside the fuel compartment. Consumers are advised to stop using these products immediately.
recalls.Title: Recall of Ozark Trail Tabletop 1-Burner Butane Camping Stoves Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT, or visit www.walmart.com/help or https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

recalls.Description: Trek is recalling specific bicycle models from Trek and Electra that feature coaster brakes, along with replacement rear wheels. These bicycles come in various colors, including red, flamingo pink, dark aquatic, Mulsanne blue, volt yellow, pink frosting, Miami green, lithium gray, viper red, purple abyss, buttercup, pixie blue, and blue. To check if your bicycle or replacement wheel is affected, use the serial number lookup tool available at https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/coasterbrake-recall-inquiry/. The serial number can be found under the bottom bracket between the pedals and begins with "WTU." The affected models include the Electra Townie Rental 1 Step Thru (2026), Trek Precaliber 12 (2026), Trek Precaliber 16 (2024 and 2026), Trek Precaliber 20 (2024 and 2026), Electra Sprocket 1 16 (2026), and their respective replacement rear wheels.
recalls.Title: Trek Recalls Coaster Brake Bicycles and Replacement Rear Wheels Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or www.trekbikes.com and click on "Recalls" for further details.

Products.Name: Trek Coaster Brake Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 68,000 (In addition, about 6,820 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Precaliber 20 Bicycle in Miami Green (MY 2024)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Authorized Trek and Electra retailers across the country, both in-store and online, including Trek.com, from August 2023 to September 2025. Prices ranged from $300 to $660 for bicycles and $66 to $77 for replacement wheels.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Coaster brake failure leading to loss of control and potential crash

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bicycles and replacement wheels immediately. Contact an authorized Trek or Electra dealer for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Trek recalls specific coaster brake bicycles and replacement wheels due to safety hazards. Check if yours is affected and learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Lezyne is recalling two models of its bicycle floor pumps: the Pressure Over Drive with an analog gauge and the Digital Pressure Over Drive featuring a digital gauge. These pumps, which are constructed from steel and aluminum and have a natural wood-colored handle, include a nylon-reinforced braided hose. The recall specifically affects pumps that have a bleed valve located on the front of the base.
recalls.Title: Lezyne Bicycle Floor Pumps Recalled Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Lezyne at 888-998-8881 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, email recall@lezyne.com, or visit ride.lezyne.com/recall. Additional details can be found by clicking on the 'Recalls' section on their website.

Products.Name: Lezyne Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Floor Pump
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 7,500 (In addition, about 680 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Lezyne Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Tire Pump with analog gauge
Recalled Lezyne Digital Pressure Over Drive Bicycle Tire Pump with digital gauge
Only Lezyne bicycle floor pumps with a bleed valve on the front of the base are included in this recall

Injuries.Name: Lezyne has received one report of a canister ejecting from its base, resulting in serious injuries to the fac.

Retailers.Name: Available at various bicycle retailers across the country, including Art's Cyclery, Incycle, Bike Bling, Sports Basement, and Over the Edge Sports, as well as online at ride.lezyne.com, Jensonusa.com, Backcountry.com, and Velotech.com from May 2016 through December 2021, priced between $150 and $180.

Importers.Name: Lezyne USA Inc., of San Luis Obispo, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury from the canister ejecting forcefully from the base when pressurized.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected pumps immediately and contact Lezyne for a complimentary replacement pump, including shipping. Registration at ride.lezyne.com/recall is required to receive disposal instructions for the recalled product. No proof of purchase is necessary.

recalls.metaDescription: Lezyne recalls bicycle floor pumps due to injury risk. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
Konges Sløjd has issued a recall for their three-wheeled children's scooters equipped with a foot brake. These scooters come in beige and feature a T-shaped handle. There are two models affected: KS101544-P00005, which has a cherry print, and KS101544-P00038, which showcases a lemon print. Both models are marked with batch number 062024, found on the information sticker located at the scooter's base.

The recall is prompted by a safety concern where the left front wheel may detach during use, posing a significant fall hazard. Consumers are urged to stop using the scooters immediately and to reach out to Konges Sløjd for a full refund or voucher.
recalls.Title: Konges Sløjd Recalls Three-Wheeled Children's Scooters Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Konges Sløjd via email at webshop@kongessloejd.com for general inquiries or Recall@kongessloejd.com for businesses. More information is available online at https://kongessloejd.com/en-us/pages/product-recall or by visiting https://kongessloejd.com/ and navigating to the 'product recall' section at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Three-Wheeled Scooter with Foot Brake (Cherry Print)

Injuries.Name: Konges Sløjd has received 18 reports of the left front wheel detaching, resulting in three injuries, including one report of a broken arm and a child's lost tooth.

Importers.Name: Konges Sløjd, of Copenhagen, Denmark

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential detachment of the left front wheel

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the scooter and contact Konges Sløjd for a full refund or voucher, either by submitting a photo of the disassembled scooter or returning it to the original place of purchase.

recalls.metaDescription: Konges Sløjd recalls three-wheeled scooters due to a fall hazard. Stop using them and contact for a refund or voucher.

recalls.Description: Peloton is recalling its Original Series Bike+ models, specifically those with model number PL02 and serial numbers starting with the letter 'T'. These bikes measure approximately 4 feet in length and 2 feet in width, featuring an adjustable seat and handlebar. The serial number can be found inside the front fork, behind the front fork, or behind the flywheel. For assistance locating your Bike+ serial number, visit https://www.onepeloton.com/history-summary.
recalls.Title: Peloton Recalls Original Series Bike+ Exercise Bikes Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the affected exercise bikes and contact Peloton for a complimentary repair. Reach Peloton toll-free at 866-679-9129 from 6 a.m. to 12 a.m. ET Monday through Friday, or visit https://support.onepeloton.com/s/article/Peloton-Recall-Information?language=en_US or www.onepeloton.com and navigate to 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page for details on obtaining the free seat post and installation instructions.

Products.Name: Peloton Original Series Bike+ Model PL02

Recalled Peloton Original Series Bike+ Seat Post

Injuries.Name: Peloton has received three reports of the seat post breaking and detaching from the bike during use, including two reports of injuries due to a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available at Peloton and Dick's Sporting Goods stores nationwide, as well as online at onepeloton.com, ebay.com, Amazon.com, and Dickssportinggoods.com from January 2020 through April 2025, priced around $2,495.

Importers.Name: Peloton Interactive Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential breakage of the bike's seat post assembly during use.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled bikes and contact Peloton for a free seat post repair that can be installed by the user.

recalls.metaDescription: Peloton recalls Original Series Bike+ due to fall risks. Stop using and contact Peloton for a free repair.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger Crew XP 1000 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in various colors including black, blue, and red, come in both three and six-seat configurations and feature the 'POLARIS' logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box on the driver's side.

The recall is prompted by a potential fire hazard caused by an improperly routed alternator cable that may contact the negative battery post, leading to an electrical short. This poses a serious risk of injury to users.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger Crew XP 1000 ROVs Due to Fire Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers should contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.polaris.com under 'Product Safety News' or by visiting https://www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls to check if their VIN is affected. Consumers can also reach out to Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar ROV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5,130 (In addition, about 460 ROVs were sold in Canada)

Polaris will provide guidance on safe operation until repairs are made, or consumers can contact an authorized dealer for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls 2025-2026 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar ROVs due to fire hazard. Check if your vehicle is affected and learn how to get it repaired.

recalls.Description: Knog is recalling the Blinder 900 and Blinder 1300 Front Bicycle Lights due to potential fire hazards linked to the lithium-ion battery. The affected models feature batch codes: 0124AA, 0423AA, 0723AA, 0923AA, 1023AA, 1123AA, and 11A23AA, which can be found on the underside of the lights. These lights are characterized by their black exterior and the brand name 'knog' along with 'Blinder' on the handle. They were sold individually and in sets, complete with a mount. It is crucial to handle the disposal of these lithium-ion batteries with care, as they pose a significant fire risk if not disposed of correctly.
recalls.Title: Knog Recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 Bicycle Lights Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to Knog at recall@knog.com or visit http://www.knog.com/recall. Additional information is available at https://us.knog.com/ by clicking on 'Blinder Recall' under the Support section.

Images.Caption: Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 900 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light
Recalled Knog Blinder 1300 Bicycle Light

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the battery catching on fire or exploding. No injuries reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers across the country and online through Amazon from January 2023 to August 2025, priced between $60 and $100.

Importers.Name: Knog Pty Ltd, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burn hazards due to overheating lithium-ion battery.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled lights immediately and register at http://www.knog.com/recall for a free replacement. Dispose of the lithium-ion batteries following local regulations and do not throw them in the trash. Wait for confirmation from Knog before discarding the light.

recalls.metaDescription: Knog recalls Blinder 900 and 1300 bicycle lights due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement and dispose of the batteries safely.

recalls.Description: Werner Paddles has announced a recall of its Stealth and Covert kayak paddles, which are identifiable by the brand name on the decals of both blades and the model name on the right paddle blade. These paddles are constructed from black carbon fiber, making them lightweight yet potentially hazardous if they break during use. This defect poses a serious drowning risk for users.
recalls.Title: Werner Paddles Issues Recall for Stealth and Covert Kayak Paddles Due to Drowning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the affected kayak paddles immediately and reach out to Werner Paddles at 800-275-3311 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, or via email at stealthcovert@wernerpaddles.com. More details can be found online at https://wernerpaddles.com/pages/covert-stealth-recall-consumers or by visiting https://wernerpaddles.com and clicking on 'Stealth/Covert Recall-Consumers' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Stealth Kayak Paddle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 700 (In addition, about 25 were sold in Canada)

Location of Model Name
Recalled Werner Covert Kayak Paddle
Location of Model Name

Manufacturers.Name: Werner Paddles, of Tennessee

Retailers.Name: 4 Corners River Sports

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking during use leading to drowning

Remedies.Name: Stop using the paddles and contact Werner Paddles for a full refund or store credit. Complete the recall form at https://wernerpaddles.com/pages/covert-stealth-recall-consumers. To process the refund or credit, follow the instructions on the website and destroy the paddle by cutting it into four equal pieces, then upload a photo of the destroyed paddle.

recalls.metaDescription: Werner Paddles recalls Stealth and Covert kayak paddles due to a drowning hazard. Immediate action required for refunds.

recalls.Description:
Textron E-Z-GO is recalling specific models of their personal transportation vehicles (PTVs) due to a potential fuel leak that could lead to serious injury or fire hazards. The affected models include the 2020-2025 RXV Freedom and 2023-2025 Valor, with serial numbers from 5538278 to 5920722, as well as the 2020-2022 TXT Freedom and Valor models, with serial numbers from 3455024 to 3582057. Serial numbers can be found near the base of the steering column for RXV models and below the operator seat for TXT models.

These vehicles, often referred to as golf carts, are primarily used for transporting individuals. Owners are urged to take immediate action to ensure their safety and the safety of others by halting use of these vehicles until they are inspected and repaired if necessary.
recalls.Title: Textron E-Z-GO Recalls Personal Transportation Vehicles for Fire and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Textron E-Z-GO at 888-438-3946 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email ezgowarranty@textron.com, or visit https://ezgo.txtsv.com/personal/owners/recall-information or https://ezgo.txtsv.com and select 'Recall Information' under the 'Owners' tab.

Products.Name: E-Z-GO RXV Freedom PTV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 90,800 (In addition, about 7,300 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled E-Z-GO Valor PTV
Recalled E-Z-GO TXT Freedom PTV
Recalled E-Z-GO RXV Freedom PTV

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Textron E-Z-GO LLC, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available through Textron dealerships and distributors nationwide from September 2019 to June 2025, priced between $8,400 and $14,000.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury and fire hazard due to potential fuel leakage

Remedies.Name: Inspect and repair the vehicle for free if fuel leakage is detected

recalls.metaDescription: Textron E-Z-GO recalls specific personal transportation vehicles due to fire and injury risks. Immediate action required for owners.

recalls.Description: iFIT is recalling NordicTrack Rowing Machines with model numbers NTRW19147.0, NTRW19147.1, NTRW19147.2, and NTRW19147.3 due to a potential fire hazard. The model number can be found on a decal located on the base of the machine. These rowing machines are available in black, gray, and white, featuring the "NordicTrack" logo in black on the front legs and "RW900" in white on the back legs.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack Rowing Machines Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 7 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email ntrw19147-recall@ifit.com or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and navigate to the "Voluntary Recalls" section at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: NordicTrack NTRW19147 Rowing Machine
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 44,800 (In addition, about 700 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled NordicTrack RW900 Rowing Machine

Injuries.Name: There have been eight reported incidents including two reports of fire, six reports of smoking or melting, and at least $6,000 in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture Mart, and ABT, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 to April 2022, priced around $1,700.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Overheating screen console may ignite, creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the rowing machines immediately. Contact iFIT to schedule a free home repair with an authorized technician, which includes a replacement screen console.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack rowing machines due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a free repair and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Yamaha is recalling its 2021-2025 Drive 2 Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs), which include both gas and electric models. These golf cars were available in various colors and have specific model numbers and serial number ranges that are part of the recall. The model identification can be found on the sides of the vehicles, while serial numbers are located in different areas depending on the model. If you're having trouble finding your serial number, reach out to Yamaha for help.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2021-2025 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Crash Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yamaha at their toll-free number 866-747-4027, available daily from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET. You can also email them at ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com or visit their website at https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Vehicles (2021-2025 Model Year)

Recalled Yamaha Drive 2 J2D Super Hauler

Retailers.Name: Available at Yamaha dealers across the country from June 2020 to July 2025, priced between $8,300 and $12,900.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Company, of Kennesaw, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to lack of stop lights, leading to crash hazards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected golf cars and contact an authorized Yamaha dealer for a free installation of stoplights. For those unable to visit a dealer, Yamaha will arrange transportation or install the stoplights on-site.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2021-2025 golf car vehicles due to crash hazard. Contact Yamaha for free stoplight installation.

recalls.Description: Yamaha has announced a recall for its 2017-2024 model year DR2A and DR2E Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs) due to a serious crash hazard. The affected vehicles, which come in various colors, have serial numbers starting with J0D, J0E, or J2D. The serial number can be found on the handlebars on the left side of the rear seat area.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2017-2024 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET daily, email ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com, or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and select 'CPSC Recalls' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Remedies.Name: Free installation of new brake shoes and pads; transportation provided if necessary

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2017-2024 golf car vehicles due to braking issues that pose serious crash risks. Immediate action is advised.

recalls.Description: YooxArmor is recalling its multi-purpose kids' helmets due to safety concerns that could lead to severe head injuries or fatalities. These helmets, available in size small (S), feature a pink design adorned with unicorns, rainbows, and stars, along with black padding and straps. A black and red adjustable knob is located at the back, and 'YooxArmor' is printed on the rear of the helmet. The helmets do not meet essential safety standards for bicycle helmets, which raises significant safety issues for users.
recalls.Title: Recall of YooxArmor Kids' Helmets Due to Serious Head Injury Risks; Sold on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, reach out to YooxArmor via email at service@yooxarmor.com or visit their Amazon Seller Profile: YooxArmor.

Images.Caption: Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (front view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (side view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (side view)
Recalled YooxArmor Multi-Purpose Helmet (rear view)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from October 2024 to July 2025 for approximately $30.

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Shi Yuxing Keji Youxiangongsi, dba YooxArmor, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with mandatory safety standards for bicycle helmets, posing a risk of serious head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the helmets immediately and contact YooxArmor for a full refund. To destroy the helmet, cut off the straps and send a photo of the damaged helmet to service@yooxarmor.com to process the refund.

recalls.metaDescription: YooxArmor recalls kids' helmets due to serious injury risks. Contact for refunds and destroy helmets immediately.

recalls.Description: StopBox USA is recalling specific AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro firearm locks that were shipped between June 2 and July 15, 2025. These locks are designed to secure the chamber of an AR-15 rifle, preventing it from firing. Made from anodized aluminum, the locks are available in red and black and feature five buttons for each finger, along with the StopBox 'S' logo and the engraving 'CHAMBER LOCK PRO AR-15' on the back.
recalls.Title: StopBox USA Recalls AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro Due to Safety Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact StopBox USA at 877-919-4637, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can reach out via email at support@stopboxusa.com or visit https://stopboxusa.com/pages/stopbox-ar-15-chamber-lock-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro (Red)
Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro (Red)
Recalled AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro Inserted in a Firearm (Black)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: StopBox USA, of Spokane Valley, Washington

Retailers.Name: Available at StopBox USA stores and online, as well as from various dealers nationwide from June to July 2025, priced around $150.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to a manufacturing defect

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product and secure firearms by other means; contact StopBox USA for a prepaid return label and choose between a full refund or replacement.

recalls.metaDescription: StopBox USA recalls AR-15 Chamber Lock Pro due to safety risks. Contact them for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: DT Swiss is recalling its Carbon Fiber Road Wheels due to a potential crash hazard linked to a structural defect. The affected models include ERC 35mm/45mm, CRC 35mm/45mm, HEC 35mm/45mm, and ARC 50mm/55mm/65mm. These wheels were available as aftermarket products and as original equipment on various premium bicycle brands. The wheels can be identified by the DT Swiss ID code, which is located on a small label on the rim, indicating 'DT Swiss ID' followed by a 7-digit code. Any wheels with a DT Swiss ID code of 2740000 or higher are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: DT Swiss Recalls Carbon Fiber Road Wheels Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DT Swiss at 800-000-1994 from 8:30 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Recall@dtswiss.com. More information is available online at https://www.dtswiss.com/recall or by visiting www.dtswiss.com and clicking on 'Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 6,000 (In addition, about 150 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled DT Swiss Carbon Fiber Road Wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the outermost carbon layer separating around the rim flange. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: DT Swiss, of Grand Junction, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available through independent bicycle dealers nationwide and as original equipment on various bicycle brands from September 2024 to July 2025, priced between $1,000 and $1,880.

Hazards.Name: Potential structural integrity failure leading to crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled wheels and contact DT Swiss for a replacement. Register the wheels at www.dtswiss.com/recall.

recalls.metaDescription: DT Swiss recalls Carbon Fiber Road Wheels due to a crash hazard. Stop using the wheels and contact DT Swiss for a replacement.

recalls.Description: C.A.M.P. is recalling Nimbus Lock Carabiners available in Gun Metal/Green, Gun Metal/Fuchsia, and Gun Metal/Blue. These carabiners were sold individually and as part of various packs, including the Nimbus Lock 3 Pack, Locker Draw KS, Energy CR3 Pack, and Energy Nova Pack. The recall specifically affects carabiners with batch numbers 10 23, 12 23, 02 24, 09 24, 10 24, 12 24, 01 25, 02 25, and 06 25, which can be located on the side of the carabiner's bottom.
recalls.Title: C.A.M.P. Recalls Nimbus Lock Carabiners Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact C.A.M.P. at 877-421-2267 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email warranties@camp-usa.com, or visit www.camp-usa.com/nimbus-lock-safety-recall-information. Additional information can be found by clicking on 'Safety Recalls' at the bottom of the homepage.

Products.Name: Nimbus Lock Carabiners

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to fall from a malfunctioning automatic closing mechanism.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled Nimbus Lock Carabiners and reach out to C.A.M.P. C.A.M.P. will inspect the returned items and send them back if they meet safety standards; otherwise, replacements will be provided at no cost.

recalls.metaDescription: C.A.M.P. recalls Nimbus Lock Carabiners due to fall risk. Stop using immediately and contact for return instructions.

recalls.Description: Leatherman is recalling its Charge Plus and Charge Plus TTi multi-tools due to a potential laceration hazard. The Charge Plus models are available in black and stainless-steel finishes, accompanied by either a black MOLLE or black nylon sheath. The Charge Plus TTi model is offered in stainless steel with a black nylon sheath. Each tool features the 'Leatherman' name etched onto its surface.
recalls.Title: Leatherman Charge Plus Multi-Tools Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Leatherman at 800-847-8665 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, email charge@leatherman.com, or visit www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall for more details.

Recalled Leatherman Charge Plus (black)
DRecalled Leatherman Charge Plus TTi (stainless steel)

Manufacturers.Name: Leatherman Tool Group Inc., of Portland, Oregon

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, hardware, cutlery stores nationwide, and online at Leatherman.com, Amazon.com, and REI.com from July 2024 to March 2025, priced between $150 and $200.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to knife blade not fully retracting

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled multi-tools immediately and check www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall to see if your tool is affected. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the tools for free repairs.

recalls.metaDescription: Leatherman recalls Charge Plus multi-tools due to laceration risks. Check if your tool is affected and learn how to get it repaired.

recalls.Description: Apollo is recalling specific serial numbers of its City 2024 model electric scooters due to a potential fall hazard. These dual-motor scooters can reach speeds of up to 32 miles per hour and feature a gray exterior with a headlight and rear brake light. The affected serial numbers range from 4202240300001 to 4202240301236 and 4202240400001 to 4202240400764, found on the bottom of the scooter deck or via the Apollo App.
recalls.Title: Apollo City 2024 Electric Scooters Recalled Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Apollo at 888-501-8862 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email support@apolloscooters.co, or visit https://apolloscooters.co/pages/apollo-city/recall for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Apollo Electric Scooter

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 10 reports of the weld cracking on the stem of the scooter, including four riders who reported coming off the scooter; one reported injury including abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy locations nationwide, as well as online at Apolloscooters.co, Amazon.com, and BestBuy.com from June 2024 to April 2025, priced around $1,800.

Importers.Name: Apollo Imports Inc., of Newark, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Potential for stem breakage due to cracked weld line, leading to fall and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled scooters and contact Apollo for a complimentary replacement stem. To participate in the recall, visit https://apolloscooters.co/pages/apollo-city/recall to complete the online form. Apollo also advises taking scooters to a service repair location for a free stem replacement. Find your nearest service location at https://apolloscooters.co/pages/locations-near-you.

recalls.metaDescription: Apollo recalls City 2024 electric scooters due to stem breakage risks. Stop using and contact Apollo for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: FENGQS is recalling its F7 Pro electric bikes due to potential fire and burn hazards associated with the lithium-ion batteries. These e-bikes, sold in blue, were manufactured on April 21, 2024, and feature the brand name "FENGQS" on the downtube and the model number "F7 Pro" on the chain stay near the rear wheel. The battery's specifications, including a capacity of 21.5Ah and the production date, can be found on a label attached to the front of the battery.
recalls.Title: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bikes Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks; Serious Injury or Death Possible
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact FENGQS at 607-307-2127 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@fengqs.work, or visit www.fengqs.com/pages/recalls. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.fengqs.com and click on "Recalls" at the top of the page.

The battery's capacity (21.5Ah) and the production date (2024/4/21) are printed on a label on the front of the battery

Injuries.Name: FENGQS has received 13 reports of e-bikes igniting, two resulting in fires with property damage totaling $4,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Fengqisi Car Industry Co., Ltd., dba FENGQS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from May 2024 through December 2024, priced between $700 and $1,200.

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and ignition of the lithium-ion battery, leading to fire and burn hazards.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the recalled e-bikes and contact FENGQS for a full refund. Consumers must mark the battery's production date with a permanent marker, submit a photo of this information to recall@fengqs.work, and ensure proper disposal of the battery according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: FENGQS recalls F7 Pro electric bikes due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for consumer safety. Full refund available.

recalls.Description: Transpro is recalling its A3 Hub Motor, A11F Spark, and R1 Commuter electric scooters due to safety concerns related to their lithium-ion batteries. These scooters, available in black and gray, pose a fire hazard as the batteries do not meet UL certification standards. Consumers are advised to handle the disposal of these batteries with care, as they require special treatment to mitigate fire risks. Contact your local household hazardous waste facility to confirm if they accept these batteries before disposal.
recalls.Title: Transpro Recalls Electric Scooters Due to Fire Risks from Unauthorized Lithium-Ion Battery Labels
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Transpro at 888-616-0328 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email sales@transprous.com. More information is available at https://transprous.com/recall-escooters or by visiting https://transprous.com and selecting "RECALL" at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup have issued a recall for above-ground pools measuring 48 inches or taller due to a serious drowning hazard. The affected models feature compression straps on the outside, which can inadvertently create a foothold for children. Consumers should check the pool liner for brand names and model numbers to determine if their pool is included in this recall. The recall spans various models sold between 2002 and 2025 across several brands, including Bestway, Coleman, Intex, and Polygroup.
recalls.Title: Recall of Certain Above-Ground Pools by Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup Due to Drowning Risk; Nine Fatalities Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, Bestway can be reached at 844-593-4003 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday, or online at https://BWrecallsupport.expertinquiry.com or via email at BWrecallsupport5516@sedgwick.com. Intex customers can call 800-549-8829 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or visit www.Intexcorp.com/recall or email recallhelp@intexcorp.com. Polygroup support is available at 888-621-4137 from 5 a.m. to 11 p.m. MT, Monday to Friday, or online at https://polygrouprecall.com or via email at poolrecall@polygroup.com.

Products.Name: Above-Ground Pools 48 Inches and Taller
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 5 million (In addition, approximately 266,000 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Example of child using the compression strap to stand on above-ground pool, illustrating the hazard involved
Examples of recalled above-ground pools with arrows showing the compression strap running externally on the outside and over the vertical support legs
Examples of Strap Configurations for Recalled Above-Ground Pools 48-Inches and Taller
Examples of Strap Configurations Running Behind the Pole for Above-Ground Pools 48-Inches and Taller that are Not Recalled

Injuries.Name: CPSC believes that nine children between the ages of 22 months and 3 years old have drowned, after gaining access to the pools via the footholds. The incidents occurred in California, Texas, Florida, Michigan, Wisconsin and Missouri between 2007 and 2022. CPSC is also aware of three other incidents in 2011 and 2012 where children who gained access to the recalled pools were reported to have previously used the compression strap to gain entry to the pool.

Retailers.Name: Available at major retailers nationwide, including Walmart, Target, Sears, Lowe's, Kmart, Toys 'R' Us, Sam's Club, The Home Depot, Big Lots, Costco, and BJ's, as well as online at Amazon.com, Wayfair.com, and the respective websites of Bestway USA, Intex, Funsicle, and Summer Waves.

Distributors.Name: Bestway (Hong Kong) International Ltd.
Intex Recreation Corp. (Long Beach, California)
Polygroup North America Inc. (El Paso, Texas)

Hazards.Name: Drowning Risk from Compression Strap Foothold

Remedies.Name: Request Repair Kit from Manufacturer

recalls.metaDescription: Bestway, Intex, and Polygroup recall above-ground pools due to drowning hazard. Learn more about the recall and how to get a repair kit.

recalls.Description: VIVI has announced a recall of 36-volt lithium-ion rechargeable batteries that were sold with specific VIVI e-bike models. These batteries can overheat, leading to potential fire and burn hazards. Consumers can identify their e-bike model on their sales order or the bicycle frame, while battery model details are printed directly on the battery. Affected models include various combinations of e-bikes and batteries, which are listed in the official recall notice.
recalls.Title: Recall of VIVI E-Bikes Lithium-ion Batteries Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact VIVI at 800-375-6103 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, or visit https://viviebikes.com/pages/recall or https://viviebikes.com and select "Important Recall Information" for further details.
