Injuries.Name: VIVI is aware of 14 reports of incidents of batteries overheating, including three reports of fires. No injuries reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Guangzhou Plenty Bicycle Co., Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: These e-bikes with recalled batteries were available for purchase online at amazon.com, walmart.com, ebay.com, viviebikes.com, wish.com, sears.com, wayfair.com, and aliexpress.com from December 2020 to November 2023, priced between $365 and $950.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Binxin Household Co., Ltd., d/b/a VIVI E-bikes, of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to fire and burn hazards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled batteries and contact VIVI for a free replacement battery and charger. Dispose of the recalled battery properly at a household hazardous waste collection center.

recalls.metaDescription: VIVI recalls lithium-ion batteries for e-bikes due to fire hazards. Learn how to get a replacement and dispose of the recalled batteries safely.

recalls.Description: SPPTTY is recalling its kids bicycles due to hazardous lead levels that exceed federal safety standards. These bicycles, available in pink with brown accents, come in 14-inch and 18-inch sizes and feature the brand name "SPPTTY" and "New Sport Bike" on the downtube. The presence of lead poses serious health risks, particularly for young children who may ingest it. Additionally, the black paint on the bicycle pumps has been found to contain lead above permissible limits.
recalls.Title: SPPTTY Kids Bicycles Recalled Due to Lead Poisoning Risks; Sold at Walmart
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology via email at lishkjie@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled SPPTTY Kids 14-inch Bicycle in Pink
Recalled SPPTTY Kids 18-inch Bicycle in Pink

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Walmart.com from August 2024 to December 2024 for approximately $85.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology, dba SPPTTY, of China

Hazards.Name: Excessive lead levels in bicycle components posing a poisoning risk to children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles immediately and contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology for a full refund. Consumers must dismantle the bicycles, photograph them, and email the image to lishkjie@163.com. After providing proof of destruction, dispose of the bicycles.

recalls.metaDescription: SPPTTY recalls kids bicycles due to lead poisoning risks. Immediate action required for refunds. Contact Shenzhen Yihuachuang Technology.

recalls.Description: Giant Bicycle has announced a recall for the Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes, available in various colors including metallic red, pearl white, metallic navy blue, metallic green, indigo blue, happy mint, and panther. These bikes come in two frame styles—mid-step and low-step—and are offered in small, medium, and large sizes. Each bike features a unique serial number located on the frame's left rear dropout and a sticker beneath the downtube, with "Momentum" on the downtube and "Vida E+" on the top tube.
recalls.Title: Giant Bicycle Recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-Bikes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Giant Bicycle at 866-458-2555 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email recall@giantbicycle.com. More information can be found at https://www.momentum-biking.com/us/recall-information, https://www.giant-bicycles.com/us/recall-information, https://www.liv-cycling.com/us/recall-info, or by visiting https://www.giant-bicycles.com/us and clicking on "Recalls."

Images.Caption: Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic green)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic red)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (pearl white)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (metallic navy)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (indigo)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (panther)
Recalled Momentum Vida E+ E-Bike (happy mint)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of broken fork steerer tubes. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized Giant Bicycle dealers across the country and online at https://www.momentum.com/us from September 2019 to May 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,200.

Importers.Name: Giant Bicycle Inc., of Newbury Park, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fork steerer tube cracking or breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should check the third character of their bike's serial number. If it is "H", "J", or "K", the fork must be replaced at no cost. If it is "G", a visual inspection by an authorized Giant Bicycle dealer is required to assess the fork.

recalls.metaDescription: Giant Bicycle recalls Momentum Vida E+ E-bikes due to a fall hazard. Check your bike's serial number for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Jeriflyer is recalling its 8-inch pool drain covers due to a serious entrapment hazard that does not meet the safety requirements of the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. These white plastic drain covers, measuring 8.1 inches in diameter, pose a risk to swimmers and bathers. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Jeriflyer 8-Inch Pool Drain Covers Recalled Due to Entrapment Risk Violating Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For inquiries, contact Jeriflyer via email at Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com or visit Amazon.com: Jerliflyer and click on 'Recall' for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2024 to May 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Distributors.Name: Junjuanshop, dba Jeriflyer, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, remove the cover, photograph it, and email the image to Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com. Ensure all pool and spa drain covers comply with VGBA standards and educate children about drain safety. Junjuanshop and Amazon will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Jeriflyer recalls 8-inch pool drain covers due to entrapment risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: 509 is recalling several models of Tactical helmets, including the Tactical 3.0 and Tactical 2.0, due to inadequate protection in the event of a crash, which could lead to head injuries. The recall affects various sizes and colors of helmets manufactured from 2020 to 2023. Helmets with the 509 logo and 'FIVE O NINE' or 'Polaris' branding are included in this recall. Consumers are urged to check their helmets against the recall list to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: 509 Recalls Tactical Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach 509 at 877-743-3509 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email support@ride509.com. More information is available at https://ride509.com/pages/recalls. Polaris Industries can be contacted at 800-765-2747 during the same hours or online at www.polaris.com under 'Product Safety News'. For further inquiries, Polaris' owner connections team can be reached at Owner.Connections@polaris.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 3.0 Trail Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Youth Helmet
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 3.0 Mountain Helmet
Recalled 509 Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 for Polaris Tactical 2.0 Small/Medium Helmets
Recalled 509 and 509 for Polaris Helmets with Fidlock®
Correct Fidlock
Incorrect Fidlock Strap Fastener

Injuries.Name: The firm received one incident where the rider's helmet became detached during a crash, resulting in a concussion.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.ride509.com and www.polaris.com, as well as at 509 and Polaris dealers nationwide from January 2020 to May 2025, priced between $140 and $900.

Importers.Name: Teton Outfitters LLC, dba 509, a Polaris subsidiary, of Rigby, Idaho

Hazards.Name: Insufficient protection in the event of a crash, leading to potential head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled helmets immediately. Contact 509 for a refund, credit, or replacement for Tactical 3.0 or Tactical 2.0 helmets. Polaris offers similar options for their helmets. No proof of purchase is needed. Follow disposal instructions after confirming your helmet is recalled.

recalls.metaDescription: 509 recalls Tactical helmets due to head injury risks. Check if your helmet is affected and learn how to get a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Bell Sports is recalling several models of children's and adult bicycle helmets, including the Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy. These helmets, identified by model numbers B0605Y, B0605C, and B0494Y, were available in various colors such as blue, green, purple, red, and pink, featuring designs like checkers and rainbows. The model name, number, and manufacture date can be located on a sticker inside each helmet. Consumers should check their helmets against this recall list to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Sports at 800-456-2355 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com. More details can be found online at https://www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-helmet-recall-notice-axle-cadence-rev-passage-and-frenzy-models/ or by visiting www.thebellgarage.com and clicking the 'Recall' banner.

Products.Name: Axle Bicycle Helmet
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 31,200 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Sports Bicycle Helmet, Model B0494Y
Recalled Bell Sports Bicycle Helmet Model, B06065C or B0605Y
The model name, model number and manufacture date, in MM/YY or M/YY format, can be found on a sticker inside the helmet

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., of Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart, Target, Academy Sports+ Outdoors, and other retailers across the country, as well as online at Amazon.com, FredMeyer.com, Academy.com, and Target.com from September 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $20.

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with safety standards may lead to inadequate protection during crashes, increasing the risk of head injuries.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the helmets and reach out to Bell Sports for a full refund. Cut the straps off the helmet to destroy it and send a photo to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com to process your refund. Bell Sports will also contact known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Axle, Cadence, Rev, and Frenzy helmets due to head injury risks. Check your helmet now for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products has announced a recall of specific Swivel Port mouthpiece equine bits from the Bob Avila collection. The affected models include the Avila Santa Maria Swivel Port (model AVB-171), featuring a 7 ¼" shank with a decorative silver design; the Avila Byron Swivel Port (model AVB-612), which has an 8" Gunmetal shank; the Avila Phoenix Swivel Port (model AVB-621), showcasing an 8" cheek with a decorative sunflower; and the Avila Sunflower Swivel Port (model AVB-222), a 7" shank with a floral design. These bits pose a risk of breaking during use, which could lead to falls for riders.
recalls.Title: Recall of Professional's Choice Equine Bits Due to Potential Fall Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products Inc. at 800-331-9421 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email info@profchoice.com, or visit https://profchoice.com/recalls or https://profchoice.com and select 'Recalls' from the Customer Care menu.

Products.Name: Avila Santa Maria Swivel Port Bit

Recalled Avila Sunflower Swivel Port equine bit model number AVB-222
Recalled Avila Byron Swivel Port equine bit model number AVB-612
Recalled Avila Phoenix Swivel Port equine bit with model number AVB-621

Injuries.Name: The company has received five reports of bits breaking.No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at horse-riding supply stores nationwide and online at profchoice.com from January 2022 through March 2025, priced between $120 and $160.

Importers.Name: Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products Inc., of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma

Hazards.Name: Risk of bit breaking during use, leading to fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled bits and contact Professional's Choice for a full refund or a replacement. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns.

recalls.metaDescription: Professional's Choice recalls specific equine bits due to fall hazard. Stop using and contact for refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Total Saddle Fit is recalling its Shoulder Relief Cinches, designed to secure saddles on horses. These cinches are available in black and brown, with lengths ranging from 24 to 36 inches. The brand name is found near the buckle, and this recall specifically affects cinches sold before August 2021, identifiable by their round buckles and absence of center elastic.
recalls.Title: Total Saddle Fit Recalls Shoulder Relief Cinches Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more details, contact Total Saddle Fit via email at care@totalsaddlefit.com or visit https://www.totalsaddlefit.com/recall. Alternatively, go to https://www.totalsaddlefit.com and select 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Shoulder Relief Cinch
Recalled Shoulder Relief Cinch in brown and black color

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the cinch coming apart and the saddle falling off, including one report of a rider fracturing their ribs.

Retailers.Name: Available online at totalsaddlefit.com and at retailers such as Hitching Post Tack Shop, Tack & Western Wear, and Saddlery Direct nationwide from August 2015 to August 2021 for approximately $140.

Importers.Name: Total Saddle Fit, LLC, of Jamul, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of cinch detachment leading to saddle falls, endangering the rider.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled cinch immediately and reach out to Total Saddle Fit for a complimentary replacement, along with return instructions for the original cinch (a pre-paid shipping label will be provided). Total Saddle Fit will contact all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Total Saddle Fit recalls Shoulder Relief Cinches due to potential fall hazards. Contact for free replacements and safety instructions.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling all VINs of its 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic and 2025 Pro XD Kinetic recreational off-highway vehicles (ROVs). The Ranger XP Kinetic models are available in camouflage and white, while the Pro XD Kinetic features gray plastic body panels with a black frame. These vehicles are configured for three passengers and have 'POLARIS' marked on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box. Additionally, accessory charger service parts with specific part numbers are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP Kinetic and Pro XD Kinetic Off-Highway Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information is available at www.polaris.com under 'Product Safety News' or at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your VIN is affected. For further inquiries, contact Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or visit their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic ROV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,900 ROVs and 185 accessory charger service parts/kits (In addition, about 330 ROVs were sold in Canada)

Recalled Polaris Model Year 2024 Ranger XP Kinetic
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2025 Pro XD Kinetic
Recalled Polaris RANGER and Pro XD VIN Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 47 reports of filter failures with the recalled ROVs, including six reports of melting/fire and four reports of sparking/smoke. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers nationwide from April 2023 to March 2025, priced between $24,000 and $37,500.

Hazards.Name: Potential fire hazard due to water leakage causing short circuit

Remedies.Name: Schedule a free repair with an authorized Polaris dealer; instructions available online

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic and Pro XD Kinetic ROVs due to fire hazards. Check your VIN for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: Mammut Sports Group is recalling its Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets, which are designed for mountain and rock climbing assistance. These sets feature a blue cord and an orange and gray carabiner. The recall specifically affects model numbers 2040-02840, 2040-02850, and 2040-02870, which can be located on a tag inside the pouch of the product.
recalls.Title: Mammut Sports Group Issues Recall for Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets Due to Fall Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should stop using the Via Ferrata Sets immediately and contact Mammut Sports Group at 802-800-2593 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at warranty.usa@mammut.com or visit https://www.mammut.com/us/en and click on the "Recall for Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata" banner, or access https://mammut.dani-o.com/voluntary-recall-mammut-skywalker-pro-via-ferrata-set for further details.

Products.Name: Skywalker Pro Via Ferrata Sets

recalls.Description: Endless Pools has issued a recall for their Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers due to potential drowning and entrapment hazards. These covers feature a hand crank system that allows users to easily cover and uncover their custom Endless Pools. Available in various colors such as tan, black, gray, light blue, and royal blue, they are designed to fit pools ranging from 8 to 16 feet wide, based on individual customization.
recalls.Title: Endless Pools Recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers Due to Drowning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Endless Pools at 800-910-2714 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at poolhelp@endlesspools.com, or visit https://myendlesspool.com/s/support/product-recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 11,500 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Manual Retractable Pool Security Cover in open position
Wall Mount Configuration
Below Deck Mount Configuration

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Endless Pools, of Aston, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Endless Pools and authorized dealers nationwide, as well as online at www.endlesspools.com, from January 1994 through December 2024, priced between $1,000 and $2,200.

Hazards.Name: Improper installation of the pool covers can create a gap exceeding 4.5 inches between the cover roller and the pool surface, leading to drowning and entrapment risks for children.

Remedies.Name: Pool owners should contact Endless Pools for a complimentary inspection and repair by a qualified representative. Until repairs are made, children should not be allowed near the pool cover.

recalls.metaDescription: Endless Pools recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers due to drowning hazards. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description:
Yamaha has broadened its recall to include approximately 352,000 golf car vehicles due to a potential crash hazard. This expansion affects various models including the 2016 Yamaha Drive Gas/Electric and models from 2017 to 2024. The issue arises when the accelerator pedal spring may not return to idle, increasing the risk of a crash. Consumers are advised to stop using these vehicles immediately, even if they have previously received repairs under earlier recalls.

The recall now includes a free replacement of the accelerator pedal return spring assembly. Yamaha is reaching out to registered owners directly with details on scheduling this replacement. Authorized Yamaha Golf Car dealers are ready to assist with mobile service or transportation of the units if needed.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Expands Recall of Golf Car Vehicles Due to Crash Hazard; New Repair for Accelerator Pedal Spring Assembly
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027 anytime or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and click on 'CPSC Recalls' for further information.

Products.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 352,000 (Yamaha previously recalled 11,000 golf cars on July 6, 2023; 5,000 on February 17, 2022; 145,000 on October 3, 2018 and 161,000 on February 22, 2018.)

Images.Caption: Recalled Adventurer Two
Recalled Concierge 4
Recalled Drive2 Fleet
Recalled UMAX Rally 2 + 2
Recalled UMAX Two

Injuries.Name: Yamaha has received at least 2,200 reports of incidents involving accelerator pedal return spring failures. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Yamaha Motor Corporation U.S.A., of Cypress, California

Retailers.Name: Available through Yamaha dealers nationwide from July 2015 to December 2024, priced between $5,970 and $13,220.

Hazards.Name: Failure of the accelerator pedal spring to return to idle, leading to crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Free replacement of the accelerator pedal return spring assembly.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 352,000 golf car vehicles due to crash risks. Free replacement of accelerator pedal spring assembly offered.

recalls.Description: Specialized Bicycle Components has issued a recall for the 13-inch telescopic seat posts found on Globe-branded Haul ST and Haul LT e-bikes. These models are identifiable by the names "Haul ST" and "Haul LT" printed on the downtube of the bicycle. The recall is due to a potential fall hazard if the seat post breaks when fully extended.
recalls.Title: Recall of Specialized Globe Haul ST and LT E-Bikes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Specialized Bicycle Components at 877-808-8154 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. They can also email ridercare@specialized.com, use the Live Chat feature at http://www.specialized.com, or visit http://www.specialized.com/safety-recall-notices for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled HAUL-ST e-bike
Recalled Haul LT e-bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents, including two minor injuries consisting of scrapes and bruises.

Retailers.Name: Authorized Specialized retailers and bicycle stores nationwide, as well as online at www.specialized.com, from January 2023 through March 2025, priced between $2,500 and $3,500.

Importers.Name: Specialized Bicycle Components Inc., of Morgan Hill, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall due to potential breakage of the seat post

Remedies.Name: Stop using the e-bikes immediately and contact an authorized Specialized retailer for a free repair involving a new seat post made from a different material.

recalls.metaDescription: Specialized recalls Globe Haul ST and LT e-bikes due to a fall hazard from faulty seat posts. Contact for repairs and safety details.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to BowFlex's Model 552 (52.5 LB) and Model 1090 (90 LB) Adjustable Dumbbells. These dumbbells, sold in pairs and individually in black, come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic tray. The Model 552 adjusts from 5 to 52.5 pounds, while the Model 1090 ranges from 10 to 90 pounds, utilizing manual adjustment knobs. Model and serial numbers are located on a sticker on the bottom of the tray. Specific serial number ranges are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Johnson Health Tech Trading Recalls BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Impact Risk Affecting 3.7 Million Units
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech Trading at 800-209-3539 from 8 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email recall@bowflex.com, or visit www.bowflex.com/recalls. Alternatively, visit www.bowflex.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 552 52.5 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell
Recalled BowFlex 1090 90 LB Adjustable Dumbbell serial number location

Injuries.Name: Johnson Health Tech Trading has received 12 reports of the plates dislodging during use with no injuries for units it sold. Nautilus received 337 reports of the plates dislodging during use for units it sold, including 111 resulting in injuries such as concussions, abrasions, broken toes or contusions.

Retailers.Name: Johnson Fitness & Wellness

Importers.Name: BowFlex Inc., (formerly Nautilus Inc.), of Vancouver, Washington
Johnson Health Tech Trading Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use, creating an impact hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled dumbbells and contact Johnson Health Tech Trading for a refund via a voucher or a replacement for units sold by them. For those sold by BowFlex Inc. (formerly Nautilus Inc.), refunds will be prorated along with a one-year JRNY digital fitness membership. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for the return of the handle and base for each dumbbell.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of BowFlex Adjustable Dumbbells due to impact hazard. Contact Johnson Health Tech Trading for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: Playworld Systems is recalling its Unity Teeter Tunnels, identified by part numbers ZZXX0182, ZZXX0182S, ZZXX0192, and ZZXX0192S. These seesaws are designed for multiple children to stand, sit, lie down, and crawl into a tunnel. The part numbers can be found on the original purchase order and installation instructions.
recalls.Title: Playworld Systems Recalls Unity Teeter Tunnels Due to Serious Injury and Crush Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Playworld Systems at 800-233-8404 from 8 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recalls@playworld.com, or visit https://playworld.com/recall-information. Additional details can be found by clicking on the 'Teeter Tunnel Recall Safety Information' link at the bottom of their homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (first model)
Recalled Unity Teeter Tunnel with Lexan Top (second model)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of children falling under the end of the seesaw plank of the recalled Unity Teeter Tunnels. One child sustained a liver laceration when the end of the seesaw plank landed on her abdomen and the second child suffered fractures to his right tibia and right fibula when the end of the seesaw plank landed on him.

Manufacturers.Name: Playworld Systems Inc., of Lewisburg, Pennsylvania

Retailers.Name: Available through Playworld Systems and authorized independent sales representatives nationwide from October 2014 to March 2025, priced between $13,450 and $13,600.

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury and crush hazard if a child falls underneath the seesaw.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled teeter tunnels and contact Playworld Systems to arrange a free repair. A certified playground installer will visit each location to perform the necessary repairs. Playworld Systems is reaching out to all known purchasers and advises that the tunnels be taken out of service until repairs are completed.

recalls.metaDescription: Playworld Systems recalls Unity Teeter Tunnels due to serious injury risks. Contact them for a free repair and safety information.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the 2023-2025 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar and Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar Crew recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in various colors including black, blue, brown, camouflage, gray, orange, red, tan, and white, come in three and six-seat configurations and feature the "POLARIS" logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box on the driver's side.

The recall stems from an issue with the lower seat belt anchor fasteners, which may not have been adequately tightened. This defect can lead to the seat belt detaching from the vehicle frame, increasing the risk of rider ejection during a crash and posing a significant injury hazard.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar Models Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information can be found online at www.polaris.com under "Product Safety News" or by visiting https://www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls/. Owners can also check if their VIN is affected by contacting Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center.

Products.Name: 2023-2025 Ranger XP 1000 NorthStar ROV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 640 (In addition, about 24 ROVs were sold in Canada)

recalls.Description: KTM has issued a recall for specific model years of GASGAS and Husqvarna off-road motorcycles due to a potential crash hazard linked to a defective front brake caliper. The affected models include motorcycles from 2022 to 2025, identifiable by specific letters in the 10th position of the vehicle identification number (VIN). Consumers should check their VIN, located on the right side of the steering head, to see if their motorcycle is impacted by this recall.
recalls.Title: KTM Recalls GASGAS and Husqvarna Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact GASGAS and Husqvarna Motorcycles at 888-985-6090, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information can be found online at https://www.gasgas.com/en-us.html or https://www.husqvarna-motorcycles.com/en-us.html under 'Safety Recall Information'. Consumers can verify their VIN at https://www.husqvarna-motorcycles.com/en-us/service/Service-and-safety-check.html or https://www.gasgas.com/en-us/service/service-and-safety-check.html.

Images.Caption: Recalled 2024 GASGAS EX 350F
Recalled 2022 HUSQVARNA TE 250i

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: KTM AG, of Austria

Retailers.Name: Authorized GASGAS and Husqvarna dealers across the U.S. from January 2022 to August 2024, priced between $10,000 and $13,000.

Importers.Name: KTM North America Inc., of Amherst, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Cracked front brake caliper leading to reduced braking effectiveness and crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease riding the recalled motorcycles and reach out to an authorized GASGAS or Husqvarna dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair. It's crucial to have the front brake caliper checked by a dealer to assess its condition. KTM/GASGAS and Husqvarna Mobility are directly notifying all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: KTM recalls GASGAS and Husqvarna off-road motorcycles due to a crash hazard. Check if your motorcycle is affected and learn how to get it repaired.

recalls.Description:
NICREW is recalling its Submersible RGB LED Lights, model N21743, due to a serious risk of injury or death from ingestion hazards associated with the remote control's lithium coin battery. These lights are packaged in a 15-inch black tube and come with a black remote featuring 21 buttons. The design does not comply with federal safety regulations regarding products with coin batteries, as it lacks necessary warnings and makes the battery easily accessible to children.

Consumers should immediately cease use of the recalled lights, remove the battery, and store it out of children's reach. Nicrew is offering free replacements for the remote and guidance on safe disposal of the product. To receive a replacement, customers must email their Amazon order number along with a photo showing the destroyed remote to nicrew@support.com. Nicrew and Amazon are proactively reaching out to all known buyers regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights Due to Ingestion Hazard and Regulatory Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Nicrew at nicrew@support.com or visit www.nicrew.com/n21743-recall/ and click on Product Recalls at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights with Remote Control

Images.Caption: Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control
Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control packaging

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.Amazon.com from May 2024 through August 2024, priced around $17.

Distributors.Name: Nicrew Technology Co. Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible lithium coin battery in the remote control, violating federal safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the product, remove the battery, and request a free remote replacement from Nicrew.

recalls.metaDescription: NICREW recalls Submersible RGB LED Lights over ingestion risks from coin batteries. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Igloo has expanded its recall to include an additional 130,000 units of the 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers, manufactured before January 2024. The manufacturing date can be found imprinted on the cooler's bottom, indicating the month and year. These coolers feature a tow handle and display the "IGLOO" logo on their sides, available in various color combinations. The recall affects specific models with tow handles, identifiable by their model numbers located on labels at the bottom of the coolers. Model numbers affected include LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER and MAXCOLD LATITUDE 90 QT ROLLER, with production dates ranging from July 2020 to February 2023.
recalls.Title: Igloo Expands Recall of 90 Qt. Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Igloo at 888-943-5182, Monday through Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com. More information is available at www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or by visiting http://www.igloocoolers.com and selecting "Recall Information" at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 130,000 additional coolers (In addition, about 20,000 in Canada and 5,900 in Mexico) (More than one million Igloo coolers were previously recalled in February 2025)

Remedies.Name: Free replacement handle for recalled coolers.

recalls.Description: Kubota Tractor Corporation is recalling its RTV-Series Utility Vehicles due to a serious crash hazard. The recall affects specific models, with details on the model numbers and serial number ranges provided below. Consumers can find the model numbers on the side of the vehicle and the serial numbers on the left side, behind the driver, where the plate is located at the rear junction of the bed and cab. These utility vehicles are available in various colors such as orange, black, and camouflage.
recalls.Title: Kubota Recalls RTV-Series Utility Vehicles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Kubota Tractor Corporation at 888-458-2682, available from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/service-support/safety/safety-notices or by visiting www.kubotausa.com and selecting "Safety & Recall" at the bottom of the page. A contact form is also available at https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/contact/form.

Recalled Kubota Model RTV520
Recalled Kubota Model RTV-X1120
Recalled Kubota Model RTV-X1140

Distributors.Name: Kubota Tractor Corporation, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to potential separation of the front suspension arm's ball joint.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled vehicles immediately and contact an authorized Kubota dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair. Kubota will also reach out to registered owners directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Kubota recalls RTV-Series Utility Vehicles due to crash hazards. Contact Kubota for inspection and repair details.

recalls.Description: Santa Cruz Bicycles has announced a recall of its Heckler 9 electric bicycles due to a potential fire hazard linked to the lithium-ion battery. These bikes feature a 720W battery housed in a black case beneath the frame, with "SANTA CRUZ" on the downtube and "Heckler" on the top tube and inside the left chainstay. Available in various colors, including Gloss Avocado Green and Maritime Gray, these bicycles pose a risk if the battery overheats. Proper disposal of the recalled batteries is crucial, as they cannot be thrown away in regular trash or recycling bins. Contact your local household hazardous waste collection center for guidance on safe disposal options.
recalls.Title: Santa Cruz Bicycles Issues Recall for Heckler 9 Electric Bikes Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Santa Cruz Bicycles at 833-944-8335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://santacruzbicycles.com/pages/recall-heckler-9-lithium-ion-battery for more details.

Products.Name: Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,850 (In addition, about 550 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle serial number on battery pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of fires, including some property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through Santa Cruz dealers across the country from October 2021 to January 2025, priced between $8,750 and $13,900.

Distributors.Name: Santa Cruz Bicycles LLC, of Santa Cruz, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bike, disconnect the battery, and register for a free replacement battery online.

recalls.metaDescription: Santa Cruz recalls Heckler 9 electric bikes due to fire hazards from lithium-ion batteries. Learn how to safely dispose and replace your battery.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall for the 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X motorcycles, which are two-wheel competition and cross-country models available in lime green. This recall affects specific model numbers and vehicle identification numbers (VINs) that can be found on the right side of the vehicle and frame head pipe respectively. These motorcycles could pose a crash hazard due to a potential failure of the primary gear for the crank shaft.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X Motorcycles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Kawasaki Motors USA at 866-802-9381 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.kawasaki.com/en-us/owner-center/recalls for more details.

Importers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Corp. U.S.A., of California

Hazards.Name: Crank shaft primary gear failure leading to crash risk

Remedies.Name: Stop using the motorcycles immediately and contact a Kawasaki dealer for a free repair, which includes replacing the faulty gear.

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X motorcycles due to crash hazard from gear failure. Contact for repairs.

recalls.Description: Lil Pick Up has announced a recall of the E-Bully Youth Electric 1000W ATVs, which were sold under various names such as 'Seangles' and 'Offroad Mall.' A warning plate on the left front side indicates that the product is part of a safety action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission. The ATVs were available in multiple colors including blue, green, white, red, and black.

recalls.Description: Honda has issued a recall for accessory heaters designed for the 2023-2025 Pioneer 700, 2022-2024 Pioneer 1000, and 2022-2024 Talon 1000 side-by-side vehicles. These heaters, sold separately from the vehicles, feature a control panel mounted on the dashboard that manages air speed, direction, and temperature. To determine if your vehicle is affected, visit https://powersports.honda.com/recalls and enter your VIN. The VIN can be found in the Technical Information - Vehicle Identification section of the owner's manual.
recalls.Title: Honda Recalls Accessory Heaters for 2022-2025 Pioneer and Talon Side-by-Side Vehicles Due to Fire Risks

recalls.Description: Specialized Bicycle Components is recalling their Vado and Como IGH e-bikes equipped with chain and belt guards. These e-bikes feature an internal gear hub and lack a rear derailleur. The Specialized brand is displayed on the bike frame, and the models are available in various colors including black, red, and purple. Model numbers can be found on the white and black serial number label under the top tube of the bike. The affected models include COMO 3.0 IGH, COMO 5.0 IGH, VADO 3.0 IGH, and others.
recalls.Title: Recall of Specialized Vado and Como IGH E-Bikes Due to Fall Risk from Chain Guards

recalls.Description: Segway is recalling all Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters due to a potential fall hazard. The Max G30LP is gray with yellow accents, while the Max G30P features a black design with yellow accents. The brand name 'ninebot' is visible on the foot platform and handlebars, with the model number located on a label on the side of the foot deck. The dimensions for the Max G30P are 46 inches long, 19 inches wide, 47 inches high, and it weighs 42 pounds. The Max G30LP measures 44 inches long, 19 inches wide, 45 inches high, and weighs 39 pounds.
recalls.Title: Segway Recalls Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Segway at 800-914-6110 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. Monday through Friday, email recall@segway.com, or visit https://service.segway.com/recall or www.segway.com and click on 'Recall Center' for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Segway Max G30P KickScooter
Recalled Segway Max G30LP KickScooter

Injuries.Name: Segway has received 68 reports of folding mechanism failures, including 20 injuries to include abrasions, bruises, lacerations and broken bones.

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Costco, Walmart, Target, and Sam's Club nationwide, as well as online at Segway.com and Amazon.com from January 2020 through February 2025, priced between $600 and $1,000.

Importers.Name: Segway Inc., of Los Angeles, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of handlebars or stem folding unexpectedly during use, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the scooters immediately and contact Segway for guidance on whether the folding mechanism requires adjustment. A free maintenance kit will be provided, including tools and instructions for checking and maintaining the folding mechanism.

recalls.metaDescription: Segway recalls Ninebot Max G30P and G30LP KickScooters due to fall hazards. Stop using them and contact Segway for a free maintenance kit.

recalls.Description: Bombardier Recreational Products (BRP) has issued a recall for specific Model Year 2025 Ski-Doo and Lynx snowmobiles that feature a 600R E-TEC, 850 E-TEC, or 850 E-TEC Turbo R engine. The brand name 'Ski-Doo' along with the model name and engine type can be found on the side panels of these vehicles. Consumers can locate the vehicle identification number (VIN) on a decal situated on the right side of the tunnel beneath the seat, and dealers are available to verify which VINs are affected by this recall. These snowmobiles were available in multiple color combinations.
recalls.Title: BRP Recalls Ski-Doo and Lynx Snowmobiles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BRP at 888-272-9222 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, seven days a week, or visit their website at https://ski-doo.brp.com/ca/en/owner-zone/responsible-rider/safety/safety-recalls/potential-loose-part-in-throttle-body-area-risk-of-stuck-throttle.html or https://ski-doo.brp.com/us/en/ and select 'Safety Recalls' at the bottom. For Lynx snowmobiles, visit https://www.brplynx.com/ca/en/owner-zone/safety/safety-recalls/potential-loose-part-in-throttle-body-area-risk-of-stuck-throttle.html or https://www.brplynx.com/ca/en/owner-zone/safety/safety-recalls.html.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for the Aiper Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool cleaners, model number ZT6001. These pool vacuums weigh around 24 pounds and have dimensions of approximately 19.5 x 18.5 x 15 inches. The 'Aiper' brand is displayed on the front of the vacuum, while the model name and serial number, starting with S/N '61', can be found on a label located on the bottom of the unit.
recalls.Title: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaners Recalled Due to Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are advised to stop using the affected pool vacuums immediately and reach out to Aiper at 888-968-0666 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@aiper.com or visit https://aiper.com/seagullpro-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,660 (In addition, about 2,530 were sold in Canada)

Label with model name and serial number located on the bottom of the recalled pool vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the recalled pool vacuums melting, smoking or catching on fire while charging causing five incidents of property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Dongguan Yuanhong Intelligent Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Home Depot, Lowe's, Walmart, and online at www.aiper.com, Amazon.com, Bestbuy.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, and Walmart.com from March 2023 through May 2024, priced between $700 and $900.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Aiper Intelligent Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating current adapter leading to burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a free replacement Aiper Scuba S1 robotic pool cleaner and a free return label for the recalled unit. Aiper will ship the replacement within 15 days of receiving the returned product.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall for Aiper Seagull Pro pool cleaners due to fire hazards. Contact Aiper for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Kimpex is recalling its Rush and Arrow II Ski Kits, along with the corresponding adaptor kits, due to a serious safety concern. These kits, which are designed to replace skis on snowmobiles, come in various colors including red, green, yellow, white, black, and blue. The brand name "Kimpex" can be found stamped on both sides of the ski handles.
recalls.Title: Kimpex Recalls Rush and Arrow II Snowmobile Ski and Adaptor Kits Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Kimpex Inc. at 800-561-6461, available from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email service@kimpex.com or visit https://www.kimpex.com/en-us/page/safety-recalls for further details. The company is proactively contacting all known purchasers.

Products.Name: Kimpex Rush Ski Kit
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,980 (In addition, 2,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kimpex Rush ski kit and adaptor kit

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the skis falling off snowmobiles. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Kimpex Inc., of Drummondville, Canada

Retailers.Name: Available through Kimpex USA dealers across the country and online at Kimpex.com and other retailers from April 2022 to January 2025, priced between $300 and $500 for the ski kits and approximately $23 for the adaptor kits.

Distributors.Name: Kimpex (U.S.A.) Ltd., of Champlain, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of skis detaching from the snowmobile, leading to potential crashes and serious injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the affected snowmobiles immediately and contact a Kimpex dealer for a complimentary repair kit.

recalls.metaDescription: Kimpex recalls Rush and Arrow II snowmobile ski kits due to crash hazards. Stop using immediately and contact Kimpex for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description:
Tzumi Electronics is recalling the FitRx SmartBell XL Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbells, which range from 10 to 90 pounds. These adjustable dumbbells, model 9034, feature a black design with red accents and come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic storage tray. Users can adjust the weight in five-pound increments by turning the handle while the dumbbell is in the storage tray. The model and serial number can be found on the side of the storage tray, with 'FitRx' printed on both the tray and the handles.

The recall is due to a safety hazard where the weight plates may detach from the dumbbell handle during use, potentially causing impact injuries. Consumers are advised to stop using the dumbbells immediately and reach out to Tzumi Electronics for a free repair that includes a new handle and storage tray.
recalls.Title: Tzumi Electronics Recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Injury Risk; Sold Only at Walmart.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Tzumi Electronics at 866-363-2237 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email XLRecall@tzumi.com, or visit www.myfitrx.com/Recall or www.fitrxrecovery.com and select 'RECALL' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: FitRx SmartBell XL Adjustable Dumbbells (10-90 lbs.)

Images.Caption: Recalled FitRx SmartBell XL, Quick-Select Adjustable Dumbbell (10-90 lbs.), Model 9034
Location of model number and serial number on the side of the unit's storage tray

Injuries.Name: There have been more than 60 reports of weight plates dislodging during use, which have been linked to at least seven injuries, including bruises, contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.walmart.com from September 2023 to June 2024, priced between $150 and $200.

Importers.Name: Tzumi Electronics Inc., of New York

Hazards.Name: Potential impact injury from dislodging weight plates

Remedies.Name: Free repair with new dumbbell handle and storage tray

recalls.metaDescription: Tzumi recalls FitRx SmartBell XL Dumbbells due to injury risks. Contact Tzumi for a free repair. Sold only at Walmart.com.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has announced a recall of all BD Vision Harnesses due to a potential fall hazard. This lightweight harness, intended for technical alpine and ice climbing, is available in all sizes and features fixed leg loops. The harness is offered in black and white, with "Black Diamond" printed on one leg loop and "Vision" on the other. Consumers should be aware of the risks associated with using these harnesses.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for BD Vision Climbing Harnesses Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond at 866-306-0865 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, email VisionHarnessRecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ for more details.

Products.Name: BD Vision Climbing Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,580 (In addition, about 130 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness
Recalled Black Diamond Vision Harness (label on side of leg loop)

Injuries.Name: Black Diamond has received one report involving the harness waist belt failing. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), and various specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from January 2018 through February 2025 for approximately $175.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to degradation over time, risking serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled harnesses immediately and contact Black Diamond for a full refund or a one-time-use $200 credit for future purchases. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the harness.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Vision Climbing Harnesses due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds or credits.

recalls.Description: BMC is recalling all Kaius 01 bicycles and framesets due to a potential fall risk. The affected models were available in various colors including Peacock Spider, Carbon Black/Brushed Blue, Purple/White, White/Black, Turquoise/Black, Saffron/Black, and Stone Grey. The BMC logo is displayed on the downtube. This recall specifically impacts bicycles and framesets equipped with a V5 fork, which need to be replaced.
recalls.Title: BMC Issues Recall for Kaius 01 Bicycles and Framesets Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BMC USA at 888-262-7755 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email kaius-fork-recall-USA@BMC-Switzerland.com. More information is available online at www.bmc-switzerland.com/pages/platform/kaius-01-voluntary-recall or by visiting www.BMC-Switzerland.com and selecting "Learn More" under the "Voluntary Recall for Inspection" section.

recalls.Description: Sublue has issued a recall for specific lithium-ion batteries and replacement batteries used in Sublue Mix underwater scooters. The affected batteries are exclusively black, while the scooters are available in arctic white, space blue, and aqua blue. Each scooter features the name 'Sublue' on top and 'Whiteshark Mix Underwater Scooter' on the side, with the battery compartment located underneath the unit.
recalls.Title: Sublue Mix Underwater Scooters and Batteries Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Sublue at 855-206-8698 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, seven days a week, email support.us@sublue.com, or visit www.sublue.com/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Sublue Mix Underwater Scooters and Black Replacement Batteries

Injuries.Name: Sublue has received 161 reports of thermal and swelling incidents involving the lithium-ion batteries, including eight reports of fire, one report of injury, and two reports of property damage totaling $651,400.

Importers.Name: Sublue Technology Inc., of Walnut, California

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment has announced a recall for its BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers, which feature a plastic body and an LCD display. These devices, characterized by their gray exterior and blue top, bear the Black Diamond logo and the model name "RECON LT" on the front. The affected transceivers were sold individually as well as part of Black Diamond's Alpine Avy Safety Sets and Recon LT Avy Safety Sets.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Issues Recall for BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceivers Due to Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled avalanche transceivers immediately and contact Black Diamond at 866-306-0865, available from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. They can also reach out via email at 2025reconltrecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ for further details.

Products.Name: BD Recon LT Avalanche Transceiver
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,660 (In addition, about 206 were sold in Canada). The transceivers were recalled in 2023 for a different hazard

Images.Caption: Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (front)
Recalled Black Diamond Recon LT avalanche transceiver (back)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the switch mechanism malfunctioning due to corrosion. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Liberty Mountain, Recreational Equipment Inc. (REI), and various specialty outdoor retailers across the country, as well as online at www.blackdiamondequipment.com, www.backcountry.com, and www.evo.com from May 2021 through February 2025, priced between $350 and $500.

Importers.Name: Black Diamond Equipment Ltd., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Corrosion in the switch mechanism may lead to malfunction, risking loss of communication and potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers can receive a full refund, a replacement transceiver, or a free repair. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns.

recalls.metaDescription: Black Diamond recalls BD Recon LT avalanche transceivers due to communication failure risk. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies has announced a recall for its TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belay Devices, which are designed to ensure safe descents for climbers. The TRUBLUE iQ model features a gray body with blue handles, while the TRUBLUE iQ+ includes an additional catch-and-hold brake. Variants of these models, such as the LT versions, have blue tags, and the XL versions are marked with purple tags. The recall affects devices with specific serial number ranges printed on their handles and bottoms, indicating potential safety concerns.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Issues Recall for TRUBLUE iQ and TRUBLUE iQ+ Auto Belay Devices Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email info@headrushtech.com, or visit https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/SA-0005-v01-en.pdf. More information is also available at www.headrushtech.com by clicking the "Recall" banner.

Recalled TruBlue iQ+ Auto Belay System

Serial Number Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 109 reports of incidents of slow retraction, including one injury involving the auto belay not retracting, resulting in a fractured leg.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2023 through December 2024, priced between $2,800 and $4,600.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to potential failure to retract

Remedies.Name: Free onsite inspection and repair

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert for TRUBLUE iQ and iQ+ Auto Belay Devices due to fall hazards. Contact Head Rush Technologies for inspection and repair.

The affected units have serial numbers ranging from NBP00052-1027 to NBP00287-1127 and NB00300-1127 to NB11675-0329, found inside the battery compartment lid.
recalls.Title: ARVA Recalls NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Transmission Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach ARVA at 844-636-2782 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://us.arva-equipment.com/content/1488-recall and select 'USA' for further details.

Products.Name: ARVA NEO BT Pro Avalanche Transceiver

Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver
Recalled ARVA NEO BT PRO Avalanche Transceiver

Importers.Name: Nic Impex ARVA Corp., of Orem, Utah

recalls.Description: Canyon Bicycles USA has announced a recall for all Speedmax CF triathlon bicycle models R073 and R41 featuring V21 aerostems. These carbon fiber bikes, available in color combinations of black/red, black/blue, gray/orange, and red/black, are marked with "Canyon" on the downtube and "Speedmax" on the top tube. The model number is located on a sticker just below the saddle on the top of the seat tube. Consumers are urged to check their bicycles for the model identification.
recalls.Title: Canyon Bicycles USA Issues Recall for Speedmax CF Triathlon Bikes Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please contact Canyon Bicycles USA at 833-226-9661 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or reach out via chat at www.canyon.com/en-us/contact/. Additional information is available at https://www.canyon.com/en-us/customer-service/repair-spares-warranty/speedmax-cf.html or by visiting https://www.canyon.com/en-us/ and selecting "Speedmax Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Canyon Speedmax CF Triathlon Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 380 were sold in Canada)

The model number can be found on a sticker at the top of the seat tube, just below the saddle

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase at Canyon.com from May 2017 through July 2024, priced between $4,000 and $5,000.

Importers.Name: Canyon Bicycles USA Inc., of Carlsbad, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential cracking or breaking of the aerostem fork steerer tube.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the bicycles immediately and contact a Canyon Authorized Service Partner for a complimentary repair. This service includes the inspection and potential replacement of the fork steerer tube, along with reinforcement of the clamping area. Canyon is reaching out directly to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Canyon Bicycles recalls Speedmax CF triathlon bikes due to safety hazards. Learn how to get a free repair and check if your bike is affected.

recalls.Description: SuperATV has issued a recall for specific carrier bearings designed for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S models. These parts, sold between April 2017 and September 2022, are identifiable by their silver and black color and the 'SuperATV' engraving on the housing. The affected bearings are located beneath the center console tunnel in two-seat models and behind the rear seats in four-seat models. The recall affects three specific models purchased within designated date ranges.
recalls.Title: SuperATV Recalls Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact SuperATV at 855-728-8256, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. To check if your carrier bearing is affected, visit https://form.jotform.com/243023918871055 or go to https://www.superatv.com/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: SuperATV Carrier Bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S

Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-002
Recalled SuperATV Carrier Bearing Model BEA01-005 (Rev A00 and A01)
Location of SuperATV Brand Name on Carrier Bearing

Injuries.Name: SuperATV has received 24 reports of the fuel tank contacting the prop shaft, including 19 reports that a hole or crack had formed in the fuel tank, including one reported fire. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: SuperATV, of Madison, Indiana

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.superatv.com and through off-road vehicle dealers across the country from April 2017 to September 2022, priced between $85 and $250 for one bearing, and $170 to $420 for a set of two.

Hazards.Name: Fire and Burn Hazards from Fuel Tank Leaks

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled bearings, remove them, and register online for a return label. Free replacement bearings will be sent upon receipt of the recalled items.

recalls.metaDescription: SuperATV recalls carrier bearings for Polaris RZR XP Turbo and Turbo S due to fire hazards. Check if your part is affected and get replacements.

recalls.Description: Super73 is recalling its 2024 Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core electric bicycles due to potential safety hazards. The Z Miami SE models are available in Bandit Black, Palladium Gray, and Astro Orange, while the Z Adventure Core is offered in Sandstorm. The model name and color can be found on the purchase receipt, aiding in identification.
recalls.Title: Super73 Recalls Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core Electric Bicycles Due to Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Super73 at 888-841-3584 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email safety@super73.com, or visit https://super73.com/pages/product-recall. They can also access the 'Product Safety Advisory' link on the homepage for further details.

Recalled Super73 ZX Adventure Core e-bike - Model Year 2024

Retailers.Name: Available at Super73's store in Irvine, CA, various bicycle retailers across the country, and online at https://Super73.com from April 2024 through September 2024, priced between $2,300 and $2,500.

recalls.metaDescription: Super73 recalls Z Miami SE and Z Adventure Core electric bicycles due to crash risks. Contact for free repair kit and reimbursement.

recalls.Description: The recall affects the 2024 and 2025 model years of the Triumph TF250-X closed course competition motorcycles. These black motorcycles have vehicle identification numbers (VIN) ranging from BP6028 to CC7868, located on the left side of the steering head. The name "Triumph" is displayed on the seats. To determine if your VIN is part of this recall, visit https://www.triumphmotorcycles.com/owners/recalls.
recalls.Title: Recall of Triumph TF250-X Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Triumph Motorcycles at 888-284-6288 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at tma.aftersales@triumphmotorcycles.com or visit https://www.triumphmotorcycles.com/owners/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Triumph TF250-X Closed Course Competition Motorcycles

Motorcycle's right switch cube

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 16 incident reports of the switch cube cracking. Seven of those resulting in the motorcycle starting unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Triumph dealers across the country from July 2023 to December 2024 for approximately $9,500.

Importers.Name: Triumph Motorcycles America Ltd., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected motorcycle start due to potential short circuit from cracked switch cube.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the motorcycles immediately, disconnect the battery, and contact an authorized Triumph dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes replacing the right-hand switch cube. Triumph Motorcycles America will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Triumph recalls TF250-X off-road motorcycles due to injury risks. Check your VIN and contact Triumph for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Holosun Technologies is recalling two models of Firearm Dot Sights: the HS503G-ACSS Red Dot Sight and the HE512C-GD Gold Dot Sight. These sights are designed to be mounted on firearms to provide an illuminated aiming point. Each product is identified by its model name on the packaging, box, manual, and display panel, and includes a matte black dot sight, a CR2032 lithium coin battery, and a user manual.
recalls.Title: Holosun Technologies Recalls Firearm Dot Sights Due to Battery Ingestion Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Holosun Technologies at 888-869-1180 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email cpscinquiries@holosun.com, or visit https://holosun.com/media/news-events-171.html or www.holosun.com and click on "Recall" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight Packaging, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Red Dot Sight Top View, Model HS503G-ACSS
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight Packaging, Model HE512C-GD
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight, Model HE512C-GD
Recalled Holosun Gold Dot Sight, Model HE512C-GD

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Primary Arms, EuroOptic, GRITR Sports, Davidson's, MidwayUSA, Carolina Sporting Arms, Palmetto State Armory, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at opticsplanet.com from February 2023 through August 2024, priced between $250 and $350 depending on the model.

Importers.Name: Holosun Technologies Inc., of City of Industry, California

Hazards.Name: The recalled Holosun Firearm Dot Sights do not comply with federal safety regulations concerning coin batteries, as the lithium battery is easily accessible, creating a risk of ingestion. The products lack child-resistant packaging as mandated by Reese's Law and do not carry the necessary warnings. Ingesting button cell or coin batteries can lead to severe injuries, internal burns, or even death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled Firearm Dot Sights immediately and keep them out of children's reach. They should contact Holosun Technologies for a free recall kit that includes a compliant battery compartment enclosure and updated instructions for the HS503G-ACSS models. For the HE512C-GD models, Holosun will arrange free return shipping to install a compliant battery compartment tray along with a new instruction manual and packaging. Holosun is reaching out to known purchasers directly. Note: Dispose of button cell and coin batteries properly as hazardous waste, not in regular trash.

recalls.metaDescription: Holosun recalls HS503G-ACSS and HE512C-GD Firearm Dot Sights due to battery ingestion hazards. Learn how to get a free recall kit.

recalls.Description: Walmart and Sam's Club have issued a recall for the SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Commuter Electric Scooters due to potential fire hazards linked to their lithium-ion batteries. These scooters, available in black and silver, feature 'SWAGGER 5' prominently displayed on the handlebars. The model numbers, including SWGR5-V2-SLV and SG-5S, are located on the side of the scooter deck. It is crucial to handle the recalled batteries properly, as they must not be disposed of in regular trash or typical battery recycling bins.
recalls.Title: Walmart Recalls Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Electric Scooters Due to Fire Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Walmart at 800-925-6278 from 7 a.m. to 9 p.m. CT daily or visit www.walmart.com/help. For more details about the recall, go to https://corporate.walmart.com/recalls.

Products.Name: Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Electric Scooter

Remedies.Name: Stop using and charging the scooters, cut the throttle cord, and dispose of the product according to local hazardous waste guidelines. Refund instructions will be emailed to purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Walmart recalls Swagtron SG-5 Swagger 5 Boost Electric Scooters due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Trek is recalling its Electra Navigator Go! (2021 and 2022 models) and Electra Ace of Spades Go! e-bikes (2018, 2021, and 2022 models) due to a potential crash hazard. The Navigator Go! features a Matte Nautilus Blue finish with map and ocean graphics, while the Ace of Spades Go! is presented in Matte Black adorned with a gold spade. The brand name 'Electra' is visible on the chain guard of the Navigator Go! and on both the down tube and chain guard of the Ace of Spades Go!, with a spade emblem on the top tube of the latter.
recalls.Title: Trek Recalls Electra Navigator Go! and Ace of Spades Go! E-Bikes Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Trek at 800-373-4594 from 8 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.trekbikes.com/us/en_US/company/legal_policies/safety_recalls/ or www.trekbikes.com and select 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled Electra Navigator Go! (2021 & 2022)
Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2021 & 2022) and Recalled Electra Ace of Spades Go! (2018)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the rear fender coming loose and making contact with the rear wheel, including one injury that involved a fractured shinbone, meniscus tear and scrapes.

Retailers.Name: Available at Trek-owned and independent bicycle retailers across the country, as well as online at www.electra.trekbikes.com from June 2018 through August 2023, priced around $3,000.

Importers.Name: Trek Bicycle Corporation, of Waterloo, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of rear fender detachment leading to unexpected stops and loss of control.

Remedies.Name: Free repair to replace rear fender bolts through authorized Trek or Electra retailers.

recalls.metaDescription: Trek recalls Electra Navigator Go! and Ace of Spades Go! e-bikes due to crash hazards. Immediate action recommended for safety.

recalls.Description: Igloo is recalling its 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers manufactured before January 2024 due to safety concerns. These coolers feature a tow handle and have 'IGLOO' printed on the side. The manufacturing date can be found on the bottom of the cooler, indicated in a circular pattern. The recall affects various models sold in different color combinations, with model numbers located on labels attached to the cooler's bottom. Consumers should check their coolers against the model numbers listed in the recall to determine if they are affected.
recalls.Title: Igloo Recalls Over One Million 90 Quart Rolling Coolers Due to Amputation and Crushing Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Igloo at 888-943-5182 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email igloo90qt@sedgwick.com. More information is available at www.igloo90qtrecall.expertinquiry.com or by visiting http://www.igloocoolers.com and selecting 'Recall Information' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: Igloo 90 Qt. Flip & Tow Rolling Coolers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,060,000 (In addition, about 47,000 in Canada and 23,000 in Mexico)

Hazards.Name: Pinching of fingertips against the cooler due to the tow handle can lead to amputation and crushing injuries.

recalls.metaDescription: Igloo recalls over one million 90 Quart Rolling Coolers due to safety hazards. Contact Igloo for a free replacement handle.

recalls.Description: Setsmart has recalled its LoGest Climbing Ropes due to a potential fall risk. These ropes are crafted from hemp and were available in lengths of 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, and 50 feet. The LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner features a silver 'D' ring and a climbing carabiner, while the version with a Heavy-Duty Metal Hook includes a blue stripe and a black metal hook secured with silver bolts and nuts. Both types have 'LoGest' prominently displayed in white on the black plastic ends.
recalls.Title: Setsmart Issues Recall for LoGest Climbing Ropes Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Setsmart via email at LoGestRecall@gmail.com or visit www.LoGestRopeRecall.com.

Products.Name: LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,500 (In addition, about 350 were sold in Canada and about 25 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Carabiner
Recalled LoGest Climbing Rope with Heavy-Duty Metal Hook

Injuries.Name: LoGest has received 13 reports of the recalled ropes breaking, including one report of a fall that resulted in a severe spinal cord injury.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com from March 2021 to November 2024, priced between $20 and $70.

Importers.Name: Setsmart LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of weakening and breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the climbing ropes immediately, uninstall them, and register for a full refund at www.LoGestRopeRecall.com. A form for disposal will be provided, which must be signed and returned with a photo of the disposed rope sent to LoGestRecall@gmail.com to process the refund. Refunds will be issued through Amazon to the original payment method, and Amazon will directly contact all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Setsmart recalls LoGest Climbing Ropes due to fall hazards. Stop using them and register for a refund today.

recalls.Description: Umarex USA is recalling the T4E (Training for Engagement) TR50 Air Pistol due to a potential safety hazard. This CO2-powered air pistol, which is designed for recreational and training purposes, can unintentionally eject the CO2 cartridge with force from the handle, posing an impact risk. The air pistol is approximately nine inches long and holds six .50 caliber rounds, using non-lethal rubber, plastic, or powder ball ammunition. The affected model numbers are 2292112, 2280182, and 2280199, with the T4E logo and 'TR50' engraved on the left side of the muzzle and the serial number on the right side.
recalls.Title: Umarex USA Issues Recall for T4E TR50 Air Pistols Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled air pistols immediately and contact Umarex at 877-212-1036 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET Monday through Friday, email service@umarex.com, or visit http://t4eguns.com/recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled T4E TR50 Air Pistol

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 30 reports of the CO2 cartridge unintentionally ejecting with force, including seven reports of injury, such as fractured hands, broken fingers and contusions.

Retailers.Name: Big 5 stores in the Western U.S., Modern Combat Sports in Vista, CA, and online at https://mcsus.com, Amazon.com, and Valken.com

Importers.Name: Umarex USA, of Fort Smith, Arkansas

Hazards.Name: Risk of CO2 cartridge being ejected with force

Remedies.Name: Free replacement air pistol and free return shipping

recalls.metaDescription: Umarex USA recalls T4E TR50 Air Pistols due to a safety hazard. Contact Umarex for a free replacement and return shipping.

recalls.Description: AMX Global is recalling its Portable Folding Stadium Seats sold under the BJ's Wholesale and Black Sierra brands due to potential fall hazards. The BJ's seats feature the "Berkley Jensen" logo, while the Black Sierra seats display the Black Sierra Equipment logo. These seats fold to a compact size of approximately 4 inches by 20 inches by 19 inches when closed, and have a padded handle and shoulder strap. Specific date codes on the tags indicate which units are affected: Berkley Jensen seats with the date code "04/2022" and Black Sierra seats with date codes "05/2022" or "11/2022" are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: AMX Global Recalls Portable Folding Stadium Seats Due to Fall Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact AMX Global at 888-439-5139 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email support@stadiumseatrecall.com. More information is available online at https://stadiumseatrecall.com/ or by visiting www.amxglobalinc.com and selecting "RECALL INFORMATION" at the top of the page.
