recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America is recalling specific models of Matrix-brand Training Cycles, including CXP-03, CXC-02, CXM-03, CXV, and CXM-02. These cycles feature adjustable saddles and handlebars, an aluminum rear flywheel, and a magnetic resistance system. They are characterized by matte black steel frames and dual-sided SPD pedals. The model and serial numbers can be found on a label located on the lower rear section of the cycle frame. Only cycles with serial numbers starting with FC32, ZFC32B, FC33, FC36, FC29D, or FC27 are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Recall of Matrix-Brand Training Cycles by Johnson Health Tech North America Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled training cycles immediately. For assistance, contact Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email cxrecall@matrixfitness.com, or visit https://www.matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for more details. The company will also reach out to all known purchasers directly.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc. ("JHTNA"), of Cottage Grove, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to unexpected lowering of the adjustable seat during use.

recalls.Description: NQDTPBOR has issued a recall for their multi-purpose helmets designed for teens due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations. These helmets are available in small and medium sizes, accommodating head circumferences of approximately 18 to 20.5 inches and 20.5 to 22 inches, respectively. They come in various colors including blue, pink, purple, green, and black, featuring black padding, straps, and a black and red buckle. The brand name "NQDTPBOR" is visibly marked on the side of each helmet.
recalls.Title: NQDTPBOR Helmets Recalled for Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach out to TPBOR via email at nqdtpbor@outlook.com or visit their Amazon.com Seller Profile: TPBOR.

Images.Caption: Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue)
Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmets (Green)

Recalled NQDTPBOR Multi-Purpose Helmet (Purple)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from May 2024 to August 2024 for approximately $23.

Importers.Name: CLSYS International Inc., of New York

Distributors.Name: Yiwu Baqianliu Trading Co. Ltd., dba TPBOR, of China

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet federal safety standards for positional stability, impact protection, labeling, and certification, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and contact TPBOR for a full refund. To process the refund, consumers should cut the straps of the helmet, take a photo, and email it to nqdtpbor@outlook.com. After providing proof of destruction, the helmet should be disposed of. TPBOR and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: NQDTPBOR recalls helmets due to head injury risks and safety violations. Contact TPBOR for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of their Model Year 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 and Crew XD 1500 recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in camouflage, red, silver, and titanium colors, come in three- and six-seat configurations and feature the 'POLARIS' logo on the front grille. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box. Additionally, accessory doors with part numbers 2212292, 2212291, 2638498, 2638499, 2638500, 2638501, 2889075, or 2889076 sold prior to November 11, 2024, are also part of this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 and Crew XD 1500 ROVs Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers should contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. They can also visit www.polaris.com and click on 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section or check https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to see if their vehicle identification number (VIN) is affected. Additionally, Polaris' owner connections team can be reached at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 ROV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 4,200 ROVs and 393 accessory doors (In addition, about 291 were sold in Canada)

Retailers.Name: Polaris dealers across the U.S. from November 2023 through November 2024, priced between $40,000 and $50,500.

Hazards.Name: Risk of door handle malfunction leading to unexpected openings while driving, potentially causing ejections and crashes.

Remedies.Name: Polaris will reach out to registered owners with guidance on inspecting door handles to ensure they are secure, allowing safe vehicle operation until repairs are made.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls 2024-2025 Ranger XD 1500 and Crew XD 1500 ROVs due to safety hazards. Check if your vehicle is affected.

recalls.Description:
Wemfg is recalling its children's multi-purpose bike helmets due to safety concerns. These helmets, available in size small (S), accommodate head circumferences of approximately 19-1/2 to 21-1/4 inches and come in various color combinations including blue/pink, green/blue, and pink/yellow/green. Each helmet features black padding, straps, and a black and red buckle, with a fitting adjustment knob at the back. The model number "MD-710-S" is labeled inside the helmet.

The helmets do not meet federal safety regulations, posing a risk of head injury in the event of an accident. Consumers are urged to stop using these helmets immediately and follow the outlined steps to receive a full refund.
recalls.Title: Recall of Wemfg Children's Multi-Purpose Bike Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Wemfg via email at 961837339@qq.com, or visit their Amazon Storefront page.

recalls.Description: SIG SAUER is recalling its ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights, specifically Models SOR52001 and 7400579, which may pose an ingestion hazard due to easily accessible button cell batteries. These sights are identifiable by the SIG SAUER logo and ROMEO5 name on one side, with the model number located on the packaging. The affected units have serial numbers ending in either K or M, and consumers can find guidance on locating their serial numbers from SIG SAUER.
recalls.Title: SIG SAUER Issues Recall for ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights Due to Ingestion Risk from Button Cell Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach SIG SAUER at 603-610-3000 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email at ROMEO5Recall@sigsauer.com. More information is available at https://www.sigsauer.com/safety-center or by visiting https://www.sigsauer.com and clicking on 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: SIG SAUER ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights

Images.Caption: Recalled SIG SAUER ROMEO5 Red Dot Firearm Sight
The product serial number is located on the bottom of the sight

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, Sportsman's Warehouse, Academy Sports, Scheels, and various local independent firearms stores, as well as online at Sigsauer.com, Amazon.com, Opticsplanet.com, EuroOptic.com, Palmettostatearmory.com, Midwayusa.com, Chattanoogashooting.com, Gritrsports.com, Bereli.com, Battlehawkarmory.com, and other online optics retailers from February 2023 through December 2024, priced between $130 and $200.

Importers.Name: Sig Sauer Inc., of Newington, New Hampshire

Hazards.Name: The recalled sights have button cell batteries that do not comply with federal safety regulations, presenting a risk of ingestion. These batteries are not packaged in child-resistant containers as mandated by Reese's Law, and lack necessary warnings. Swallowing these batteries can result in severe injuries, internal burns, or even death, particularly for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease use of the recalled sights immediately and contact SIG SAUER for guidance on submitting a photo of the serial number to receive a free recall kit. This kit includes a new cap with proper labeling and an updated manual. Instructions for locating the serial number will be provided. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous and should be disposed of or recycled according to local hazardous waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: SIG SAUER recalls ROMEO5 Red Dot Sights due to ingestion hazards from button cell batteries. Learn how to get a free recall kit.

recalls.Description: BCI Burke is recalling the Inclusive Orbit, a playground spinning component resembling a merry-go-round. Designed for up to six children, it features three handrails and a platform that sits flush with the ground. The affected model numbers are 560-0042 and 560-0051. Owners are encouraged to contact BCI Burke to verify their unit's status.
recalls.Title: BCI Burke Issues Recall for Inclusive Orbit Playground Merry-Go-Rounds Due to Crush Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to BCI Burke Company at 800-266-1250 from 7:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email orbit@bciburke.com, or visit https://www.bciburke.com/recall. You can also go to www.bciburke.com and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Products.Name: Inclusive Orbit Playground Merry-Go-Round
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 415 (In addition, about 6 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Inclusive Orbit merry-go-round

Injuries.Name: The firm has received reports of two incidents of fingers getting trapped in the gap between the rotating platform and the outer rim, resulting in two finger crushing injuries.

Manufacturers.Name: Playdale Playgrounds LTD, of United Kingdom

Retailers.Name: Available through factory representatives directly to municipalities, schools, and churches from April 2019 to October 2024, priced between $11,900 and $14,550.

Importers.Name: BCI Burke Company Inc., of Fond Du Lac, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Crush hazard due to a gap between the rotating platform and the stationary outer rim.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the merry-go-rounds immediately and contact the equipment owner, such as a park director or school principal, for inspection and repair. BCI Burke can be contacted to arrange a free onsite inspection and repair.

recalls.metaDescription: BCI Burke recalls Inclusive Orbit merry-go-rounds due to crush hazards. Learn how to check your unit and arrange repairs.

recalls.Description: Precor has announced a recall of its Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment, Model No. RMS905, which is primarily used in gyms. This equipment features an adjustable pulley carriage that allows users to perform various cable resistance exercises at different heights. The brand name Precor is displayed above the vertical rail, and the model number can be found on a label located on the underside of the pulley assembly's top frame, situated between the vertical rail and the weight stack. The equipment dimensions are 68 inches x 58 inches x 94 inches, and it comes in silver, black, or white frames with multiple upholstery choices.
recalls.Title: Precor Issues Recall for Resolute Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Precor Incorporated at 800-347-4404, via email at support@precor.com, or visit www.precor.com/recall. More details can also be found by navigating to "RECALL INFORMATION" on the Precor website.

Products.Name: Precor Resolute Cable Multi-Station Exercise Equipment

Images.Caption: Recalled Resolute Cable Multi-Station

Injuries.Name: Precor has received nine reports of the pulley dropping during use, including two minor head injuries.

Retailers.Name: The equipment was sold to fitness facilities across the nation from July 2023 to October 2024, with prices ranging from $3,020 to $3,340.

Importers.Name: Precor Incorporated, of Woodinville, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential for pulley carriage to drop unexpectedly due to pop-pin failure, posing a risk of impact injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to cease using the recalled equipment immediately. Precor will reach out to all affected gyms to arrange a complimentary on-site repair by a qualified technician, which includes the installation of a redesigned pop-pin and a new warning label.

recalls.metaDescription: Precor recalls Resolute Cable Multi-Station exercise equipment due to a potential injury risk. Learn how to get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling all PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers due to a potential failure in emergency communication. These transceivers are made of black plastic and feature an LCD screen situated beneath a yellow mark button, measuring approximately 5 inches in length and 3 inches in width. The front of the unit displays 'PIEPS' in yellow and 'PRO IPS' in white. Recalled units have serial numbers ranging from 2326 to 2445, which can be located on the label inside the battery compartment door.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls PIEPS Pro IPS Avalanche Transceivers Due to Emergency Communication Failure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the transceivers immediately and reach out to Black Diamond at 866-306-0865, available from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email 2024ProIPSrecall@bdel.com or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/product-recalls/ for more details.

recalls.Description: Polaris has issued a recall for specific VINs of the Model Year 2024 Ranger 1000, Crew 1000, Ranger XP 1000, Crew XP 1000, Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs, as well as ProXD Full Size and Bobcat UV34 Utility Task Vehicles. These vehicles are available in various colors including blue, bronze, camouflage, gray, green, tan, and white, and come in both three and six seat configurations. The ProXD models feature gray body panels with black accents, while Bobcat vehicles showcase black side panels with white and orange highlights. Each vehicle has identifying marks, including the Polaris logo on the Ranger and ProXD models and the Bobcat logo on the Bobcat vehicles. The VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger and Utility Task Vehicles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section. To verify if your vehicle's VIN is part of the recall, visit https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/. Polaris' Owner Connections Team is also available for Ranger and ProXD inquiries at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/emai. For Bobcat vehicle concerns, contact Bobcat at 800-743-4340 from 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, or check www.bobcat.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: Academy Sports + Outdoors is recalling the Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes due to a critical safety concern. These safes, designed to secure firearms and valuables, may have mechanical internal locking plates that do not align properly, potentially allowing unauthorized access. The 12-Gun Safe measures 53 inches high, 15 inches wide, and 16 inches deep, while the 18-Gun Safe is 55 inches high, 18 inches wide, and 18 inches deep. Both models feature 'Redfield' branding on the front door above the keypad and have model numbers 179498 and 179499 respectively. Affected serial numbers begin with specific codes that consumers should check against their safes.
recalls.Title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Issues Recall for Redfield 12-Gun and 18-Gun Fireproof Safes Due to Serious Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Academy Sports + Outdoors at 888-922-2336 from 7 a.m. to 10 p.m. CT, email customerservice@academy.com, or visit www.academy.com/product-recalls for more details.

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to misaligned locking plates

Remedies.Name: Return for a free replacement or refund

recalls.Description: Todson is recalling Nerf Barrage Youth bike helmets due to safety concerns. These helmets feature a blue and orange design, black straps, and an orange buckle, and are labeled as medium (M) size. The model number 6011 can be found on a label inside the helmet, along with specific lot numbers and reference numbers that are included in this recall. The affected lot numbers are BC01BJ01-200623 with REF# 5382-BL and BC01BJ01-111521 with REF# 4970-BL.
recalls.Title: Todson Issues Recall for Nerf Barrage Youth Bike Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled helmets and contact Todson at 800-278-2565 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. For further assistance, email NerfHelmetRecall@Todson.com or visit www.Todson.com/NerfHelmetRecall for more details.

Recalled Nerf Barrage Youth Bike Helmet (Side view)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with federal safety regulations for helmet retention systems, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

recalls.Description: Segway is recalling its Ninebot KickScooters, model P100S, manufactured in 2023. The affected units have serial numbers starting with date codes 2308 or later (e.g., xxxxx2308xxxxx, xxxx2310xxxxx, xxxxx2311xxxxx). You can find the serial number on the left side of the KickScooter, where the 6th and 7th digits indicate the year of manufacture, and the 8th and 9th digits show the week of manufacture. The KickScooter features a black and gray design with red accents and measures 46 inches in length, 25 inches in width, and 50 inches in height, weighing approximately 72.5 pounds.
recalls.Title: Segway Recalls Ninebot P100 KickScooters Due to Risk of Falls and Injuries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Segway at 866-473-4929, available from 5:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at recall@segway.com, or visit https://service.segway.com/recall or www.segway.com and click on 'Recall Center' for further details.

Hazards.Name: Risk of front fork breakage leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled KickScooters and reach out to Segway for a free repair. Segway is also contacting all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Segway recalls Ninebot P100 KickScooters due to fall hazards. Contact Segway for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: woom Bikes USA is recalling its ORIGINAL 6 bicycles from model years 2016 to 2020, along with the OFF 5, OFF AIR 5, OFF 6, and OFF AIR 6 models from 2019 to 2020. These bicycles are intended for children aged 10 to 14 and feature the brand name 'woom' prominently displayed on the downtube and headtube. The ORIGINAL 6 bikes come in vibrant colors such as red, green, blue, purple, and yellow, while the OFF mountain bikes are designed with black frames and silver forks.
recalls.Title: woom Bikes USA Issues Recall for Children's OFF and ORIGINAL 6 Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact woom Bikes USA at 855-966-6872 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email productsafetyUS@woom.com. More information is available online at https://faq.us.woombikes.com/category/88-recall-notice or visit www.woom.com/en_US/ and navigate to 'Safety and Recalls' to register for the recall using the online crank checker tool.

Images.Caption: The recalled cranks have no space between the well with the woom logo and the pedal thread

Recalled OFF AIR 6 – Black with silver suspension front fork, 26-inch wheels
Recalled ORIGINAL 6, 26-inch wheels

Injuries.Name: The firm has received five reports of the crank breaking, including one report of an injury to a child.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at http://www.woom.com in the U.S. from September 2016 to October 2020, priced between $500 and $900.

Importers.Name: woom Bike USA LLC, of Austin, Texas

Hazards.Name: Crank breakage due to fatigue fractures leading to loss of control and potential falls or crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled bicycles and reach out to woom Bikes USA for guidance on obtaining a complimentary replacement crankset. The online crank checker tool at woom.com can help determine if your bike is affected. Affected cranksets will be replaced for free, with installation by a professional bike technician at the consumer's chosen bike shop. Consumers can submit their repair receipts for reimbursement of crank replacement costs. woom is contacting all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: woom Bikes USA recalls children's OFF and ORIGINAL 6 bicycles due to safety hazards. Learn how to get a free replacement crankset.

recalls.Description:
Polaris Industries has issued a recall for the Model Year 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in camouflage and white colors, feature a three-seat configuration and have the brand name 'POLARIS' displayed on the front grille. The model number and VIN can be found on the left rear frame beneath the cargo box on the driver's side.

The recall is prompted by concerns that loose high voltage cables on the motor controller unit may lead to arcing or overheating during operation, which poses significant fire and crash hazards, potentially resulting in serious injuries. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: Polaris Industries Recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs Due to Fire and Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.polaris.com by clicking on 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section or by visiting https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your vehicle identification number (VIN) is affected. Consumers may also reach out to Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Recreational Off-Road Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,400 (In addition, about 70 were sold in Canada)

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available through Polaris dealers across the nation from May 2023 to September 2024, priced between $24,900 and $37,800.

Hazards.Name: Loose high voltage cables on the motor controller unit can cause arcing or overheating, leading to fire and crash risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled ROVs and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary inspection of the motor controller unit. If any high voltage cables are found to be loose or damaged, Polaris will arrange a free repair at the dealer's location. Registered owners will be contacted directly by Polaris.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs due to fire and crash hazards. Immediate action is required for affected vehicles.

recalls.Description: Ricky Powersports is recalling the Tumble Weed youth ATVs, model TK-110ATV-1, and TGB Blade 600 SE adult ATVs due to significant safety concerns. The Tumble Weed models, available in various colors including burgundy, purple camo, and army green camo, are designed for children aged 6 and older. The model number can be found on an identification plate located between the front tires. The TGB Blade 600 SE, intended for adults aged 16 and older, is sold in black, with the model number located next to the front right tire. Both models display a notice indicating compliance with Ricky Powersports LLC's action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission.
recalls.Title: Ricky Powersports Recalls Youth and Adult ATVs Due to Serious Injury Risks and Safety Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Ricky Powersports at 844-250-2199 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email at RickyPowerSports@gmail.com, or visit www.Rickypowersports.net/Recall or www.RickyPowerSports.net and click on the 'RECALL' button for further details.

recalls.Description: PowerBlock is recalling the Commercial Pro 100 weight set, which includes a Pro Rack Stand, two adjustable weight plate sets, and 5 lb and 10 lb grip handles. The 5 lb grip handles, identifiable by their black design and silver weight increment decals, are the focus of this recall. Only the 5 lb grip handles that come with the Commercial Pro 100 set are affected by this issue. The hazard arises when weight plates exceeding 55 lbs can detach from these handles, posing a risk of impact injuries to users.
recalls.Title: PowerBlock Recalls Commercial Pro 100 Weight Sets Due to Injury Risk from 5 lb Grip Handles
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to PowerBlock at 800-997-3999 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.powerblock.com/pages/product-recalls for more details.

The CFMOTO logo can be found on the rear fender sides, while the CFORCE branding is located on the upper side panels. The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) is located on the frame behind the right rear wheel for identification purposes.

recalls.Description: Reyhee Group has recalled its Droyd multi-purpose helmets designed for youth due to significant safety concerns. These helmets, available in medium (M) and large (L) sizes, feature a black exterior with matching padding, straps, and buckle. The brand name "DROYD" is displayed on the rear, and model numbers can be found on the internal serial number label, such as "DHM000001" or "DHL000001".

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Reyhee Group at 800-333-3729 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, email support@droyd.com, or visit www.droyd.com/pages/safety-recall for further details.

recalls.Description: Polaris has issued a recall for certain 2023-2024 Sportsman 850, XP 1000, and XP 1000 S all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) due to a fire hazard. These models were available in various colors including black, blue, camouflage, crimson, gray, green, red, silver, and tan. Additionally, the recall includes specific fuel pump kits and fuel tank assemblies sold as service parts.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Select Sportsman ATVs and Fuel Pump Kits Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747, available from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information can be found at www.polaris.com under "Product Safety News" in the Safety section, or visit https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/ to verify if your VIN is affected. Consumers can also contact Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Polaris Sportsman 850 ATV
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 10,300 ATVs and 3,158 fuel pump kits and fuel tank assemblies (In addition, about 1,500 ATVs were sold in Canada)

Recalled Polaris Sportsman ATVs VIN Location (1)

Hazards.Name: Fuel leak at the pump flange outlet posing a fire hazard

recalls.Description: GOETAS has issued a recall for its pool drain covers due to a serious entrapment hazard. These white plastic drain covers, designed for swimming pools, stand approximately 7 inches tall and feature top grates that are around 9 inches in diameter. They do not meet the safety standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA), which is critical for protecting swimmers and bathers from potential entrapment.
recalls.Title: GOETAS Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Entrapment Risk; Noncompliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to GOETAS via email at Atlaspool@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – with top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – without top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – side view
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – front view

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Manufacturers.Name: GuangZhouShiYiYangKangTiSheBeiYouXianGongSi, dba GOETAS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from March 2022 through July 2024 for approximately $61.

Importers.Name: GuangZhouShiYiYangKangTiSheBeiYouXianGongSi, dba GOETAS, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to noncompliance with VGBA regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove and dispose of them, and take a photo to send to GOETAS at Atlaspool@163.com for a full refund. It's essential to ensure that all pools have VGBA-compliant drain covers and educate children about staying away from drains. Amazon will contact all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: GOETAS pool drain covers pose entrapment risk; learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: Petzl America has issued a recall for three models of climbing harnesses: the GYM harness (Model No. C032AA00), the PANDION harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01), and the PANJI harness (Model No. C028AA00). These harnesses are commonly utilized by climbing gym operators, adventure parks, guide services, and individual consumers. Serial numbers can be found on a label inside each harness. The affected serial numbers for each model are specified for identification purposes.
recalls.Title: Petzl America Recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI Climbing Harnesses Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Petzl America, Inc. at 877-807-3805 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, or email Returns@petzl.com. More information is available at https://www.petzl.com/US/en/Operators/safety-alerts or www.petzl.com/US/en/Operators.

Products.Name: Petzl GYM Harness
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 22,000 (In addition, about 5,800 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Petzl GYM Climbing Harness (Model No. C032AA00)
Recalled Petzl PANDION Climbing Harness (Model Nos. C029AA00 and C029AA01)
Recalled Petzl PANJI Climbing Harness (Model No. C028AA00)
Format of serial numbers located on a label inside of the recalled harnesses

Injuries.Name: There has been one report of a user in the United States suffering serious injuries from a fall.

Retailers.Name: Available online and in physical stores at KarstSports, PeakTrading, Backcountry, OmniProGear, Moosejaw, and various other outdoor equipment retailers from January 2019 through May 2024, priced between $59.95 and $69.95.

Importers.Name: Petzl America, Inc., of Salt Lake City, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of falling due to improper harness usage, potentially resulting in serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled harnesses immediately and contact Petzl America for a free repair.

recalls.metaDescription: Petzl recalls GYM, PANDION, and PANJI climbing harnesses due to fall hazards. Contact Petzl for a free repair.

recalls.Description: Delta Cycle is recalling its Ceiling Hoists with Straps, model number RS2300, which is indicated on the product packaging. These hoists are designed to lift heavy items such as bicycles, kayaks, and ladders using a pulley system. It is important to note that if the hoists are used without the straps, no recall action is necessary. The affected units will have serial numbers within specific ranges or may not have a serial number at all, located on the underside of the pulley.
recalls.Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Delta Cycle Corp at 800-474-6615 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email hoiststraprecall@deltacycle.com, or visit https://designbydelta.com/hoiststraprecall for more details.

Products.Name: Ceiling Hoists with Straps
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 25,140 (In addition, about 3,390 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Hoist with Straps holding kayak
Location of strap buckle (view from above)
Location of serial number and strap buckle (view from below)
Location of serial number (if present) on underside of flat part of the ~6" long pulleys
Recalled product packaging showing Model RS2300

Injuries.Name: The firm has received four reports of incidents, including one minor injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at REI, various independent stores, and online at Amazon.com and designbydelta.com from February 2017 through July 2024 for approximately $40.

Importers.Name: Delta Cycle Corp., of Randolph, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Plastic buckles on the straps may break, creating an injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the Ceiling Hoist with Straps and contact Delta Cycle for free replacement straps. Old straps should be cut in half and discarded. If the hoist is used solely for bicycles without the straps, no action is needed.

recalls.metaDescription: Delta Cycle recalls Ceiling Hoists with Straps due to injury risks. Contact for free replacement straps and safety information.

recalls.Description: Head Rush Technologies has issued a recall for its TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices, available in 12.5m and 16m lengths. These devices are designed to ensure climbers can safely descend after speed climbing activities. The devices feature a gray body with blue handles; the 12.5m version includes a black webbing handgrip above the carabiner, while the 16m version has a blue webbing handgrip. Each device bears the model number TRUBLUE Speed TBSD150-20, with a serial number range from TBSD00098 to TBSD01180 located on a label on the handle.
recalls.Title: Head Rush Technologies Recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Head Rush Technologies at 720-565-6885 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, contact them via email at inf@headrushtech.com or visit their website at https://info.headrushtech.com/hubfs/Product_Recalls/2024_TRUBLUESpeed_Limited_Recall.pdf or www.headrushtech.com and click on the 'Recall' banner for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (12.5 Meter Version)
Recalled Head Rush Technologies TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Device (16 Meter Version)
Location of Model Number and Serial Number

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one incident report involving brake failure, causing the user to fall, resulting in a broken ankle.

Manufacturers.Name: Head Rush Technologies, of Louisville, Colorado

Retailers.Name: Available for purchase online at www.headrushtech.com from May 2017 to July 2021, priced between $2,800 and $2,900.

Hazards.Name: Potential failure of the backup brake, leading to fall hazards and serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled devices immediately and contact Head Rush Technologies for a complimentary inspection and repair. The company is proactively reaching out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Head Rush Technologies recalls TRUBLUE Speed Auto Belay Devices due to a fall hazard. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the Brompton T-Line foldable bicycle, which measures 25.4 inches in height, 23.6 inches in width, and 12.6 inches in depth when folded. This model features the name 'Brompton' prominently displayed on the center tube of the frame, along with a label on the back of the vertical seat tube that includes the frame number and serial number. A unique 'T' logo helps identify this specific bicycle model, which is available in a Blasted Titanium color.
recalls.Title: Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycles Recalled Due to Potential Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Brompton Bicycle Inc. at 833-507-7975 from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at support@brompton.co.uk, or visit us.brompton.com/legal/t-line-recall for more details. Additional information is also available at https://us.brompton.com under the 'Recall' section.

Products.Name: Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,080 (In addition, about 31 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bicycle
Recalled Brompton T-Line Foldable Bike in Folded Position
T-Line Logo on Label of Recalled T-Line Foldable Bike

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 13 reports of incidents of steering misalignment or loss of steering control worldwide. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bike dealers, Brompton Junction Stores nationwide, and online at us.brompton.com from February 2024 through June 2024, priced between $5,225 and $5,500.

Importers.Name: Brompton Bicycle Inc., of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Risk of losing control of steering due to misalignment between the handlebar and front wheel, posing a fall hazard

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled bicycles and contact an authorized Brompton dealer for a complimentary repair to secure the steerer clamp bolts on the handlebar stem. Brompton will reach out directly to all registered owners and dealers.

recalls.metaDescription: Brompton recalls T-Line foldable bicycles due to a fall hazard. Contact Brompton for repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Peace Industry Group is recalling its "Peace Sports" 125CC Utility and Sport Youth ATVs, specifically models 512 CY125ATV-1 and 518 CY125ATV-6. These ATVs feature the "Peace Sports" branding on the handlebar pad and side of the vehicle, with model numbers found on the VIN plate located on the lower front frame. A notice indicating that the ATV is part of a safety action plan approved by the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission is also present on the vehicle.
recalls.Title: Peace Industry Group Recalls Youth ATVs Due to Safety Risks and Noncompliance with Federal Regulations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using these ATVs immediately and can reach Peace Industry Group at 888-855-6031 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More information is available at www.peacesports.com/ATV-Recall or by visiting www.peacesports.com and selecting "RECALL" at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Peace Sports 512 CY125ATV-1 Utility Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports 518 CY125ATV-6 Sport Youth ATV
Recalled Peace Sports Youth ATV VIN plate

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Zhejiang Changying Car Industry Co. LTD, of China

Retailers.Name: AutoWorx Sales LLC, Daryl's Motorcycle Service, Hialeah Scooters, LJS Repairs LLC, M&M Small Engines, Marobo Motors, Motor Werks LLC, One Stop Automotive Repair LLC, Scooters & Small Engine Repairs, Solano Cycles, Unique Motorsports, Wild Hogs Scooters and Motorsports LLC, and other authorized dealers across the country.

Importers.Name: Peace Industry Group, of Norcross, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard from handlebars, collision risk due to faulty parking brakes, and missing safety reflectors. The 518 CY125ATV-6 model has footguards that can cause laceration, contusion, or amputation injuries.

Remedies.Name: Full refund for returned ATVs

recalls.metaDescription: Peace Industry Group recalls youth ATVs due to serious safety risks. Stop using immediately and contact for a full refund.

recalls.Description: DGL Group has announced a recall for Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooters that feature specific serial numbers. Only those scooters with 'WY' as the seventh and eighth characters in the serial number are affected. Consumers can find the serial number on a label located on the underside of the scooter. The recalled models are matte black, equipped with a kickstand and reflective lights at both the front and rear of the stand board, with the Hover-1 logo displayed prominently on the stand board and stem.
recalls.Title: DGL Group Issues Recall for Hover-1 Dynamo E-Scooters Due to Serious Injury and Crash Risks; Available Exclusively at Walmart.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DGL Group toll-free at 844-451-7300 from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information is available online at www.hover-1.com/pages/dynamo_recall or by visiting www.hover-1.com and clicking on 'Important Recall Information' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooter
Serial number with the letters "WY" as the seventh and eighth letter appears on a label on the underside of the recalled e-scooter

Injuries.Name: DGL Group has received 22 reports of the e-scooter's brakes failing, which resulted in seven injuries, including abrasions, contusions, a dislocated shoulder, fractured teeth, lacerations and strains.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Walmart.com from November 2021 through July 2022 for approximately $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Brake failure leading to serious injury and crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled e-scooters immediately and contact DGL Group for full refund instructions. To process the refund, consumers need to provide photos of the e-scooter's serial number and the severed motor cable.

recalls.metaDescription: DGL Group recalls Hover-1 Dynamo e-scooters due to serious injury risks. Find out how to get a refund and check if your scooter is affected.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has issued a recall for the 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles, available in multiple colors. These snowmobiles feature a distinctive 'M' shape resembling a mountain on their side panels, and the 10th digit of the VIN is 'R', which can be found stamped on the tunnel section or on the decal located at the top or rear of the tunnel.
recalls.Title: Recall of 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday. You can also reach them via email at arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls or https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/ and select Recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Artic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile (black)
Recalled Artic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobile (white)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of cross pins that have become loose or have fallen out. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available through Arctic Cat dealers across the country from September 2023 to June 2024, priced between $14,500 and $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Improperly swagged u-joint pin can lead to loss of steering control, increasing crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the snowmobile and contact an authorized Arctic Cat dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair if necessary. The dealer will check the steering post joint pin; if it is correctly swagged, no repair is needed. If not, the entire steering post assembly will be replaced.

recalls.metaDescription: Textron recalls 2024 Arctic Cat Mountain 600 Snowmobiles due to a steering control hazard. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: Razor has issued a recall for all Icon electric scooters due to a potential fall hazard. The affected models can be identified by their model numbers, which are located in the lower right corner of the label on the bottom of the deck. The serial numbers can be found below the barcode on the same label and on the back of the charger. The scooters were available in various colors including black, blue, orange, pink, and red, with 'Razor' prominently displayed on the handlebars and floorboard.
recalls.Title: Razor Recalls Icon Electric Scooters Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled scooters immediately and contact Razor at 866-467-2967 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Additional details can be found online at www.razor.com/iconrecall or by visiting www.razor.com and clicking on 'Recall Information' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Razor Icon electric scooter
Serial Number sticker located on front left side of the deck with serial number beginning with "31"

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 34 reports of partial or complete downtube separation with two reported injuries, including bruising.

Manufacturers.Name: Razor USA LLC, of Cerritos, Calif.

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Walmart, Best Buy, and other retailers nationwide, as well as online at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, Razor.com, and more from September 2022 through March 2024 for approximately $600.

Importers.Name: Razor USA LLC, of Cerritos, Calif.

Hazards.Name: Fall hazard due to downtube separation from the floorboard

Remedies.Name: Refund or coupon for future purchase

recalls.metaDescription: Razor recalls Icon electric scooters due to fall hazards. Stop using immediately and contact for refunds or coupons.

recalls.Description: Torquay eTrading LLC is recalling its Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip & Rip re-gripping kits due to safety concerns. These products, which include 14 strips of golf tape, are packaged in an 8-ounce white bottle featuring a blue label. The labeling claims the solvent is 'Safe & Non-Toxic,' but it fails to include necessary cautionary warnings about petroleum distillates, which could lead to poisoning risks for consumers. The products were previously available in various pack sizes, including 2-pack, 4-pack, and 6-pack options.
recalls.URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
recalls.Title: Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Kits Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also email recall@essentialvalues.us or visit https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall for further details.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Golf Grip Solvent, 8-Ounce
Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 through May 2024, priced around $9, with kits including golf grip tape costing approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading labeling as 'Safe and Non-Toxic' without necessary warnings.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled products immediately and reach out to Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label. The company, along with Amazon, eBay, and Walmart, will contact known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Torquay eTrading recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Kits due to poisoning risks. Contact for a free corrected label.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki USA has announced a recall for the 2024 Mule PRO-FX™ 1000, PRO-FXR™ 1000, and PRO-FXT™ 1000 off-highway utility vehicles due to a potential fire hazard. These vehicles, available in various colors including Metallic Sierra Blue and Firecracker Red, may experience improper combustion when starting the engine, presenting a fire risk. The Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) can be found on the forward frame rail beneath the seat, and model details are located in the owner's manual.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki USA Issues Recall for Mule PRO 1000 Utility Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Kawasaki toll-free at 866-802-9381 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT Monday through Friday, or visit www.kawasaki.com/en-us/owner-center/recalls for more information.

Recalled MULE PRO-FXT 1000 LE in Metallic Onyx Black

Recalled MULE PRO-FXT 1000 Platinum Ranch Edition in Metallic Stardust White

Injuries.Name: Kawasaki has received three reports of fires occurring during engine start up. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Manufacturing Corp., U.S.A., of Lincoln, Neb.

Retailers.Name: Available at Kawasaki dealers across the country from June 2023 until April 2024, priced between $15,100 and $21,900.

Distributors.Name: Kawasaki Motors Corp. U.S.A., of Foothill Ranch, Calif.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to improper combustion when starting the engine.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled vehicles and contact a Kawasaki dealer for a free repair, which includes reprogramming the ECU and installing a sub-harness.

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls Mule PRO 1000 utility vehicles due to fire hazards. Stop using immediately and contact a dealer for repairs.

recalls.Description: iFIT has announced a recall of the ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set, model PAMSDB20. This set features black dumbbells that come with handles, weight plates, and a molded plastic tray. The weights can be adjusted from 5 to 50 pounds in increments of 5 pounds using a manual selection pin, with the weights stored in the base. The model number can be found on a sticker located under the lip of the molded tray.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls ProForm 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Sets Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT Monday through Friday, or email pamsdb20-recall@ifit.com. More details can be found at www.proform.com/product-recalls or by visiting www.proform.com and clicking on 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 54,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Recalled iFIT ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set
Recalled iFIT ProForm Rapid Strike 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Set

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of weight plates dislodging during use with three resulting in injuries, including contusions and abrasions.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Risk of weight plates dislodging from the handle during use, leading to potential impact injuries.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls ProForm 50 LB Adjustable Dumbbell Sets due to injury risks. Stop using them and contact iFIT for a replacement.

recalls.Description:
CFMOTO is recalling specific models of the ZFORCE 950 HO Sport Side-by-Side Recreational Off-Highway Vehicles (ROVs) from the years 2022 to 2023. These vehicles are available in gray, orange, and tan colors, with the CFMOTO logo on the front and ZFORCE branding on the chassis side. The vehicle identification number (VIN) can be found stamped on the frame near the right rear wheel.

The recall is due to a safety concern where the shock absorber rod assembly may become unthreaded and detach, leading to suspension failure. This poses significant crash and tip-over hazards for riders. Consumers are urged to take immediate action regarding this recall.
recalls.Title: CFMOTO Recalls ZFORCE 950 HO Sport ROVs Due to Potential Crash and Tip-Over Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach CFMOTO at 888-823-6686, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT. Additional information can be obtained via email at info@cfmotousa.com or by visiting https://www.cfmotousa.com/vehicle-recall. Alternatively, visit www.cfmotousa.com and navigate to the "Vehicle Recall" section under "Customer Care".

Products.Name: CFMOTO ZFORCE 950 HO Sport Side-by-Side ROVs (2022-2023)

Images.Caption: Recalled CFMOTO ZFORCE 950 HO Sport Side-by-Side ROV (gray)
CFMOTO ZFORCE 950 HO Sport Side-by-Side ROV (orange)
VIN Location

Injuries.Name: CFMOTO Powersports has received approximately 86 reports of incidents in which the shock absorber failed and the suspension collapsed, including one tip-over which resulted in impact injury to the ribs.

Retailers.Name: Available through CFMOTO dealers nationwide from March 2022 to June 2024, priced around $14,000.

Importers.Name: CFMOTO Powersports Inc., of Plymouth, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash and tip-over due to potential suspension collapse

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled ROVs and contact an authorized CFMOTO dealer for a free repair

recalls.metaDescription: CFMOTO recalls ZFORCE 950 HO Sport ROVs due to crash risks. Immediate action required for safety. Contact CFMOTO for repairs.

recalls.Description: TonGass is recalling its 8-inch round pool drain covers due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act, which poses a serious entrapment risk for swimmers. These plastic drain covers, identified by model WGX1048E and ASIN B0CBK7HQQ, feature two screws and have a diameter of 7-7/8 inches. Pool owners should take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: TonGass Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Safety Violations and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact TonGass via email at tongass@126.com, visit their Amazon Seller Profile, or call Amazon's toll-free number at 888-280-4331.

Images.Caption: Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (top view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (side view)

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from September 2023 to June 2024 for $23.

Importers.Name: Oneida International Ltd., dba TonGass, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, remove the drain cover, take a photo, and email it to tongass@126.com. Ensure compliance with VGBA standards and educate children about drain safety. TonGass will reach out to all purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: TonGass recalls pool drain covers for safety violations. Immediate action required to prevent entrapment hazards. Contact for refunds.

recalls.Description: American Honda Motor is recalling certain off-road motorcycles from the 2023-2024 model years, specifically the CRF50F, CRF110F, and CRF125F. The CRF50F and CRF110F are available in red or white, while the CRF125F is sold in red. Each motorcycle features the "HONDA" logo on the seat and the model number on the frame above the engine. The vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on the left side of the steering head, and consumers can verify their VIN against the recall list at Honda Powersports & Motorcycle Recalls.
recalls.Title: Honda Recalls 2023-2024 Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Safety Risks

Additional information is available online at https://powersports.honda.com/recalls or https://powersports.honda.com/contact-us, where you can find the "Recalls" link at the bottom of the page.

recalls.Description: REI is recalling its Co-op Cycles REV 12-, 16-, and 20-inch children's bicycles, manufactured between 2022 and 2024. The affected bicycles have serial numbers starting with 'ET22,' 'ET23,' '01VX,' or '07VX,' which can be found printed on the bottom bracket or down tube of the bike frame. These bicycles are available in various colors and are equipped with training wheels.
recalls.Title: REI Recalls Co-op Cycles REV Children's Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact REI at 800-426-4840 between 6 a.m. and 8 p.m. PT on weekdays, or from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT on weekends. Additional support can be accessed via email or chat at rei.com/help, or by visiting https://www.rei.com/help?a=REI-Co-op-Cycles-REV-Bikes---id--He33EfUtSkKpMKyK7ZX27A for more details on the recall.

Manufacturers.Name: RECREATIONAL EQUIPMENT INC. (REI), of Sumner, Washington

Retailers.Name: Available at REI locations nationwide and online at REI.com from March 2022 to May 2024, priced between $200 and $300.

recalls.metaDescription: REI recalls Co-op Cycles REV children's bicycles due to fall hazards. Learn how to get a free repair or kit.

recalls.Description: Babboe B.V. has issued a recall for all models of their two-wheeled and three-wheeled cargo bicycles, both electric and traditional. These bicycles are designed for transporting children, pets, and light goods in urban settings. The recall is due to a significant safety concern regarding the bike frames, which may crack, bend, or break, creating a fall hazard for riders.
recalls.Title: Babboe Cargo Bicycles Recalled Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Babboe at their toll-free number 833-220-7867, available Monday to Friday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at service.us@babboe.com. More details can also be found on their website at www.internationalcargobike.com.

recalls.Description: Black Diamond Equipment is recalling Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits, Style No. 400073, with date codes 01-2021, 05-2022, 07-2023, 01-2024, and SS1271. The date codes can be found on the rubber heel strap pull-tab and the underside of the toe piece beneath the anti-balling plate. These products feature aluminum plates with spikes designed for ice climbing and are identifiable by their bright green color, along with black plastic components and webbing straps that secure around the ankle. This recall also encompasses the Neve Strap Binding Accessory Kit, Style No. 400683, and Neve Strap Heel Cup Screw & Nuts, Style No. 400687, which were sold separately as accessories.
recalls.Title: Black Diamond Equipment Recalls Neve Strap Crampons and Accessory Kits Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Black Diamond Equipment at 866-306-0865 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email NeveStrapRecall@bdel.com, or visit https://www.blackdiamondequipment.com/en_US/content/neve-strap-recall/ or www.blackdiamondequipment.com and select 'Recalls' for further details.

recalls.Description: Retrospec is recalling Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes equipped with disc brakes due to a safety concern. These bicycles are available in three colors: bluebell (blue), eggshell (white), and mint (green). Each bike features the 'Retrospec' brand name on the frame and the company's 'R' logo on the front fork. A silver label located near the pedals indicates 'Retrospec, Made in Tianjin, China' along with the batch date of 12-15-2023.
recalls.Title: Retrospec Recalls Beaumont Plus ST City Bikes Due to Potential Crash and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Retrospec at 888-488-2369 between 9 a.m. and 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, email recall@retrospec.com, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-beaumont-plus-w-disc-brakes-recall. recalls.Description: Salewa USA has announced a recall of the orange Wild Country 'Ropeman 1' ascenders, which are utilized in mountaineering and rock climbing activities. These products, manufactured in July 2022, are identifiable by the batch number 05A0722 printed in white on the side. The recall is due to a potential failure of the ascender's teeth to engage properly, posing a safety risk during use.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Wild Country at 844-412-7013 from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or via email at Ropeman1Recall@wildcountry.com. Additional information is available at https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/product-recalls or by visiting https://www.wildcountry.com/en-us/ and selecting 'Ropeman 1 Voluntary Recall' at the top of the page.

recalls.Description: TOPINCN has issued a recall for its 1.5-inch and 3-inch round pool drain covers designed for swimming pools. The 1.5-inch drain cover features an outer diameter of approximately 4 inches and an inner diameter of about 2 inches, while the 3-inch version measures around 5 inches in outer diameter and 3 inches in inner diameter. Both covers are white and constructed from plastic.
recalls.Title: Recall of TOPINCN Pool Drain Covers Due to Entrapment Risk; Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, contact Sanure via email at sanure710@126.com, or visit their Amazon Storefront page at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&isAmazonFulfilled=1&marketplaceSeller=1&orderID=114-2704361-2776233&seller=A3FYRQNET3AI4U.

Images.Caption: Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (top view)
Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (side view)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.amazon.com from September 2022 through March 2024, priced between $9 and $32.

Importers.Name: Dong Guan Shi Li Peng Zhi Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, dba Sanure Storefront, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA) standards.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove them from pools, and email a photo to sanure710@126.com for a full refund. It's crucial to ensure all pools have VGBA-compliant drain covers and educate children about staying away from drains. Sanure will reach out to all purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of TOPINCN pool drain covers due to entrapment risk. Ensure safety compliance and get a refund.

recalls.Description: Korimefa is recalling its multi-purpose bike helmets designed for teens and adults in size Medium (M), which accommodate head circumferences of approximately 21-1/2 to 22-1/2 inches. These helmets are blue with black padding, straps, and a black and red buckle. Each helmet features a black plastic adjustment knob at the back, and the model number 'W-001', serial number 'LDW202103', and manufacture date '03/2021' can be found on a white label inside. The brand name 'KORIMEFA' is displayed prominently on the front of the helmet.
recalls.Title: Korimefa Bike Helmets Recalled for Head Injury Risk; Federal Safety Standards Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach Yangxi via email at marketing.us@hotmail.com, visit the Yangxi Storefront on Amazon.com, or check the Amazon.com Seller Profile: Yangxi.

Recalled Korimefa Multi-Purpose Helmet

Importers.Name: Shanghai Yangxi Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, DBA Yangxi, of China

Hazards.Name: Non-compliance with CPSC safety standards poses a risk of head injury in crashes.

To process the refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and email it to marketing.us@hotmail.com. After providing proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet. Yangxi will reach out to all buyers directly.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has announced a recall for certain 2022 and 2023 models of the Prowler Pro, Prowler Pro Crew, Tracker 800SX, and Tracker 800SX Crew utility vehicles. These vehicles are available in several colors, including green, gray, red, fossil, and timber, with "Prowler" or "Tracker" marked on the cargo box. The vehicle identification number (VIN) can be found on the operator side of the frame beneath the cargo box, where the 10th digit indicates the model year: 'N' for 2022 and 'P' for 2023.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281, Monday through Friday, from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT. Alternatively, they can email arcticcatwarranty@textron.com or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls, https://www.trackeroffroad.com/recalls.html, or https://arcticcat.txtsv.com and click on 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

This issue may lead to instability in the handlebars, increasing the risk of a crash.

recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email arcticcatwarranty@textron.com. recalls.Description: Crosman has issued a recall for its Icon .177 and .22 Caliber Air Rifles, which are PCP-powered bolt-action models identified by the numbers CPI77S and CPI22S. recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should cease using the recalled air rifles and reach out to Crosman at 800-724-7486 between 9 a.m. and 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at www.crosman.com/iconrecall or by clicking on the 'Recall' link on the Crosman homepage. You can also email iconrecall@crosman.com for assistance.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 65 reports of incidents of debris-clogged pedals sticking, of which 15 of the vehicles crashed into permanent structures with one minor injury, a chafed hand.

Importers.Name: JAKKS Pacific Inc., of Santa Monica, California

Hazards.Name: Potential crash hazard due to sticking acceleration pedal

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including replacement pedal and installation instructions

recalls.Description: Full Speed Ahead is recalling its Gossamer Pro AGX+ bicycle cranksets, model CK-6037, due to potential safety hazards. These cranksets were sold both individually and as part of bicycles from brands such as Cannondale, Canyon, Marin, Haro, Fuji, and Pinarello. Look for "FSA Gossamer Pro" etched on the front of the crankarm. The model and serial numbers are located on the back of the crankarm, adjacent to the pedal threads, with only those starting with B1, B2, or B3 being affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Full Speed Ahead Recalls Gossamer Pro AGX+ Cranksets Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach Full Speed Ahead at 877-743-3372 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at Recall-USA@fullspeedahead.com. More information is available online at www.fullspeedahead.com/en/support/recall or by visiting www.fullspeedahead.com and selecting the "Gossamer Pro AGX+ crankset recall" link.

Products.Name: Gossamer Pro AGX+ Cranksets
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 12,300 (In addition, about 250 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset
Recalled FSA Gossamer Pro AGX+ Bicycle Crankset

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 277 reports of bicycle crankarms loosening or detaching. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at independent bicycle retailers across the country and online through brands including Cannondale, Canyon, Marin, Haro, Fuji, and Pinarello from January 2022 to June 2023, priced around $190 for the crankset and between $1,700 and $4,000 for the bicycles.

Importers.Name: Full Speed Ahead Inc., of Mukilteo, Washington

Hazards.Name: Risk of crank arms loosening or detaching, leading to falls and injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the affected bicycles and contact Full Speed Ahead for free dealer installation of a replacement crankset.

recalls.metaDescription: Full Speed Ahead recalls Gossamer Pro AGX+ cranksets due to fall hazards. Contact for free replacement installation.

recalls.Description: Gasaciods children's multi-purpose helmets are being recalled due to safety concerns. These helmets are designed for head circumferences of approximately 19-1/2 to 20-1/2 inches and are available in pink and blue colors. They feature red removable padding, black straps, and a black and red buckle, along with a red plastic knob at the back that includes an LED warning light for fit adjustment. The brand name "GASACIODS" is displayed on the sides of the helmets.
recalls.Title: Gasaciods Children's Helmets Recalled for Head Injury Risk; Noncompliance with Federal Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled helmets immediately and reach out to Fengwang Sports for a refund. Contact them via email at george66305@163.com or send a message through the Temu seller profile.

Images.Caption: Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue - front view)
Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Blue - side view)
Recalled Gasaciods Children's Multi-Purpose Helmet (Pink- rear view)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available online at Temu.com from July 2023 to January 2024, priced between $23 and $42.

Importers.Name: Dongguan Feng Wang Sports Goods Co., Ltd. DBA Fengwang Sports, of China

Hazards.Name: Noncompliance with safety standards for bicycle helmets, increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the helmets and request a refund from Fengwang Sports. To obtain a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, take a photo of the destroyed helmet, and send it to Fengwang Sports via email or onsite message on Temu.com. After providing proof of destruction, dispose of the helmet.

recalls.metaDescription: Gasaciods children's helmets recalled due to head injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact Fengwang Sports for a refund.

recalls.Description: Johnson Health Tech North America has expanded its recall of Matrix Fitness commercial treadmills, specifically the T1xe, T3x, and T3xm models. These treadmills feature a running platform measuring 60 inches in length and 20 inches in width, along with a 33-inch-wide console. Notably, they have a straight power cord without a power cord bracket, and the Matrix brand is displayed on the console masts. Model names and serial numbers can be found on the connecting bar near the power socket, with serial numbers formatted as TM, a three-digit model number, the manufacture date in YY/MM format, followed by a five-digit serial number. The affected treadmills were produced between March 2018 and January 2024.
recalls.Title: Expanded Recall of Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm Commercial Treadmills by Johnson Health Tech North America Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Johnson Health Tech North America at 866-218-3674 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email reworks@johnsonfit.com, or visit https://matrixfitness.com/us/eng/recalls for more details.

Products.Name: Matrix T1xe Commercial Treadmill
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,700 (About 19,900 were previously recalled in January 2022)

Images.Caption: Recalled Matrix T3xe Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T3xm Treadmill
Recalled Matrix T1xe MAX Treadmill
Location on recalled treadmill of serial number
Location of model name and the serial number on the connecting bar at the bottom of the treadmill

Injuries.Name: Matrix Fitness has received one report of a fire in a fitness facility since the last recall, in addition to the 58 reported in the January 2022 recall, of the treadmill's power cord and socket sparking, smoking, and/or melting, including seven reports of fire; two resulting in property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Johnson Health Tech North America and Johnson Fitness & Wellness stores across the country from March 2018 to January 2024, priced between $3,400 and $8,900.

Importers.Name: Johnson Health Tech North America Inc., of Cottage Grove, Wis.

Hazards.Name: Loose power cord from treadmill's power socket creating a fire hazard.

Remedies.Name: Inspect treadmills for power cord bracket installation. If absent, ensure the power cord is securely inserted before each use until repaired. Contact Johnson Health Tech North America for a free power cord bracket installation or to order one for self-installation. The company will reach out to all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: Johnson Health Tech recalls Matrix T1xe, T3x, and T3xm treadmills due to fire hazards. Contact for inspection and remedy details.

recalls.Description: Jugader has issued a recall for its Zipline Kits, which include a 160-foot long and 4-mm thick stainless-steel cable, a blue trolley, and a hanging plastic seat. The kits may present a serious fall hazard if the cable breaks. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure their safety.
recalls.Title: Jugader Zipline Kits Recalled Due to Fall Risk; Available Exclusively on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Jugader at 800-360-8078, email support@Jugader.com, or visit www.jugader.com/recall. Alternatively, go to www.jugader.com and click on 'Recalls' at the top of the page for further details.

Importers.Name: Jugader, of China

Hazards.Name: Potential fall hazard due to cable breakage

Remedies.Name: Free repair kit including a new cable and updated installation instructions

recalls.metaDescription: Jugader recalls Zipline Kits due to fall hazards. Immediate action recommended for safety. Contact Jugader for a free repair kit.

recalls.Description:
Intimidator and Mahindra are recalling several models of utility vehicles (UTVs) due to a potential crash hazard. The affected models include Intimidator GC1K Classic, GC1K Crew, GC1K Truck, and various Mahindra Retriever models from 2013 to 2023. These vehicles are identifiable by the brand names displayed on the front grill and model series printed on the rear-side panels. Serial numbers for the recalled Intimidator UTVs range from 000000 to 014034, while Mahindra UTVs range from 000000 to 010545. 
Consumers should take immediate action if they own one of the affected models. The recall includes UTVs sold in different colors and models, such as the Intimidator 750 Classic and Mahindra Retriever 1000 Diesel Classic, among others. It is crucial for owners to verify their vehicle's serial number against the recall list to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Intimidator and Mahindra Utility Vehicles Recalled Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Intimidator at 888-215-8121 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or email recall@intimidatorutv.com. Visit http://www.intimidatorutv.com and click on 'RECALL INFORMATION' for details. For Mahindra-branded UTVs, visit www.mahindrausa.com/--recall-notices or click on 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the homepage. Intimidator will reach out to all registered owners by mail.

recalls.Description: Delta Cycle is recalling specific models of bicycle stem raisers due to a potential fall hazard. The affected products include Delta Cycle and Dimension Stem Raisers with model numbers TD3318B, TD3318S, TD3418B, TD3418S, SM1977, and SM1979. These stem raisers, available in black and silver, range in height from five to seven inches. Consumers should be aware that the stem raisers may shift during use, which could unexpectedly alter the bicycle's handlebar position and lead to falls.
recalls.Title: Delta Cycle Recalls Stem Raisers Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Delta Cycle at 800-474-6615 between 9 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday to Friday. You can also reach them via email at stemrecall@designbydelta.com or visit www.designbydelta.com/stemrecall for further details.

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 500,000 (In addition, about 8,510 were sold in Canada)

recalls.Description: Fischer Sports is recalling its RC4 50, RC4 60, and RC4 65 Junior Ski Boots, available in sizes 21.5 to 27.5. The RC4 50 and RC4 60 models were offered in Rhino Grey and Snow colors, while the RC4 65 was available in yellow. Each boot features the 'Fischer' logo on the lower shell, with the model name and size clearly marked on the upper cuff and sole, respectively.
recalls.Title: Fischer Sports Recalls RC4 Junior Ski Boots Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Fischer Sports US at 800-844-7810, Monday through Friday, from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET. You can also email juniorboot.us@fischersports.com or visit https://www.fischersports.com/voluntary-product-update-junior-alpine-ski-boot-recall for further details.
