recalls.Description: Excalibur Crossbow is recalling its C2 Crank cocking aid, which is designed for use with crossbows. This product features two plastic cocking hooks that can potentially break during operation, leading to dangerous flying projectiles that may cause impact injuries and lacerations to users and nearby individuals. The C2 Crank is identifiable by the 'Excalibur Crossbow' logo on its front and part number 2199 on the packaging.
recalls.Title: Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank Cocking Aids Recalled Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Excalibur Crossbow at 888-689-1289, available Monday to Thursday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or email warranty@excaliburcrossbow.com. Additional information can be found at www.excaliburcrossbow.com under the 'Recalls' section or directly at https://excaliburcrossbow.com/c2-crank-recall/.

Products.Name: Excalibur C2 Crank Cocking Aid
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,000 (In addition, about 3,920 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank with cocking hooks

Injuries.Name: Excalibur has received 538 reports of the C2 Crank cocking aid hooks breaking during use, resulting in plastic projectiles flying and hitting consumers, including one injury where a consumer reportedly suffered a permanent eye injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Excalibur Crossbow US LLC, of Canada

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at excaliburcrossbow.com, ebay.com, and amazon.com from January 2008 to December 2021 for approximately $190.

Hazards.Name: Risk of impact injuries and lacerations from breaking cocking hooks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement rope and hooks upon contacting Excalibur Crossbow

recalls.metaDescription: Excalibur Crossbow recalls C2 Crank cocking aids due to injury risks from breaking hooks. Contact for free replacements.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling certain 2022-2023 snowmobiles equipped with PATRIOT BOOST engines, including models such as the Pro RMK Slash, RMK Khaos Slash, INDY VR1, and Switchback Assault. These snowmobiles were available in black and various custom color options, with "POLARIS" marked on the front side panel. The vehicle identification number (VIN) and model number can be found on the right side of the frame.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls 2022-2023 Snowmobiles with PATRIOT BOOST Engines Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or visit www.polaris.com and navigate to "Product Safety News" under the "Safety" section. To check if your VIN is affected by any recalls, visit https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/. You can also reach out to Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at https://www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2022-2023 Snowmobiles with PATRIOT BOOST Engines

recalls.Description: Ravin Crossbows is recalling eight models from the R500 series due to a safety concern. These crossbows measure approximately 8 inches wide when un-cocked and about 28 inches long. Each model features a split limb design with cams positioned between the limbs. Available in slate gray and camo, the crossbows come complete with a scope, quiver, cocking handle, quiver bracket, and six arrows. The Ravin logo and model identification can be found on decals on the upper limbs, with the serial number located on the left side of the rail above the trigger.
recalls.Title: Ravin Crossbows Issues Recall for R500 Series Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to Ravin Crossbows at 888-298-6335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, email R500recall@ravincrossbows.com, or visit https://www.ravincrossbows.com/r500recall. More details can also be found on their website under "Safety & Instruction" and then "Recall Notices."

Products.NumberOfUnits: About 13,300 (In addition, about 425 were sold in Canada)

Manufacturers.Name: Ravin Crossbows LLC, of Superior, Wisconsin

Retailers.Name: Ravin Crossbows website

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected discharge when cocking or uncocking if not fully cocked

Remedies.Name: Stop using the crossbows and contact Ravin for a free repair

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has announced a recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles with serial numbers ranging from 8029703 to 8031430. These serial numbers can be found beneath the charger receptacle. The recall is due to a potential fire hazard caused by water intrusion into the lithium-ion battery pack.
recalls.Title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Issues Recall for Tracker Off Road OX EV Light Utility Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease using the Tracker OX EV and reach out to Textron Specialized Vehicles at 888-438-3946 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, they can email msizemore@textron.com or visit https://www.trackeroffroad.com and navigate to the "Recalls" section for further details.

Products.Name: Tracker Off Road OX EV Vehicles

Images.Caption: Recalled Tracker Off Road OX EV

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's stores, and Tracker Marine and off-road dealerships nationwide from January 2023 through December 2023, priced between $12,000 and $13,000.

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to water intrusion in the lithium-ion battery pack

Remedies.Name: Schedule a free inspection and repair at an authorized Tracker Off Road dealer or service center. Do not charge the vehicle and park it outdoors at least 15 feet away from buildings or other vehicles until the service is completed.

recalls.metaDescription: Textron recalls Tracker Off Road OX EV vehicles due to a fire risk. Contact Textron for inspection and repair details.

recalls.Description:
Anker has issued a recall for the lithium-ion battery packs used in their EverFrost Coolers, designed for outdoor activities like camping and fishing. These coolers are dark gray, feature wheels, and have a drop-down handle, with a blue charging port for alternative power sources. The model number is located on the bottom of the cooler, and the name "Anker EverFrost" is displayed in silver and blue on the side. The affected models include various sizes ranging from 30 to 50 liters.

Consumers are advised to stop using these coolers immediately due to the risk of the lithium-ion batteries overheating, which poses a fire hazard. Anker is offering a free replacement battery pack for those affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Anker EverFrost Coolers Recalled Due to Fire Hazard from Lithium-Ion Batteries
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Anker at 800-988-7973 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or email support@anker.com. Additional information can be found at https://support.anker.com/s/emailcontactus, and a LiveChat option is available at https://support.anker.com/s/contact-us. For more details on the recall, visit https://www.anker.com/anker-solix/cooler-battery-recall-pps.

recalls.Description: Textron Specialized Vehicles has announced a recall for all 2024 Arctic Cat Catalyst 600 series snowmobiles. These snowmobiles are identifiable by the model names "ZR," "Riot," or "Mountain" displayed on each side of the engine cowling and were available in various colors. The recall is due to a potential safety hazard involving the brake line.
recalls.Title: Textron Specialized Vehicles Issues Recall for 2024 Arctic Cat Catalyst 600 Snowmobiles Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Textron Specialized Vehicles at 800-279-2281 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email arcticcatwarranty@textron.com, or visit https://arcticcat.txtsv.com/owners/recalls for more details.

Manufacturers.Name: Textron Specialized Vehicles, of Augusta, Georgia

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Arctic Cat dealers across the country from November 2022 to January 2024, priced between $13,800 and $16,100.

Hazards.Name: Risk of brake loss due to brake line contact with the brake disc, leading to potential injury.

recalls.Description: Bombardier Recreational Products (BRP) has issued a recall for all 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade snowmobiles that feature Pilot X skis. These snowmobiles were available in various colors, with the Ski-Doo brand and model name displayed on the side panels. The Pilot X marking can be found engraved on the back of the ski. To identify affected units, the vehicle identification number (VIN) is located on a decal on the right side of the tunnel beneath the seat. Dealers are available to verify VINs involved in this recall.
recalls.Title: BRP Recalls 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade Snowmobiles Due to Serious Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach BRP toll-free at 888-272-9222 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, seven days a week, or visit https://ski-doo.brp.com/us/en/owner-zone/responsible-rider/safety/safety-recalls/left-pilot-x-ski-may-break-loss-of-control.html, www.ski-doo.com/us/en/owner-zone/maintenance/safety-recalls, or https://ski-doo.brp.com/us/en/ and select 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ Snowmobiles with Pilot X Skis
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 8,100 (In addition, about 5,600 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled MY 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ Snowmobile
Recalled MY 2024 Ski-Doo Renegade Snowmobile
Pilot X is engraved at the back of the snowmobile’s ski

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 27 reports in the United States of the snowmobile's left ski breaking. There are no reports of injuries in the United States. The firm is aware of one incident in Canada resulting in an injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Bombardier Recreational Products Inc., of Canada

Retailers.Name: Authorized Ski-Doo dealers across the nation from June 2023 to January 2024, priced between $10,000 and $18,000.

Importers.Name: BRP US Inc., of Sturtevant, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of left ski breaking, leading to serious injury and crash potential.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the snowmobiles immediately and contact BRP for a complimentary inspection and repair, which includes replacing the left ski at no cost. BRP will reach out to all buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: BRP recalls 2024 Ski-Doo MXZ and Renegade snowmobiles due to a serious injury risk from Pilot X skis. Immediate action required.

recalls.Description: Cannondale is recalling its 2021 through 2023 Model Year 26" Dave bicycles and framesets due to potential safety issues. These bicycles, available in deep teal and stealth gray, feature 'Dave' on the top-tube and 'Cannondale' on the downtube. The weld at the headtube/downtube may fail, which could lead to falls and injuries.
recalls.Title: Cannondale Recalls 2021-2023 Dave Bicycles Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Cannondale at 800-245-3872 from 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email ridersupport@cannondale.com. More information can be found at www.cannondale.com/en/safety-and-recalls or by visiting www.cannondale.com and selecting 'Recalls & Notices' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2021-2023 Cannondale Dave Bicycles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 660 (In addition, about 113 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Cannondale 26" Dave bicycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of damage to the bicycle headtube/downtube weld, causing the bicycle headtube to separate from the bicycle frame. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle retailers nationwide and online at Cannondale.com from June 2021 to September 2023, priced around $1,300.

Importers.Name: Cycling Sports Group Inc., dba Cannondale, of Wilton, Connecticut

Hazards.Name: Risk of fall and injury due to potential weld failure

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycles and contact an authorized Cannondale dealer for a free replacement frame.

recalls.metaDescription: Cannondale recalls 2021-2023 Dave bicycles due to fall hazards. Contact Cannondale for a free replacement frame.

recalls.Description: The Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Sets, sold under the Garden Games and Big Game Hunters brands, are being recalled due to safety concerns. These sets include four adult mallets, two winning posts, one red and blue children's mallet, six balls, clips, hoops, and a storage bag featuring either a blue Big Game Hunters logo or a green Garden Games logo. The products were available for purchase exclusively on Amazon.
recalls.Title: Recall of Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Sets Due to Lead Paint and Phthalates Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DOM Sports toll-free at 877-725-5056 from 8:30 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email support@domsports.com or visit www.domsports.com/recalls.html for additional information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Set - Big Game Hunters branded
Recalled Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Set - Garden Games branded

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Garden Games Ltd, of United Kingdom

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from May 2015 to June 2021 for approximately $225.

Importers.Name: DOM Enterprises & Mfg. Ltd, dba DOM Sports, of Canada

Hazards.Name: Excessive lead content in paint and surface coatings, along with high levels of regulated phthalates, posing health risks to children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled croquet sets and contact DOM Sports to email a photo of the disposed product for free replacement parts. DOM Sports and Amazon are reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Sandford Family 6-Player Croquet Sets due to lead paint and phthalates violations. Contact DOM Sports for remedies.

recalls.Description: Polaris is recalling specific MATRYX snowmobiles from model years 2022 to 2024, which are equipped with PATRIOT 650 and 850 engines. These snowmobiles, available in black and various custom colors, include models such as the MATRYX Voyageur, Indy, Switchback, RMK, and SKS. Additionally, the recall covers primary (drive) clutches sold as service parts with part numbers 1323613 (e-start) and 1323614 (manual start). The vehicles are marked with 'POLARIS' on the front side panel, and the VIN and model number can be found on the right side of the frame.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls MATRYX Snowmobiles with PATRIOT 650 and 850 Engines Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Polaris Industries at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. More information is available online at www.polaris.com, where users can click on 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety' section or check recalls at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road-recalls/. Consumers may also contact Polaris' owner connections team via email at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through an online forum at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: MATRYX Snowmobiles with PATRIOT 650 and 850 Engines (Model Year 2022-2024)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 9,200 (In addition, about 3,800 were sold in Canada) (Polaris MATRYX snowmobiles have been previously recalled in May 2023, August 2023, and November 2023)

Injuries.Name: Polaris has received 109 reports of clutch failure, including nine reports where the clutch components separated from the unit and one incident that resulted in a laceration injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers across the country from October 2021 to June 2023, priced between $13,000 and $17,600.

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury due to potential failure of the primary (drive) clutch, which may cause separation from the vehicle.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected snowmobiles immediately and contact Polaris or an authorized dealer for a free clutch replacement. Polaris has already informed consumers to stop riding the vehicles and is reaching out to dealers and registered owners.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls MATRYX snowmobiles with PATRIOT engines due to injury risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: Bulldog Cases is recalling several models of their Magnum Biometric Pistol Vaults, including the Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault, the Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault with Shelf, and the Magnum Biometric Top Load Pistol Vault. These black steel safes are designed for securely storing firearms and valuables. The dimensions of the models are as follows: the standard vault measures 11.5 inches by 8 inches by 5.5 inches, the vault with shelf measures 11.5 inches by 10 inches by 8 inches, and the top load vault measures 11.5 inches by 9.8 inches by 2.5 inches. Each model's identification number can be located on a silver sticker on the back right corner of the safe, while the brand name 'Bulldog' is prominently displayed on the front.
recalls.Title: Bulldog Cases Recalls Biometric Gun Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bulldog at 888-799-8001 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at recall@bulldogcases.net, or visit https://bulldogcases.com/recall. They can also access the recall form on the Bulldog website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4030B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Pistol Vault with Shelf, Model No. BD4040B
Recalled Bulldog Magnum Biometric Top Load Pistol Vault, Model No. BD4055B

Injuries.Name: The firm received four reports of the safe being opened by an unauthorized user. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Walmart, various firearm retailers nationwide, and online at www.Amazon.com from July 2016 through January 2024, priced between $194 and $216.

Importers.Name: Bulldog Cases, of Danville, Virginia

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to potential serious injury or death

Remedies.Name: Stop using the biometric feature, remove batteries, and use the key for storage. Contact Bulldog for a repair kit or a free replacement safe.

recalls.metaDescription: Bulldog Cases recalls biometric gun safes due to serious safety risks. Learn how to get a repair kit or replacement.

recalls.Description: Machir has issued a recall for specific biometric personal safes designed for storing valuables. These safes measure approximately 10 inches by 14 inches by 10 inches, and the serial numbers can be found on the lower right corner of the front door. The recall affects a range of serial numbers, including several from SW190035551 to SW190264301 and SVA2001200005001 to SVA2001200174080.
recalls.Title: Machir Recalls Biometric Personal Safes Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Machir at 888-505-5195 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, or via email at Info@machir.com. More details are available online at https://machir.com/biorecall/ or by visiting https://machir.com/ and selecting 'Biometric Safe Recall' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe with key inserted
Recalled Machir Biometric Personal Safe packaging

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 15 reports of the recalled safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from September 2021 through February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Importers.Name: Machir LLC, of Springfield, Missouri

Hazards.Name: The biometric lock may fail, allowing unauthorized access, which poses a serious injury risk and potential death if firearms are stored inside.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the biometric lock immediately, remove the batteries, and switch to using the key for storage until a free replacement is obtained. Machir will provide guidance on disabling the biometric lock and how to receive a replacement safe. Customers will need to disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to Info@machir.com to qualify for a replacement. Detailed instructions for safely destroying the biometric reader can be found at http://machir.com/biorecall/.

recalls.metaDescription: Machir recalls biometric personal safes due to serious injury and death risks. Contact for details on disabling locks and obtaining replacements.

recalls.Description: BBRKIN is recalling its Biometric Gun Safe, model QCJJ01, which is designed for securely storing firearms and valuables. This gray steel safe measures approximately 13 inches by 14 inches by 15 inches and features one shelf, accommodating around five firearms. The serial number can be found on a label located on the front of the safe's lower right corner, with the brand name 'MouTec' displayed prominently on the front. The affected serial numbers range from SQC202121000 to SQC202247603.
recalls.Title: BBRKIN Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Serious Injury and Death Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to BBRKIN via email at support@bbrkin.com or visit www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement. Additional details can be found by clicking on 'Recall Form' at the top of www.securitysafe.store.

Images.Caption: Recalled BBRKIN Biometric Firearm Safe model number QCJJ01

Injuries.Name: The firm received one report of the safe opened by an unauthorized user, a 6-year-old boy. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from September 2021 to February 2023, priced between $170 and $400.

Importers.Name: Jomani International Inc., of Monterey Park, California

Distributors.Name: Ningbo Moyumaoyi Co. Ltd., dba BBRKIN, of China

Hazards.Name: Unauthorized users can access the biometric lock, leading to serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the biometric feature, remove the batteries, and use the key for firearm storage. Contact BBRKIN for a repair kit or free replacement safe. To receive a replacement, consumers need to disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to support@bbrkin.com. Instructions for safely disabling the biometric reader are available at www.securitysafe.store/recallreplacement.

recalls.metaDescription: BBRKIN recalls Biometric Gun Safes due to serious injury risks. Immediate action required for safe use. Contact for repairs or replacements.

recalls.Description: Awesafe has issued a recall for specific biometric gun safes that can accommodate two pistols. These safes are black and prominently display the brand name 'Awesafe' on the front. The recall is due to a malfunction in the biometric lock, which may allow unauthorized access, posing significant safety hazards.
recalls.Title: Awesafe Biometric Gun Safes Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Awesafe via email at Recall@awesafeus.com or visit https://awesafeus.com/RECALL for further details. Alternatively, they can navigate to http://awesafeus.com/ and select 'RECALL INFORMATION' at the top of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - closed
Recalled Awesafe Gun Safe - open

Injuries.Name: The firm has received reports of 71 incidents of the recalled gun safes being opened by unauthorized users when the biometric lock failed to secure the safe. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Walmart stores nationwide and online at Amazon.com and Walmart.com from August 2019 until December 7, 2022, priced around $130.

Importers.Name: Shenghaina Technology Co. Ltd., d/b/a Awesafe, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of unauthorized access due to biometric lock failure, leading to potential serious injury or death.

Remedies.Name: Immediately cease using the biometric feature, remove batteries, and utilize the key for firearm storage. Contact Awesafe for instructions on disabling the biometric feature and to obtain a free replacement safe. Consumers will need to disable the biometric reader and send a photo of it to Recall@awesafeus.com to receive their replacement. Detailed instructions for safely disabling the biometric reader are available at http://awesafeus.com/RECALL. Once the replacement safe is received, dispose of the recalled safe according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: Awesafe recalls biometric gun safes due to safety risks. Learn how to disable the feature and receive a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Cascade Designs has issued a recall for specific MSR camping cooking pots due to safety concerns. These anodized aluminum pots, available in 1- to 2-liter sizes, were sold individually and as part of stove systems and kits. The affected models include Reactor, Trail Lite, and PocketRocket Stove Kits, all marked with 'MSR' on the side.
recalls.Title: Cascade Designs Recalls MSR Camping Cooking Pots Due to Burn and Scald Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Cascade Designs Inc. at 800-531-9531 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://www.msrgear.com/pot-recall.html. Alternatively, go to https://www.msrgear.com and click on the 'Recall Header' at the top of the page.

Products.Name: MSR Camping Cooking Pots
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 60,655 (In addition, about 22,320 were sold in Canada and about 35 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled MSR Reactor Accessory cooking pot (1 liter)
Recalled MSR Trail Lite pot (2 liter)
Recalled MSR Reactor Stove System (1.7 liter)
Recalled MSR PocketRocket Stove Kit

Injuries.Name: The company has received 22 reports of the pot handle either detaching or becoming loose, including two reports of burn injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at outdoor equipment retailers across the country from January 2008 to October 2023, priced between $40 and $290.

Importers.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Distributors.Name: Cascade Designs Inc., of Seattle, Washington

Hazards.Name: Potential for pot handle detachment leading to burns and scalds from hot contents

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled pot and contact Cascade Designs for a free replacement pot handle/lifter, which is easy to install without tools.

recalls.metaDescription: Cascade Designs recalls MSR camping cooking pots due to burn risks. Learn how to get a free replacement handle.

recalls.Description: Bell Sports is recalling Soquel Youth helmets manufactured before September 2021 due to a safety concern. These helmets, available in multiple colors, were sold in the U.S. and Canada. You can identify the recalled helmets by the inner sticker displaying the model name 'Soquel' and the code 'B0726Y'. Additionally, helmets with a date code of August 2021 ('8/21') or earlier are included in this recall.
recalls.Title: Bell Sports Recalls Soquel Youth Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk and Safety Regulation Violation
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com, or visit www.thebellgarage.com/bell-sports-inc-is-voluntarily-recalling-the-bell-youth-soquel-helmet for more details.

Products.Name: Soquel Youth Helmet Black / Red MIPS
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,425 (In addition, about 2,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Soquel Youth Helmet
Recalled Bell Soquel Youth Helmet Label

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Fred Meyer, Walmart, and various other retailers nationwide, as well as online at www.Amazon.com from January 2017 to January 2024, priced between $25 and $50.

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc, of Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: The strap anchor may detach from the helmet under force, breaching CPSC safety regulations and increasing the risk of head injury in crashes.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled helmets and contact Bell Sports for a refund. To obtain a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, then send photos of the destroyed helmet to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com as proof before disposing of it.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Soquel Youth helmets due to head injury risks. Contact Bell for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: FeraDyne Outdoors is recalling the Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks, designed for game hunting. These climbing sticks feature extended tree braces with angled steps and are composed of six four-foot sections. The model name 'RTSL-24' along with lot codes from 2023 and earlier are indicated on a label attached to the support pole. Lot codes follow a format of a two-digit year and a two-digit month, such as 2312xx or 2212xx.
recalls.Title: FeraDyne Outdoors Issues Recall for Rhino 24-Foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks Due to Fall and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact FeraDyne Outdoors at 800-787-7589, available Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT. You can also reach them via email at RTSL24recall@FeraDyne.com or visit www.RTSL24recall.com for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Stick, Model RTSL-24

Injuries.Name: FeraDyne has received nine reports of the recalled climbing sticks breaking, resulting in three reports of laceration injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at Amazon.com, Walmart.com, and various sporting goods retailers across the country from January 2021 to December 2023, priced around $100.

Importers.Name: FeraDyne Outdoors, LLC, of Superior, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Risk of breaking at the weld point, leading to potential fall and laceration injuries.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the affected climbing sticks immediately and contact FeraDyne for a full refund. They can either return the product using a prepaid mailer provided by FeraDyne or receive guidance on how to properly dispose of the climbing sticks.

recalls.metaDescription: FeraDyne recalls Rhino 24-foot Hot Foot Climbing Sticks due to fall and laceration hazards. Learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Box Components is recalling its BMX Race Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta bicycle stems, which are designed to secure the fork steerer and handlebars. These stems feature dual-tone gray stripes that are laser-etched at a 45-degree angle on the sides, along with the 'Boxone' name and logo. Available in several colors including black, red, blue, gold, purple, and silver, these stems were sold separately from bicycles. The bolts used in these stems measure 6 mm in diameter and 20 mm in length.
recalls.Title: Box Components Recalls BMX Race Bicycle Stems Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Box Components at 800-797-6880 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Thursday. You can also email orders@boxcomponents.com or visit https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-claim-form-delta-stem to initiate a recall claim. For more details, check https://boxcomponents.com/pages/recall-bulletin or go to https://boxcomponents.com and click on 'Recalls and Bulletins' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: BMX Race Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta Bicycle Stems

Images.Caption: Recalled Box One Oversized 31.8 Delta Stem

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a stem faceplate's cracking. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available through BMX Race dealers across the country and online at https://boxcomponents.com from January 2023 through October 2023, priced around $100.

Distributors.Name: The Cycle Group, Dba Box Components, from Anaheim, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of handlebars loosening due to faceplate cracking, potentially causing loss of control and injury.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected bicycle stems and contact Box Components for a full refund or a complimentary replacement stem, with shipping included.

recalls.metaDescription: Important recall of Box Components BMX Race Bicycle Stems due to fall hazard. Stop using immediately and contact for a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Flat River Group is recalling HolaBelle NextGen children's bicycles in sizes 10-inch, 12-inch, 16-inch, and 20-inch due to safety concerns. These bicycles, available in various colors including black, blue, red, purple, and pink, feature the NextGen logo on the frame. The recall is prompted by the bicycles not meeting U.S. safety standards, which could lead to crash and injury hazards for children. Notably, the smaller models lack foot brakes, and the 20-inch model does not have a chain guard or reflective pedals, violating federal regulations.
recalls.Title: Flat River Group Issues Recall for HolaBelle Children's Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to Flat River Group at 833-857-1010 at any time or visit www.Flatrivergroup.com/nextgen-recall/ or www.Flatrivergroup.com and select 'Recall' at the top of the homepage.

Images.Caption: Recalled NextGen 10-inch Children's Bike (Black)
Recalled NextGen 12-inch Children's Bike (Blue)
Recalled NextGen 16-inch Children's Bike (Pink)
Recalled NextGen 20-inch Children's Bike (Red)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Army & Air Force Exchange Service

Importers.Name: Flat River Group, of Belding, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Failure to meet U.S. safety standards, posing crash and injury risks to children

Remedies.Name: Return the bicycles for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Flat River Group recalls HolaBelle children's bicycles due to safety violations. Consumers urged to return for a full refund.

recalls.Description: SRAM has issued a recall for all 12-speed shift brake levers sold as individual upgrade or replacement kits. These levers feature black hoods and may pose a safety risk. The clamp bolt on the brake lever can loosen, leading to potential crashes while riding, as the brake lever is crucial for slowing or stopping the bicycle.
recalls.Title: SRAM Recalls 12-Speed Shift Brake Levers Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact SRAM at 800-346-2928 from 11 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. More details can be found at https://www.sram.com/en/service/recalls by selecting 'Recall Notice' or by visiting www.sram.com and navigating to 'Recalls' under 'Service/Support.'

Products.Name: SRAM 12-Speed Shift Brake Levers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 61,300 (In addition, about 2,940 were sold in Canada)

Recalled SRAM Rival eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Red eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Force eTap AXS shift brake lever
Recalled SRAM Force eTap AXS shift brake lever

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: SRAM LLC, of Chicago, Illinois

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com from January 2022 to November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Hazards.Name: Loose clamp bolt on brake lever may cause crash hazard

Remedies.Name: Inspect levers for tightness and receive a free brake installation inspection at an authorized SRAM retailer

recalls.metaDescription: SRAM recalls 12-speed shift brake levers due to a crash hazard. Inspect your levers and contact SRAM for a free inspection.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to the iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells, model NTSAW23020.0. These dumbbells, available in black, come with handles, weight plates, a tablet holder, and a base. They can be adjusted from 5 to 50 pounds in 5-pound increments via a voice-controlled electronic motor or a manual knob. Additionally, these dumbbells feature Bluetooth connectivity that links to the iFIT app for workout guidance and data tracking.
recalls.Title: iFIT Recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach iFIT at 833-680-4348 from 6 a.m. to 6 p.m. MT, Monday through Friday, email iselect-recall@ifit.com, or visit www.nordictrack.com/product-recalls or www.nordictrack.com and select 'Voluntary Recall' at the bottom of the page for further details.

Products.Name: NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,400 (In addition, about 650 sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled iFIT NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Voice-Controlled Adjustable Dumbbells

Injuries.Name: There were 40 reports of weight plates dislodging during use with eight resulting in injuries such as contusions and abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at NordicTrack.com, Amazon.com, and DicksSportingGoods.com from January 2022 through November 2023, priced between $500 and $600.

Importers.Name: iFIT Inc., f/k/a Icon Health & Fitness, of Logan, Utah

Hazards.Name: Potential for weight plates to detach from the handle during use, creating an impact risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled dumbbells immediately and contact iFIT for a full refund. iFIT will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: iFIT recalls NordicTrack 50 LB iSelect Adjustable Dumbbells due to a safety hazard. Learn how to get a refund.

recalls.Description: Pacific Cycle is recalling the Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret Electric Bikes due to a potential fire hazard. The Cabrillo models, identified by model numbers R7583BPS and R7585BPS, feature a rear storage rack and were sold in sand or gray colors. The Minaret model, with the number R7586BPS, is available in black with red and gray accents or as a step-through design. Each bike has the model number and 'Pacific Cycle' printed on the service label located on the seat tube above the bottom bracket. These electric bikes are equipped with an electric assist motor.
recalls.Title: Pacific Cycle Recalls Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret E-Bikes Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can contact Pacific Cycle at 877-564-2261 from 7 a.m. to 6 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday, email customerservice@pacificycle.com, or visit www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls for more details.

Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (gray)
Recalled Ascend Minaret Electric Bike (black)
Recalled Ascend Cabrillo Electric Bike (serial number location)
Recalled Ascend Minaret or Cabrillo Electric Bike service label

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of the battery catching on fire, resulting in one injury of second-degree burns.

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops and Cabela's locations nationwide and online at basspro.com and cabelas.com from January 2023 through November 2023, priced between $1,400 and $1,500.

Importers.Name: Pacific Cycle Inc., of Madison, Wisconsin

Distributors.Name: Bass Pro LLC, of Springfield, Missouri

Hazards.Name: Risk of overheating and fire due to improperly assembled wiring harness for the lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using and unplug the recalled electric bikes. Check your serial number at www.pacific-cycle.com/safety-notices-recalls to verify if your bike is affected. Return the bikes to Bass Pro Shops or Cabela's for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Pacific Cycle recalls Ascend Cabrillo and Minaret E-Bikes due to fire hazards. Stop using them immediately and check for recall details.

recalls.Description: Retrospec is recalling its Scout model kid's bike helmets available in sizes XS and S. These helmets come in various colors and designs, including Blippi, Blush, Brash Blue, Chameleon, Cool Mint, Matte Forest Green, Matte Black, Matte Navy, Matte White, Matte Burnt Orange, Matte Green, Matte Royal Blue, and Matte Whisper Pink. Each helmet features a green hang tag with the name 'Retrospec,' the Scout brand, and the size, along with the Retrospec logo on the right side.
recalls.Title: Retrospec Recalls Scout Kid's Bike Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Retrospec at 888-488-2369 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday, or visit https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-scout-recall for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Black)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Blue)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Green)
Recalled Scout model Retrospec kid's bike helmet (Pink)
Hang tag label on recalled helmet, listing the name “Retrospec,” the Scout brand name, and the helmet size

Injuries.Name: None reported.

Retailers.Name: T Scheels, Play it Again Sports, independent bicycle stores nationwide, and online at Retrospec.com and Amazon.com from February 2019 through March 2023, priced between $18 and $23.

Importers.Name: Xander Bicycle Corporation, DBA Retrospec, of Perris, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of head injury

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the helmets immediately and contact Retrospec for a full refund. To receive the refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, marking it with initials and the date near the Retrospec logo, and upload a photo showing the destruction to the recall registration page at https://retrospec.com/pages/retrospec-scout-recall.

recalls.metaDescription: Retrospec recalls Scout kid's bike helmets for head injury risk. Stop using and contact for refunds. Details inside.

recalls.Description: Bell Sports is recalling its Bell Slope adult bicycle helmets due to a potential risk of head injury. The affected helmets were available in dark green and light blue colors. You can identify the recalled helmets by the inner sticker that displays the model name 'Slope' along with the code B1161X. This recall specifically pertains to adult-sized Bell Slope helmets only.
recalls.Title: Recall of Bell Slope Adult Bicycle Helmets Due to Head Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Bell Helmets at 800-456-2355 (Option 3) from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday. You can also reach them via email at consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com or visit their website at www.thebellgarage.com and click on the recall banner for additional details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Dark Green)
Recalled Bell Slope helmet (Light Blue)

Injuries.Name: None reported

Manufacturers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., Irvine, California

Retailers.Name: Available at Target, Fred Meyer, Academy, and other retailers nationwide, as well as various online platforms from January 2023 to April 2023 for approximately $30.

Importers.Name: Bell Sports Inc., Irvine, California

Hazards.Name: The strap anchor may detach from the helmet under force, failing to meet CPSC safety standards and increasing the risk of head injury in a crash.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled helmets immediately and contact Bell Sports for a refund. To obtain a refund, destroy the helmet by cutting off the straps, then send photos of the destroyed helmet to consumersupport-bell@bellhelmets.com as proof before disposing of it.

recalls.metaDescription: Bell Sports recalls Slope adult bicycle helmets due to head injury risks. Immediate action required for refunds and safety.

recalls.Description: Aventon is recalling its Class 2 Sinch.2 electric bicycles due to potential safety concerns. These folding e-bikes, featuring a powerful 500w motor, were available in silver (quicksilver) and blue (sapphire) colors. Each bike is equipped with front and rear tires and foldable handlebars, with the brand name 'Aventon' displayed on the downtube and 'Sinch.2' on the chain stay connecting the rear wheel to the frame.
recalls.Title: Aventon Recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bicycles Due to Safety Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Ride Aventon at 866-300-3311 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday to Friday. Alternatively, they can email info@aventon.com or visit https://www.aventon.com/pages/recalls for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Quicksilver)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire)
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike (Sapphire) with handlebars folded
Recalled Class 2 Sinch.2 e-bike - portable

Injuries.Name: The firm has received six reports of the recalled e-bikes accelerating unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at bicycle stores nationwide and online at BestBuy.com and Eriksbikeshop.com from April 2023 to August 2023, priced around $1,800.

Importers.Name: Ride Aventon Inc., of Brea, California

Hazards.Name: Unexpected acceleration leading to loss of control, increasing crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the affected e-bikes and contact an authorized Ride Aventon dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes a software update to the controller.

recalls.metaDescription: Aventon recalls Sinch.2 Folding E-Bicycles due to safety hazards. Contact Aventon for repairs and updates.

recalls.Description: FeraDyne Outdoors is recalling the Treestand Safety Harness models RTH-2000S and RTH-2001S, which were packaged with various Rhino Tree Stands and Ladder Stands. These stands include models RTH-100, RTH-200, RTL-100, RTL-200, RTL-300, RTL-400, RTL-1000, RTL-3000, and RTL-4000. The safety harnesses feature black cloth strips with a white cloth tether, stitched with orange thread, and the model number is labeled on each harness. Consumers should check their products for these identifiers.
recalls.Title: FeraDyne Outdoors Issues Recall for Rhino Tree Stands Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach FeraDyne Outdoors at 800-787-7589, Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, email rhinostandsrecall@feradyne.com, or visit www.rhinotreestandsrecall.com and www.feradyne.com for more information.

Products.Name: Rhino Tree Stands
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 50,000 (In addition, about 340 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled FeraDyne Outdoors Rhino Tree Stand Safety Harness
Recalled FeraDyne Outdoors Rhino Tree Stand stitching

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available at Dick's Merchandising & Supply, Orscheln Farm & Home, various hunting and supply stores nationwide, and online at Amazon.com from February 2021 through December 2022, priced between $110 and $360.

Importers.Name: FeraDyne Outdoors, of Superior, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Safety harness may not meet industry safety standards, posing a fall risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled harnesses and contact FeraDyne Outdoors for a replacement, which will be provided at no cost upon proof of destruction of the original harness.

recalls.metaDescription: FeraDyne Outdoors recalls Rhino Tree Stands due to safety harness fall hazard. Contact for replacement details.

recalls.Description: DGL Group is recalling specific Hover-1 Helix hoverboards due to a potential fire hazard linked to their lithium-ion batteries. The affected models feature serial numbers with 15914, 19203, or 19988 as the fifth digit in the six-digit serial number located on the underside of the board. These hoverboards were available in camouflage and galaxy color schemes, with 'Hover-1' displayed prominently on the front. The model name and serial number can be found on separate labels underneath the unit.
recalls.Title: DGL Group Issues Recall for Hover-1 Helix Hoverboards Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach DGL at 888-556-8426, available from 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.hover-1.com/pages/important-safety-information or by visiting www.hover-1.com and selecting 'Important Recall Information' at the top of the homepage.

Products.Name: Hover-1 Helix Hoverboards (Camouflage and Galaxy Colors)

Recalled Hover-1 Helix hoverboard in Galaxy
Hover-1 Helix label on the bottom of the hoverboard
Hover-1 Helix serial number label on the bottom of the hoverboard

Injuries.Name: DGL has received three reports of fire, which are associated with five reports of people experiencing headaches, nausea, or coughs from smoke inhalation and approximately $25,000 in reported property damage.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.walmart.com and www.amazon.com from September 2020 to December 2021, priced between $170 and $200.

Importers.Name: DGL Group Ltd., of Edison, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Fire hazard due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the hoverboards immediately and contact Hover-1 for a free replacement

recalls.metaDescription: DGL Group recalls Hover-1 Helix hoverboards due to fire hazards. Check your hoverboard's serial number for safety.

recalls.Description: BRP is recalling specific 2022 and 2023 Can-Am Commander, Defender, and Maverick Trail series side-by-side vehicles equipped with HD7 or 700 engines. These vehicles, available in various colors, feature the brand and model name on their sides. The recall is prompted by safety concerns related to incorrect maintenance information in the operator's guide, which may lead to clutch failure and potential injury from ejected fragments.
recalls.Title: BRP US Recalls 2022-2023 Can-Am Side-By-Side Vehicles Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact BRP at 888-272-9222 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, seven days a week, or visit https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/resources/contact-us.html. Additional recall information can be found at https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/owner-zone/safety-recalls.html or by navigating to the "Safety Recalls" section on any page of https://can-am.brp.com/off-road/us/en/models/side-by-side-vehicles.html.

Products.Name: Can-Am Commander Series (2022-2023)
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 14,555 (In addition, about 2,285 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Commander Series MY2022
Recalled Defender Series MY2022
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2022
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2023
Recalled Defender Series MY2023
Recalled Maverick Trail Series MY2023

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a clutch malfunction. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: BRP Mexico, of Mexico

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized BRP Can-Am dealers nationwide from October 2021 to October 2023, priced between $13,000 and $36,000.

Importers.Name: BRP US, of Sturtevant, Wisconsin

Hazards.Name: Incorrect maintenance information may cause clutch failure, posing a serious injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the affected vehicles until they receive updated maintenance instructions. BRP will send a revised maintenance booklet to registered owners and will directly contact all known purchasers.

recalls.metaDescription: BRP recalls 2022-2023 Can-Am side-by-side vehicles due to injury risks from incorrect maintenance information. Immediate action required.

recalls.Description: Weatherbeeta USA has announced a recall affecting nine models of Collegiate English horse-riding saddles, including Degree Mono Event, Graduate Close Contact, Honour Close Contact, Integrity Mono Dressage, Lectern Dressage, and Warwick Close Contact. Each saddle features the Collegiate brand logo and a Collegiate nail head, with sizes ranging from 15 to 18 inches available in black or brown leather or synthetic materials. This recall specifically includes all Lectern saddles and those manufactured between March 2019 and July 2023, identifiable by the last six digits of the serial number stamped on the underflap of the saddle below 'Made in India.'
recalls.Title: Weatherbeeta USA Issues Recall for Collegiate Horse-Riding Saddles Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, please reach out to Weatherbeeta USA at 800-526-6310 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or email collegiaterecallusa@weatherbeeta.com. Additional information can be found online at www.weatherbeeta.com/collegiate-recall or www.Collegiatesaddlery.com under 'Saddle Recall Information.'

Images.Caption: Recalled Weatherbeeta USA Collegiate Horse-Riding Saddles

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 12 reports of saddle's rivet or stirrup bar breaking. One fall injury has been reported.

Retailers.Name: Dover Saddlery, Equss Now, Strafford Saddlery Shop, The Carousel Horse Tack, and various other retailers across the country, as well as online at www.doversaddlery.com, www.smartpakequine.com, www.statelinetack.com, and www.weatherbeeta.com from May 2019 through October 2023, priced between $430 and $1,300 based on the model.

Importers.Name: WeatherBeeta USA Inc., of Easton, Pennsylvania

Hazards.Name: Risk of injury from saddle's rivet or stirrup bar breaking, leading to potential falls.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled saddles immediately and contact Weatherbeeta for guidance on returning the saddle to receive a free replacement, including complimentary shipping.

recalls.metaDescription: Weatherbeeta USA recalls Collegiate horse-riding saddles due to injury risks. Contact Weatherbeeta for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: Kubota is recalling its RTV-XG850, commonly referred to as the 'Sidekick,' due to potential crash and collision hazards. The affected utility vehicles feature the model name and number on their sides, and they come in various colors. The data plates on these vehicles display the model number along with serial numbers ranging from 31706-35058, Y1025-Y1310, 50011-50012, and 50075-52920.
recalls.Title: Kubota Recalls RTV-XG850 Sidekick Utility Vehicles Due to Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Kubota Tractor Corporation at 888-458-2682 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday, or visit https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/contact/form. More details are available at www.kubotausa.com under the Parts & Service section by selecting the 'Safety & Recall Notices' tab, or directly at https://www.kubotausa.com/recall/service-support/safety/safety-notices.

Products.Name: RTV-XG850 Sidekick
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,170 (In addition, about 119 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Kubota Utility Vehicle Model RTV-XG850, Sidekick
Recalled Kubota Utility Vehicle Model RTV-XG850, Sidekick

Injuries.Name: None reported

Retailers.Name: Available through authorized Kubota dealers nationwide from March 2023 to September 2023, priced between $14,500 and $18,200.

Distributors.Name: Kubota Tractor Corporation, of Grapevine, Texas

Hazards.Name: Improper assembly of steering shaft components may cause loss of control, leading to crash risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled vehicles and reach out to an authorized Kubota dealer for a complimentary inspection and repair. Kubota will also contact known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Kubota recalls RTV-XG850 Sidekick utility vehicles due to crash hazards. Contact Kubota for free inspections and repairs.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has issued a recall for specific VINs of the Model Year 2021-2024 RZR 200 youth recreational off-road vehicles. These vehicles, available in blue, gray, and white, feature the POLARIS logo on the front grille and the sides. The model name is also displayed on the chassis, and the VIN can be found on a plate attached to the upper frame rail near the right rear wheel well.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls RZR 200 Youth Off-Road Vehicles Due to Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact Polaris at 800-765-2747, available from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday to Friday. For more information, visit www.polaris.com and navigate to 'Product Safety News' under the 'Safety Column' or go to www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls to verify if your VIN is affected. Additionally, you can reach out to Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or through their online help center at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2021-2024 RZR 200 Youth Off-Road Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 19,000 (In addition, about 950 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Model Year 2022 Polaris RZR 200
Recalled Model Year 2023 Polaris RZR 200
Recalled Model Year 2024 Polaris RZR 200
Recalled Polaris RZR 200 VIN Location

Injuries.Name: Polaris has received 32 reports of the steering system locking, including two reports of crashes and two reports of rollover/tip over, which resulted in two injuries involving abrasions.

Retailers.Name: Available at Polaris dealers across the country from May 2021 to September 2023, priced between $6,300 and $7,500.

Importers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Hazards.Name: Potential steering system lock-up leading to crash risk and serious injury for children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled vehicles and contact an authorized Polaris dealer for a complimentary repair to replace the steering rack assembly. Polaris has informed consumers to stop riding the vehicles and is reaching out to registered owners directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls RZR 200 youth off-road vehicles due to a crash hazard. Contact Polaris for free repairs and safety information.

recalls.Description: Polaris Industries has issued a recall for the Model Year 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic recreational off-road vehicles (ROVs). These vehicles, available in camouflage and white colors, feature a three-seat configuration and have "POLARIS" emblazoned on the front grille. The model number and VIN can be found on the left rear frame under the cargo box on the driver's side.
recalls.Title: Polaris Recalls Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Polaris at 800-765-2747 from 7 a.m. to 7 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additionally, visit www.polaris.com and select "Product Safety News" in the Safety Column at the bottom of the page, or go to www.polaris.com/en-us/recalls to verify if your vehicle identification number (VIN) is affected. Consumers can also email Polaris' owner connections team at Owner.Connections@polaris.com or use the online forum at www.polaris.com/en-us/off-road/owner-resources/help-center/email.

Products.Name: 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic Off-Road Vehicles
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,000 (In addition, about 175 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic (white)
Recalled Polaris Model Year 2023-2024 Ranger XP Kinetic (camouflage)
Recalled Polaris RANGER VIN Location

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of loss of throttle response. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Polaris Industries Inc., of Medina, Minnesota

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Polaris dealers across the country from April 2023 to September 2023, priced between $25,000 and $37,500.

Hazards.Name: Simultaneous pressing of the brake and throttle pedals may deactivate the throttle, leading to unintended vehicle movement and a crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled vehicles immediately and contact Polaris or an authorized dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes an update to the vehicle control system. Polaris is proactively informing consumers to stop using the vehicles and is reaching out to registered owners directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Polaris recalls Ranger XP Kinetic ROVs due to crash hazards. Learn how to check if your vehicle is affected and get a free repair.

recalls.Description: Segway Powersports has issued a recall for the 2022 Fugleman Utility Terrain Vehicles (UTVs) identified by model numbers UT10E and UT10X. These vehicles, available in colors such as white, black, gray, and camo, feature 'FUGLEMAN' decals on the rear side panels and the Segway logo on the hood. The model number can be found on the side panels adjacent to the Fugleman logo.
recalls.Title: Segway Powersports Recalls 2022 Fugleman UT10E and UT10X Utility Terrain Vehicles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Segway Powersports at 877-628-0202, available from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. Additional information can be found online at https://www.segwaypowersports.us/recalls/ or by visiting https://segwaypowersports.us and selecting 'Safety Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: 2022 Fugleman UT10E Utility Terrain Vehicle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 1,100 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Recalled Segway Powersports 2022 Fugleman UT10X Utility-Terrain Vehicle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a vehicle fire with property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Segway Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Authorized Segway Powersports dealers, selling off-road vehicles from December 2021 to July 2023, priced between $14,400 and $16,700.

recalls.Description: SABRE has issued a recall for its Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canisters, model number SDP-G-03. These canisters resemble toy guns, featuring a black handle and an orange body. The recall specifically affects units that have an expiration date of 9/2027, which can be found on the white label located on the side of the canisters.
recalls.Title: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray Canisters Recalled for Emergency Failure
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Security Equipment Corp. at 800-325-9568 from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT, Monday through Friday. You can also email customersupport@sabredred.com or visit www.saberered.com and click on "Aim & Fire Pepper Spray Recall" or go to https://www.sabrered.com/2023-aim-and-fire-pepper-gel-recall/.

Products.Name: SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel with Training Canisters

Images.Caption: Recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray with Training Canister
Red nozzle on top of the recalled SABRE Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Canister

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports of the red nozzle missing. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Security Equipment Corporation, of Fenton, Missouri

Retailers.Name: Available at sporting goods stores across the country and online at Amazon.com from July 2023 through August 2023 for approximately $50.

Hazards.Name: The canisters lack the necessary red nozzle on the valve stem, preventing the deployment of pepper gel spray during emergencies.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled canisters and reach out to Security Equipment Corporation for guidance on obtaining a complimentary replacement. A free shipping label will be provided for product return.

recalls.metaDescription: SABRE recalls Aim & Fire Pepper Gel Spray canisters due to failure to operate in emergencies. Contact for free replacement details.
